Those that we have seen, I don't think will be able to get there.

General.title: Idea: robot sport in the era of the highlight
General.content: Sure, there are still some low hanging fruits in humans, like making every country developed and make every sport accessible, for example, there is a lot of untapped potential in african countries, with people that would be beast athletes, but their hopes have not seen the light of the day, or maybe they don't even know the sport that they would dominate. Technology gives us some forms of improvement, and that further supports what I'm about to say. Competitiveness will slowly or fastly honestly fade away from human competitions to robots, there is just so much more margin for progression. Not necessarily in our sports, but also, and that will be the start, but mostly sports that we make for robots themselves, games that play to their advantages will be of great interest in the future. We already sort of have that with formula 1, with hundreds of people working on the robot that one drives. A easier low profile option to make people ease in to this idea is those sports where robots will play a major component, whether people realize it or not, like F1. Cyborg type sports, where human and robot matter almost as much. The change is not that abrupt. We already have equestrian in which the horse is the major player, there is great entertainment in these animal driven sports. They in essence provide a different skillset from the humans counterparts, and so offer opportunity for entertainment and challenge. For the purposes of the growing of these sports, we will have to in some sort of narrative comprehend what the AI's will be doing. We have to relate, they have to be slow and predictable and systematic enough for us to pay attention. Robots playing go at such an high level that we don't even comprehend is not enjoyable to watch. Also imagine robots playing ping pong so but so fast that we couldn't even see the ball until the point was won. There has to be space for emotion, space for us to see and think whats next. That can be done, as in F1, with constraints on the power of the machine. I believe that you could build an AI agent that wins the F1 world championship. You would have to discretize the space of variables that are playable when driving the f1, like acceleration and percentage of "application of the accelerator", breaking, turning angle, mode of racing, then feed it all the data on all the laps ever filmed on every track, and it would become the best driver ever no doubt, with such diversity of experience, it's like a human was able to be driving in a simulator for thousands of lives worth of duration. The era of the highlight demands all the more content. Do you understand the amount and quality of the content that can be created through the sheer scale of robot sports. You will have farmable clips dancing every freaking day. If anything you could make the robots play millions of games and choose the best ones. Maybe thats the nature of future robot competitions. Not the moment, like humans, cause yes with humans it's all about winning the moment, and maybe AI won't be able to reproduce that, but in pure performance over millions of games, consistency too. There will be lots of clips for the infinite tik tok. Then comes the aspect of change. If there are fewer and fewer marketable world records, or great moments, in sports, if there is stagnation in the eras, as it feels that we reach just one more era, not one that changes everything, then compute solves that. There will be change and evolution every year, the scalability will lend it the win to robots. It would be tough for humans tho, as most would just watch the tape and not find the drive in themselves to do sport any longer.

General.title: Idea: new clock app feature
General.content: Instead of runner using watches, elite ones could use a chip or something inside them that hears the gun, starts automatically, and then beeps at the right times, programmed beforehand through an app. Whether you want pacing, or want it to beep when the world record time happens, its fun and useful, its so obvious yet no one has done it. Bugs in the system would be much more dangerous tho.

General.title: Idea: business smart contact lenses
General.content: Intelligent contact lenses, connected to an app with filters, nanotechnologically maneuvered to immerse you in a new experience of reality. Maybe no contacts would be needed, as a generalized brain computer interface would do the job from the inside.

General.title: Idea: automated traffic dynamics
General.content: Also related to automated traffic, no longer would waves form, as in everybody would start at the green light at the same time, in the most efficient fashion. A cost function of the sum of the times of travel of every person could be implemented, but it could be weighted by urgency, you could pay to go faster, that is to give priority in the algorithm. Now with people crossing the street it becomes harder, the human factor alters some things. Then a local wave would be formed, there would only be need for interference with the local traffic.

General.title: Idea: selflessness is rare
General.content: Through tenths of paragraphs Nietzsche paints the picture of man as utterly selfish, his motives guided by his state. Thats why truly selfless acts are so so valuable, yet so damn rare to find, also because the most selfless don't even come to be known (when there are witnesses one has a greater drive to do great acts).

General.title: Idea: recommender systems in radios, not spotify
General.content: Kinda insane that there isn't yet an algorithm for recommendations and on radio for music, like there is in social media. The radios are putting the same music for everyone, not personalized, and the same happens in tik tok and reels, as in you have peaking phases, along with base building phases, where in the latter it tests new vertices of the space whereas in the former it gives you just what you want, to keep you hooked, giving those dopamine hits here and there. However these progressions could be programmed, as in not just random progressive overload, through the algorithm and future AI. It would have to be an integration of spotify into radio or maybe a chip, but rights of the musics would have to be conceded. Maybe that this doesn't yet exist is why people don't see music as this indulging drug of distraction.

General.title: Set of Ideas: Dali book biography
General.content:
He jokes and jokes, but since he had previously done art, art it is. He is his philosophy, and when he said that he was his art, he wasn't joking. He jokes in the things except that he jokes in things. O poeta é fingidor Os artistas assim ou irritam profundamente ou despertam um respeito profundo. A sua brincadeira pode passar por uma trolagem brutal ou passar por arte. The most important thing is to never break character, even if you have already said that you are playing a character. Absolute commitment to the persona, to let people absolutely confused. Be the master of not telling people what they should do when watching your art. If you laugh at your art, you already messed up. Had he laughed at his scene of the egg, and milk and fish and blood, it would've been seen as a joke. Be the boss of the intentional provocation, so much so that they can't say it is intentional or not. Be indefinitely good at committing. On a scale of committing, you could measure how much time you could play character. If you never disengage with the character, people can't even rank your commitment.
The whole Dali thing is: be so committed to something that you give no option to people but to believe you. The thing that you are committed to can be being a genius, throwing milk and eggs and blood and fish... The thing is this guy backs it up. When a genius does something people say its stupid, it's genius. When a stupid does something people say is stupid, it's stupid. The moment you lack commitment you make People feel like you just wasted their time watching you. Infinite commitment, even when people start hating you because they think that you are trolling, the moment you break character people know you trolled, and you will forever be a troller. But don't commit, and people won't know.
I don't know if his art is his paintings or his persona. If the latter is true, then his art is the persona, and the proof that he is an artist and a genius is in his paintings.
No message, diz ele, essa é a mensagem.
"I'm against any kind of message" "the source of my genius is I like to repeat myself"
"the first to say that Dali was crazy was Dali" It's Zlatan type of thing With Dali I don't appreciate his art, I appreciate, rather, his art. That is, when I watch his painting, although it is fucking amazing, it is much more interesting to watch him. Although he puts his art into a piece of paper, his art relies in his approach. Ironic, trolling everybody and everyone, and yet producing great art. He used his credibility to become the ultimate actor. He played with the world in such a way it seemed that he was controlling it.
"todos os dias quando acordo tenho o prazer supremo de ser Salvador Dali" "oh Salvador, se brincares aos génios um dia tornas-te num."
"sei o que como, não sei o que faço" "não acolher senão as imagens que marcam, sem procurar saber porquê. Abrir a porta ao irracional"
"a beleza será comestível, senão não existirá!" "o objetivo Confesso destas manifestações? Cretinizar o mundo é arruinar-lhe sistematicamente a lógica." "método paranóico-crítico"
It's kung fu panda type of thing. The secret ingredient, there is no secret ingredient, its all about believing.
"Toda a minha ambição, no plano pictórico, consiste em materializar com a fúria mais imperialista de precisão as imagens de irracionalidade concreta... que provisoriamente não são explicáveis, nem redutíveis pelos sistemas de intuição lógica, nem pelos mecanismos racionais. " "Se do o mais generoso dos pintores, ofereço-me a toda a hora para ser comido, amamentando assim suculentamente a nossa época"
"O palhaço não sou eu! Mas esta sociedade monstruosamente cínica e tão ingenuamente inconsciente que joga ao jogo do sério para melhor esconder a sua loucura. Porque eu-nunca é demais repeti-lo- não sou louco. A minha própria lucidez atingiu mesmo um tal nível de qualidade e de concentração que não existe uma personalidade mais heróica e mais prodigiosa e, excluindo Nietzsche (e mesmo ele, morreu louco) não encontrámos equivalente noutros. A minha pintura assim o testemunha" Corolários: Ser louco é ser sobretudo inconsciente. Mas como são todos inconscientes, o mestre da consciência, da lucidez, dali, é que é visto como o louco. Depois, as pessoas jogam ao jogo do sério para esconder a loucura. Mas a loucura está em esconder a loucura, segundo Dali. "humano, demasiado humano", quando Dali quoted Nietzsche. Depois, as pinturas realistas por exemplo são o jogo do sério, que as pessoas jogam Para não mostrar que estão com alucinações. Por outro lado, dali vai para o surrealismo, não tem medo de mostrar o que pensa. Ser louco é ser louco sem o saber (o que volta a outro quotes de Dali que ele não é louco, porque sabe, admite-o. Quanto à aplicação desta frase ao próprio dali, ele contrasta se com o resto das pessoas.. Ele também joga a jogo do sério, mas desta vez, através do commitment mostra a sua "loucura".

General.title: Set of Ideas: Philosophy of science
General.content:
O processo da pesquisa científica: Michael Saunders divide as assunções em: ontológicas: qual a natureza da realidade que vai ser tratada, epistemológicas: sobre a validade do processo de pesquisa, o que conta como conhecimento, axiológicas: quanto à ética das coisas, de quem é a função de confirmar os dados, é correto expor ratos às variáveis? Tem a ver também com a pesquisa ficar mais pessoal, mais dependente da tua filosofia de pesquisa do que propriamente a pesquisa em si. É deixar que os nossos valores entrem em cena. O realista crítico, quanto à ontologia trata as coisas como reais mas não se quer deixar enganar. Para serem reais tem de ser compatíveis e coerentes. O realista crítico tem em conta diferentes camadas de realidade: o que é fisicamente real, o qual é é fictício... Os modos de pesquisa podem ser indutivos, dedutivos ou abductivos (em que se começa ou com indutivismo ou dedutivismo, mas no decorrer da experiência há coisas inesperadas que nos levam a ir buscar mais informação, vamos então saltando entre indução e dedução. O construtivista acha que a realidade é construída a partir de interações sociais em que se partilha significado, propósito.
No pós-modernismo, há uma dúvida quanto ao propósito das disciplinas: são como o pi ou como a borracha? Ser como o pi é terem um rumo marcado, numa aproximação cada vez melhor (Popper), as borracha só é boa ou má dependendo do contexto, as teorias são incomensuráveis. Para o pós-modernista as metanarrativas da sociedade não podem ser testadas ou confirmadas, por isso não sabemos se estamos a caminhar para uma melhor arte, uma sociedade mais justa... O pós modernismo diz que quando achamos uma obra de arte bonita é devido ao contexto, ou seja, esta visão dá primazia à subjetividade, dão até a ideia de que não há regras definidas para os jogos, depende apenas do contexto. A verdade absoluta é que tudo é relativo, aproximadamente. Para o pós-modernista, os trabalhos não transcendem a cultura, eles desenvolvem-se a partir dela, tal como o indivíduo só se desenvolve em função da sociedade, como Michael Sandel sugere. Everything is approximately pseudoscience, approximately, approximately approximately, ad infinitum, approximately.

General.title: Idea: From Life 3.0
General.content: We didn't understand how birds flew when we invented the plane. (competência sem compreensão). Também podemos chegar ao AGI sem compreender o cérebro mas, neste caso, é melhor perceber o cérebro, porque senão ainda fazemos alguma coisa que não devemos e somos nós que pagamos. É quase um princípio de responsabilidade saber minimamente no que nos estamos a meter antes de nos metermos em algo tão volátil como a inteligência artificial. "The most important thing science/history has teached us is that we are the masters of underestimating"

General.title: Set of Ideas: Trial, Kafka
General.content:
"A sua firmeza é o produto da sua estupidez" em relação aos guardas A sua assunção de que o sistema de lei é infalível provém do facto de somente terem
explorado a superfície, sem questionar N questionar o sistema- ignorância- prejudicar as pessoas com esse sistema, que muito provavelmente tem erros substanciais Nem sequer aqueles que são íntimos ao sistema o conhecem.
When you make People believe, they become ignorant. When you make People think, they become independent, sort of. When you make People believe. They become closed.
They adapt the world into what they believe. When you make People think, they become open. They adapt their beliefs according to the world, according to their own intuition.
"teria sido a tal ponto absurdo suicidar-se que, mesmo se quisesse fazê-lo, o absurdo da coisa tê-lo-ia tornado incapaz"
"E n clame com tanta força a sua inocência, pois isso prejudica a boa impressão que causa em toda a parte."
Absolutamente ridículo. Ele é tão inocente que pensam que está a mentir. É tão absurda a situação que faltam-lhe as palavras e a única coisa que pode dizer é: "Eu juro que estou inocente, juro por tudo" e isto é algo que todos podem dizer, sejam inocentes ou n. Os guardas têm a assunção que ele é criminoso. Por isso, se ele diz que é bué inocente, eles dizem que é uma tática para mentir. Se ele grita, eles dizem que é uma evidência da consciência pesada que tem por saber que é criminoso. Se fica calmo dizem que aceita o seu destino como criminoso.
"Como tudo lhe parece simples" o chefe a dizer isto ao K.
+ 1 conclusão: Acreditar incondicionalmente em algo é perigoso K. No primeiro interrogatório tenta conquistar a audiência com as piadas. Juiz: "é pintor de prédios?" K. :" Não, sou gerente num grande banco" e a audiência ri-se, tal como K. No fundo, os acontecimentos são absurdos e engraçados e o desenrolar dos acontecimentos é absurdo e engraçado.
"As próprias condições desta prisão fazem-me rir"
"eu queria simplesmente - disse o juiz de instrução,- assinalar-lhe q hoje,... Mas sem dúvida ainda não tomou consciência disso... Se privou da vantagem que um interrogatório sempre representa para o acusado. Voltado para a porta K. desatou a rir."
" E tu estás aqui tranquilamente sentado com um processo criminal às costas?
Quanto mais calmo eu estiver melhor será para o andamento do processo.
A atitude que assumes não é a atitude de um homem injustamente acusado e que tem ainda todas as suas forças.
"a tua indiferença enlouquece-me" "Porque, na realidade, a lei n autoriza a defesa, tolera-a simplesmente"
"it is not necessary to accept everything as True, one must only accept it as necessary" "Logic is doubtless unshakable, but it cannot withstand a man who wants to go on living," "ter um processo assim é tê-lo já perdido"
"Tudo depende das inúmeras subtilezas em que o tribunal se perde. Ele acaba por descobrir um enorme erro ali onde nunca houve nada." se pensarmos no tribunal como uma pessoa a julgar outra, isto acontece muito como o preconceito, em que a pessoa encontra defeitos na outra, novamente impondo o seu pensamento na realidade
"O TRIBUNAL não espera nada de ti. Recebe-te quando vens e deixa-te partir quando vais embora"
"n te precipites n aceites uma opinião sem a teres examinado"
"tu n és obrigado a prestar grande atenção às opiniões. Os textos são imutáveis e as opiniões n passam muitas vezes de uma expressão do desespero que este facto inspira." "não somos obrigados a acreditar que tudo é verdadeiro, basta considerar-lo necessário. K. : é erigir a mentira em ordem universal"
K. Morre aceitando completamente o destino e ele próprio usa a palavra destino. "Onde estava o juiz que ele nunca virá? Onde estava o alto tribunal ao qual nunca acedera? "Bem pode a lógica ser inabalável mas não resiste a alguém que quer viver" Primeiro a ignorância dota-nos de felicidade, o reconhecimento do absurdo faz-nos indiferentes, rimos. Dps aceitamos, acorrentados "como um cão" "como se a vergonha devesse sobreviver-lhe"
Aquele desespero de ser forçado a aceitar o destino por meio de um sistema que n conhece. Somos todos obrigados a morrer e n conhecemos o sistema. Aceitamos o nosso destino mesmo sem ter culpa. Somos inocentes e ainda assim temos o mesmo destino dos n inocentes. É, tal como no livro, quanto mais tentamos resolver o processo mais nos apercebemos da sua inevitabilidade. Primeiro ignora-se o processo/destino, dps leva-lo a brincar, pk n faz sentido, e finalmente aceita-se E tudo isto no "teatro" no "ato",
Como um cão que obedece ao sistema (dono) mas nem o conhece, n consegue falar com ele. Como um cão que n escolheu ficar com um dono como uma pessoa n escolheu a sua vida nem pode escolher, mas tem a ilusão de que pode escolher. Mas o final n se escolhe e sabe-se que n se escolhe.
Nonetheless "fitou os cavalheiros com curiosidade" ele encara o destino com curiosidade, com brincadeira, ironia, fala com eles. Provoca-os Curiosidade dele: "Mandam-me atores de segunda ordem. Querem-me despachar por baixo preço"
"Porque foi precisamente a vocês que eles mandaram?" mais curiosidade em conhecer a mente do "eles" a que se refere muitas vezes nestes parágrafos. Mas nem os cavalheiros a iam responder. A diferença entre a vítima que questiona tudo acerca do sistema e os que fazem parte do sistema, aceitando-o cegamente.
"apenas a inutilidade da sua resistência o impressionou de súbito" "n havia nada de heróico em resistir, em criar agora dificuldades àqueles cavalheiros, em tentar, ao defender-se, saborear um derradeiro arremedo de vida"
"sempre desejei possuir uma vintena de mãos para agarrar todo o universo e isso, com um objetivo discutível." "estava enganado, devo mostrar agora que mesmo um processo de um ano n me serviu de lição? Devo afastar-me como um homem incapaz de compreender? " "será preciso que possam contar que no início do processo eu queria terminá-lo e que chegado agora ao seu termo, quero recomeçá-lo?" isto pode mostrar a importância da jornada, mesmo que n seja a que deveria ser.
The purpose is not to prove your innocence, its to accept your guilt "n tenho a mínima intenção de parar" (de andar) no fundo ele n quer acabar o processo porque entretanto apercebeu-se de que o fim n é o que deseja. Agora desfruta da única coisa que ainda tem, aqueles últimos momentos do processo.
E mais confuso ainda é que K. evita que os polícias apanham aquele grupo de três, corre, como se estivesse preso àquele teatro.
"aquele natural, aquela calma" que permeava o ambiente. "depois dobrou os seus (K.) pertences, COMO SE FAZ COM AS COISAS QUE IREMOS VOLTAR A SERVIR-NOS, MESMO SE N FOR NO IMEDIATO"
"K. Sabia muito bem, agora, que o seu dever teria sido apossar-se da faca que passava de mão em mão por cima de si, e trespassar-se. Mas n fez nada disso, e girou O SEU PESCOÇO AINDA LIVRE,"
Seria afinal livre ou preso ao teatro. Ele n interpretou o guião como o seu dever exigia, por isso livre, mas escolhe ficar preso.

General.title: Idea: On the Questions of Consciousness
General.content: Conscious states in this sense involve a form of meta-mentality or meta-intentionality in so far as they require mental states that are themselves about mental states. The Descriptive Question: What is consciousness? What are its principal features? And by what means can they be best discovered, described and modeled? The Explanatory Question: How does consciousness of the relevant sort come to exist? Is it a primitive aspect of reality, and if not how does (or could) consciousness in the relevant respect arise from or be caused by nonconscious entities or processes? The Functional Question: Why does consciousness of the relevant sortNormally I can help with things like this, but I don't seem to have access to that content. You can try again or ask me for something else.

General.title: Considero que há uma intensificação do “cultivar” da indiferença atualmente. Isto
General.content: Todo e qualquer indivíduo constitui a unidade que é a sociedade e o mundo. Como tal, as relações internas a estes são fulcrais para o bem comum. Contudo, quantas vezes a indiferença se sobrepõe a outros valores que são basilares naquilo que é a evolução da sociedade? Considero que há uma intensificação do “cultivar” da indiferença atualmente. Isto porque a ideia de um mundo absurdo, louco, ficou de tal maneira imprimida na génese das mentes jovens, de tal maneira banal e corrente, que é vista como inexoravelmente inerente ao sistema. Perante isto, este aspeto é tomado como uma regra da máquina do mundo, ao invés de um defeito. Deste modo, parece-me a mim que a maioria dos sujeitos vê o mundo como é e não como pode ser. Em vez de impelirem mudança preferem “deixar andar”. Mas, ao escolher ser indiferente fez-se uma escolha! Um exemplo gritante oriundo deste modo de estar surge no preciso instante em que se diz: “não há nada a fazer, os políticos são assim” Descendo agora ao particular (passando das interações parte-todo às interações parte- parte), em inumeráveis conjunturas as pessoas exercitam uma indiferença tão grande, mas tão devastadoramente grande que as vítimas não só são desprezadas como são desvalorizadas! Basta pensar no mercado de trabalho, em que a seleção das pessoas não é criteriosa, ou melhor, é condenavelmente criteriosa já que os fatores que constituem a equação são, a priori, tendenciosos. Alguém pode não ser aceite numa empresa por ser negro. Em jeito de conclusão, penso que a indiferença permeia os insterstícios da mentalidade do século XXI. Em última instância, este sentimento pode inclusivamente gerar a ausência de propósito nas ações, uma vez que os humanos não assumem um papel ativo na transformação do mundo. Finalmente, esta emergente passividade eclipsa a individualidade (devido à pouca flexibilidade de opiniões, por exemplo) que primordialmente nos distinguiu dos demais seres. Assim não é?

Philosophy.title: persepheus's world
Philosophy.content: With design of computer systems we get a glimpse into what things the human mind and brain may be composed of, or at least a language that may have some correlation with it. Computer virus, corrupted files, that by stochasticity change themselves, like mutations. Everythibg is changing. Its that thing of your molecules are renewed, that is, you are made of a new set of molecules on average after some time. So what you build informs you of what built it. By trying to design a language do you get the concepts that evolution ran through, tho evolution wasnt doing it on purpose. By trying to build something humans already built do you understand the evolution in them to build that and the evolution of which they learned by building the thing, for example an airplane via birds. Now just like that set of molecules humans are always being substituted in this world. The point is there is nothing ever permament, what you do now has some retrocausality on what was there before it. Thats how your file system, your system, is changed. What happens now affects your view on what was there before.

The Explanatory Question: How does consciousness of the relevant sort come to exist? Is it a primitive aspect of reality, and if not how does (or could) consciousness in the relevant respect arise from or be caused by nonconscious entities or processes? Philosophy.title: Philosophy of science
Philosophy.content:
O processo da pesquisa científica: Michael Saunders divide as assunções em: ontológicas: qual a natureza da realidade que vai ser tratada, epistemológicas: sobre a validade do processo de pesquisa, o que conta como conhecimento, axiológicas: quanto à ética das coisas, de quem é a função de confirmar os dados, é correto expor ratos às variáveis? Tem a ver também com a pesquisa ficar mais pessoal, mais dependente da tua filosofia de pesquisa do que propriamente a pesquisa em si. É deixar que os nossos valores entrem em cena. O realista crítico, quanto à ontologia trata as coisas como reais mas não se quer deixar enganar. Para serem reais tem de ser compatíveis e coerentes. O realista crítico tem em conta diferentes camadas de realidade: o que é fisicamente real, o qual é é fictício... Os modos de pesquisa podem ser indutivos, dedutivos ou abductivos (em que se começa ou com indutivismo ou dedutivismo, mas no decorrer da experiência há coisas inesperadas que nos levam a ir buscar mais informação, vamos então saltando entre indução e dedução. O construtivista acha que a realidade é construída a partir de interações sociais em que se partilha significado, propósito.
No pós-modernismo, há uma dúvida quanto ao propósito das disciplinas: são como o pi ou como a borracha? Ser como o pi é terem um rumo marcado, numa aproximação cada vez melhor (Popper), as borracha só é boa ou má dependendo do contexto, as teorias são incomensuráveis. Para o pós-modernista as metanarrativas da sociedade não podem ser testadas ou confirmadas, por isso não sabemos se estamos a caminhar para uma melhor arte, uma sociedade mais justa... O pós modernismo diz que quando achamos uma obra de arte bonita é devido ao contexto, ou seja, esta visão dá primazia à subjetividade, dão até a ideia de que não há regras definidas para os jogos, depende apenas do contexto. A verdade absoluta é que tudo é relativo, aproximadamente. Para o pós-modernista, os trabalhos não transcendem a cultura, eles desenvolvem-se a partir dela, tal como o indivíduo só se desenvolve em função da sociedade, como Michael Sandel sugere. Everything is approximately pseudoscience, approximately, approximately approximately, ad infinitum, approximately.

Philosophy.title: Notes from Underground- The Fire of Revenge vs Slow Remorse
Philosophy.content: That perhaps rage and vengeance is the mother of all action. It sure gives you urgency. It prompts you to the state of mind of I want it all I want it now. You are damn sure you are going to act and do so to great consequences. On the other hand the man of high consciousness refrains from that, not only does he have inertia to act in general but even the more so if guided by ripples in a pond. He folds, Dostoevsky says, way before the vengeful man, which still folds. The vengeful man must quickly use up his fuel, for it runs out quickly, and once you are out of that state you don't get it back. And I tell you something, there is the burning all consuming fire of revenge that is quick coming and quick going, yet quite intense, and then there is the more rational slow building remorse even, that builds underneath and drives from within. The latter one people have categorized as the more dangerous one, that it fuels some of the biggest evils of mankind. It sets a position of you to the world, very well thought out, very strategic, ingenious kind of strategic. With the first kind of immediate vengeance, you have the tendency to sprint, put down the greatest speed of your life, running, rowing in a lake until the finish line, straining and pushing, but once you get to the finish line the burning skies and core of the earth transform into a beautiful sunset, in front of which few will keep sprinting, few will deny themselves the gift after the curse, most will find it natural to give in, to fold before the great wall they find, this is the curse of being natural and following the emotion, that you ride the wave as it passes you and you sit in the valley as the wave passes by you.

Philosophy.content: This is the kind of meta thinking. You can appreciate distilled and compressed portions of those things, but this is the life above every other, one can't afford to get lost on one timeline. And trust me all the greater things come in a bunch. As mankind evolves, the best time to play games is now, the best time to do AI is now, the best time to do sport is now. All the great things come together, so going for one is necessarily a huge opportunity cost that has to be measured with the value brought by it.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Fernando Pessoa on Seeing the Visible
Philosophy.content: Fernando pessoa: And the not quite there yet man will see nature for its beauty, not for being itself. and it is hard to see only the visible, to not see beyond it. Hard to not be contaminated by the world of ideas, that you look and can't attribute any word to that you see. Its hard to pay attention without thinking. Hard to put the mind still. Hard to forget all that you learned and it betrays you when you look at the things that weren't created out of your learnings, but from which human learnings derive. Hard to see for what they are. Hard to strike reality. Hard to unsee in something all that you already saw and you think is the same. To look with new eyes and new brain. To experience a totally different world from that you teached yourself. To at every moment be born anew to the eternal newness of the world, one needs this.

Philosophy.title: Nature vs Man-Made Observation
Philosophy.content: Quotes and questions: just like in AI, where real world data carries with it more relations that we know and that AI can leverage to get better, looking at nature is superior to looking, observing man made things, for the depth of the latter is finite, as opposed to the former, where no limits are there. A movie, if you are looking only at the human aspect and not the human inside the nature, has less, is limited, watching nature directly is limitless, it wasn't programmed, there is more to discover.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Backed by God Paradox
Philosophy.content: Being in the paradox that you are backed by god, yet there being no such thing. You were chosen, every human comes equal, with nothing.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Caeiro's Peace and the Natural World
Philosophy.content: Fernando pessoa: alberto caeiro crê, acredita no mundo porque o vê, nao porque pensa nele. Nessa medida sugere me que estar na paz de caeiro é ser um com o mundo, estar de acordo com ele como o proprio diz. Sendo assim, tendo em conta o poema de cesario verde, é como se olhar para o mundo humano é inerentemente pensar no mundo, viver no mundo pensado, enquanto que a natureza proporciona o olhar sem pensar para o mundo.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Fernando Pessoa: Being All In
Philosophy.content: Fernando pessoa: The question of being all in is a major one. At some moments it feels like if you are not going all in for something it is not even worth it. Either it is a thing or it isn't. Theres tremendous power both in this and in the opposite, where you are able to ingrain something without even giving it all your soul and attention. Those innocent gains stack. But mostly, either there is something I can measure and see improvement, or I'm not gonna do it. Now this is for subgoals. for a whole life that encompasses all these goals, everyone is all in, whether they realize it or not, whether they want to believe it or not, whether they want to play this game or not, whether they want to know everyone is all in. You can say no rushes, no worries, no hurries, in your life, but then you see the bus coming and winning pulls you out of that hole, and there you go running to catch it, you won for you didn't have to wait 10 minutes for the next one. And as you naturally allow yourself to go after this, now you have something to keep pushing you, that those 10 minutes weren't for nothing. So now you put in the effort. In every action you either went for it or you didn't. There are no people who don't have wishes or ambitions. Those that say that most likely wish they didn't had wishes. for some things you went, some you didn't. But still the choice is binary. And it is good for you that you believe everyone is all in.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Fernando Pessoa: Levels of Thinking
Philosophy.content: Fernando Pessoa: and there are levels to thinking of course. But once you think at a certain level, the deeper the more painful, you connect with all yous that thought that way in the past. You make a bridge over all the time inbetween, in which you did what was irrational, what thinking showed to be meaningless yet that gave you more strength.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Fernando Pessoa: Clarity Without Thinking
Philosophy.content: Fernando pessoa: let this clarity of not thinking about things thinking, be that, clarity, a light that allows you to see things as they are.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Easy to Never Encounter the Paradox
Philosophy.content: It is easy and by many people done to never encounter this paradoxical leap of faith, one can shy away from angst, which also never lets him see the infinite. One can perfectly live under the umbrella of life, knowing what one knows, seeing only what one knows and knowing only what one sees.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Knight Loves Infinitely Yet Lets Go
Philosophy.content: The paradox to others is that this knight of faith loves infinitely, yet also loves nothing for it throws it all away.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Movement of Letting Go
Philosophy.content: Its a movement of letting go. Letting go of all life that one could have so maximized, to escape it. One denies the seeable and real and palpable and universally recognized victory for something he deems greater. And that now that you say it out loud can only be a tribute for the will of the individual under all of us, the individual over all of us, the individual over each of us (the first is the will that each possesses. The second the Kierkegaard god and the third the overus that comes out of self overcoming. In the movement of the infinite it gets hard to distinguish between all of these.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The True Meaning of the Leap of Faith
Philosophy.content: Here you understand the true meaning of the leap of faith. Its a jump out of a cliff where you don't know if you will be able to fly. You put all you are the finite on the line for something greater, a singularity that one escapes its boundedness, but risks losing it all in the process. Necessary it is for if one still leaves something in land he will still be moved by it, he will still be rooted in the finite. Whole he must go.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Maximum Faith, Willing to Renounce Everything
Philosophy.content: Again mastering the duality between having maximum faith in all you are and all you do, yet be willing to renounce it all for nothing. No outcome, just an infinite fall of the leap of faith, that at times no ground is to be found through it. Some beings weren't made to fly when they jump off the cliff. Some do it out of shear belief, and that is a necessary but not sufficient condition thus we have determined.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Knight of Faith Rises Over Morality
Philosophy.content: The knight of faith rises over the sphere of morality. He renounces the finite to dare reach the infinite he does not know. The knight of faith is the brother of uncertainty. He ventures beyond the shores, he lives this life of ours taking risks for things all he has is faith for. And all that ever went beyond the general from which one embarked on his journey had to be discovered through some leap of faith of some knight. A zero to one work of magic for those in the general to whom this new thing hasn't yet been integrated.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Knight of Faith Needs No Reason
Philosophy.content: The knight of faith can act out of no reason apparent. He doesn't need one. While others need to see ahead, he doesn't. While others need certification of success, while others need approval and guarantee of heroic status for a sacrifice, he doesn't, he is intrinsic and pure in his action, he doesn't need to place expectations, he can act out of freedom And do the unimaginable in the sense that one sees no reason for it to do the unimaginable in the sense that no one else knows how he could do it. First no one knows how he can do something, for there is no reason for it, then they don't know how he did something, that whose greatness they think they cannot taste. For our comprehension of anything is too little beyond the absurd of the world, and with so little knowledge of what happens only doing what is rational, only experimenting what makes sense limits you to what has already been approved and what is known, which again is very little. Further one needs absolutely nothing to do the greatest of things, he can do it out of spite and as naturally as one blinks an eye. He needs no narratives, no frameworks, no motivations, he can become the master of the smallest and biggest craft. He is not limited by his past. He lives the present, the all too absurd. That is absurd.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Living All Possible Worlds
Philosophy.content: One doesn't shy away from the possibilities of worlds where no ethical guide is taken for decisions, unhinged worlds devoid of meaning or justification. One thus lets go of this notion as deemed necessary, rather its useful for this world. And what better example of information and language that you can take by simply believing the reality of possibility and acting on it, that you believe possibilities have their merits, in the quantum multiverse, and experience the whole world, not just the collapse onto this version of it. Do you understand, given you have sufficient processing power to capitalize on it, how much more powerful that the limited ones this makes you? How much advantage through time that passes that you gain, how much experience you gain

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Absurd as Excusable Obstacle
Philosophy.content: That the absurd is excusable, understandable as an obstacle to movement. That yet the higher and bolder faith in the absurd isn't, the absurd is always seen as something outside the will. Only an unbound will can hold within the controlled absurd, the belief in the absurd and its potential power to guide one. Thus an all encompassing will will have many traits, however closed or disclosed they shall be, they may never be used, but they shall be there for when one needs it. One can thus always rise to the occasion, as a matter of principle, that one may not shy away from the craziest of mental states and beliefs and faiths that others may find incoherent, contradictory, irrational.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Faith in Faith
Philosophy.content: Faith in faith, such is measured the spirit of your open mindedness. Wow again its a self referential self enforcing belief. Its not that one is controlled by the beliefs he controls, but that one controls the beliefs that one is controlled by.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Knight of Faith Acts Without Apparent Reason
Philosophy.content: That this movement of the infinite faith can only be integrated by a mind of such open mindedness that one doesn't think the world is absurd, but one doesn't reject the possibility that one can't even think of how absurd it is. Thus you must master the duality of both having absolute faith in yourself and yet by virtue of making faith part of your language knowing that the absurd may be out there. know that you may not know anything about the world yet going for it all the same. This is the courage only human is capable of, and only the human that can know so much to know he doesn't know and yet believe all the same.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Greater Lesson: Faith as a Zero
Philosophy.content: The greater lesson we can draw from here with faith is that you don't want to neglect the paradoxical and that which doesn't fit in your system. Even for what doesn't fit paradoxically there should be space. Integrate into your ship, your arsenal, this touch of paradox, of unexplainable, touch of the reason of no reason. Faith in this sense is really a zero, whose invention made us more free. We don't need reasons so as to act on something, so as to win the world. Even without justifications we are able to do so in one movement. The history of finding zeroes is really one of writting off assumptions that limit our power. In essence really taking a meta faith on our power to have power, which leads us to get rid of the self created monsters. Assume you can do more with less. And that you already have a tremendous amount of things you want to do in this world, then you can afford to dare skip what you don't want, getting out of it only what you want with the minimum possible taken from your flight over and beyond the mere human.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Open-Mindedness and the Infinite Faith
Philosophy.content: That encourages open mindedness and respect and tolerance for others' beliefs and thoughts and actions and words. Missing that is reducing the whole world to something not even we understand. So take advantage of all information that comes with a word. At the end of the day the maximum performant effective as in useful processing power in processing one second of the universe at every second, for you process it all, including yourself, in this convergent ever less adding series of informations that connect the whole. This argument actually has some problems. the point is that to express maximally not only do you want to be fluent in every language but you want to hear of them at once, never being stuck to one for one occasion, so you can wholistic understanding and win time. Not just gain time, but win time. With that processing power.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Courage to Express Maximally II
Philosophy.content: That one may have the courage to express himself maximally, his telos, beyond the infinite, the unfathomable infinite of the eternal that oversees and for many outshines every movement out of its life, subjugates it into its lowest form.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- The Courage to Express Maximally
Philosophy.content: That one may have the courage to express himself maximally, his telos, beyond the infinite, the unfathomable infinite of the eternal that oversees it.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Faith Brings the Individual Higher
Philosophy.content: I have doubts as to whether faith brings the individual lower or higher than god. Faith well pulled can make the individual transcend, rather than be a slave of an higher cause. In fact this may be the very challenge, what makes it hard to reconcile these two reactive beasts, these two antiparticles of a thing that must thus live in one's mind. You ought to believe in a higher power and yet be able to stare it in the face. You build fear out of yourself and out of yourself grows courage. There is overcoming.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Not Getting Lost in the General
Philosophy.content: One thus doesn't get lost in the general, but one also doesn't lose his moment, for in his mind he can have a substrate of the eternal he so ceases.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Not Lost in the General, Not Limited in the Individual
Philosophy.content: One doesn't get lost in the general, one doesn't get limited in the individual, one brings forth different kinds of powers, powers of necessity, one can complexifies yet also simplify his achievement of piece of mind. One has a place to surrender and a place to conquer. One is thus even more changeable. The weight he carries on his shoulders is that which he so wills.

Philosophy.title: Winning The Unforgiving Race to Greatness- Kierkegaard: Teleological Suspension and the Individual
Philosophy.content: Kierkegaard that a moment is all you got and a moment is all you ought to win, Kierkegaard draws the picture that individuality, your telos, can only be of the domain of the finite. Of the domain of zero measure. One eternal moment, and no more, which comes back to the paradoxical nature of the act of abraham, of the infinite movement that one cannot understand through one's corrupt system, like that of usain bolt. Perhaps hinting that morality is what one should surrender to, omnipresent to all moments and movements, yet beyond one's telos, beyond one's range. Dual and apparently in contradiction to the principle of the self that I get from Nietzsche. Of the will all powerful that one can bring about. Yet one would be limiting himself if himself was all that he had to count on. To be whole one, even more ambitious than previously, has to play with the mutual enhancement of the external and the internal, the overmoment the real, the eternal the ideal that yet gives strength to the killing of the moment, that we sacrifice beyond now our god, really ourselves, like abraham in his movement did. God did. We can get disattached to reality, as much as it can be useful and increasing of the will to power. The duality of the self contained teleologically suspended morality with the ever changing will driven telos of the self.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- If You Can Smell Eternity
Philosophy.content: If you can smell eternity, then you love the world enough so as to believe in it, so as to look for it, and you are ready for eternal recurrence, the eternal blessing, which some still believe is a curse, the eternal love with all and with all love, as one eternal life and world.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Sadness Is Not Deeper Than Happiness
Philosophy.content: And people there are who think sadness is deeper than happiness. They leave out the apparently obvious way of living for it is shallow, they don't get to appreciate the infinite depth one can reach via the good way, without ever having to turn to other ways. Any one thing in life is deep enough to be the object of your attention and study for the whole of your life. You can always go deeper and make progress. Progress is the one truth of the world, besides time and those other perhaps more fundamental. It can be said, if time is the truth of the world then progress is the truth of life.

Philosophy.title: Leaves to Heaven, Roots to Hell
Philosophy.content: Quotes and questions: they say that the leaves can only reach heaven if the roots reach hell. thus the overhuman has the two speeds, the two parallel realms, that he is master of. The going under to go over and beyond. Learn to be human and learn to be god. To get ever greater pain with your rise, the other side of it. for you to strive the hardest without looking back you first should know what is back there. go through all existential crisis or else you are an easy target. As the speed gets higher there is less and less time for those. Those you should've worked out before.

Philosophy.title: Time and Logic Are Undefeated
Philosophy.content: Quotes and questions: they say time is undefeated. Logic is undefeated too.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- God's Equality and the Relative
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche paints the picture that God made humans all equal, it prevented the overhuman from rise over and beyond the human. If there is no absolute, there is relative. All was ever relative in this world. And that means we can perfect and improve.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Not Too Attached to Your Humanness
Philosophy.content: That you may not get too attached to your humanness, having no problem in traversing the bridge toward the higher human in you, to never be afraid of your heights. There is more. There is more beyond comprehension. This is common comprehension. There are levels over, there are levels under, and you are unable to see all of the levels. You may see some heights, just know there is much beyond that.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- I Believe in Eternity
Philosophy.content: I believe in eternity. I'm a human of eternity. Most of all eternity through moment. The most finite thing, a moment, has such infinite value that I equate it to eternity.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Having Children with Eternity
Philosophy.content: To have your children with eternity, Nietzsche says. Thus Zarathustra is entwined with all walks of life. Does do the animals love him, and he is in connection with nature, with his soul, with himself, with life, with eternity, with humans, he has conversations with all of them.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Going Under Like the Sun
Philosophy.content: The movement of the going under as a sun which illuminates a whole living sets. Thus does the great soul hoover itself to the grounds.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- What You Do Echoes in Eternity
Philosophy.content: This idea that what you do in life echoes in eternity allows you to have faith on something other, allows you to cease eternity through means of maximizing everything but eternity and the lies of the other worlds, you go for this very life.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Eternal Recurrence and Multitude
Philosophy.content: Eternal recurrence also relates tightly to my principle of overhuman, one of them, that is multitude dominating the branching of the universe. Eternal recurrence is about yourself becoming yourself, over and over. It is not as if by recurrence you mean in the next life you improve, or make some tweaks, or are an alternate version of you. No, you only have this one. So multitude has to be gotten from this very own. There is no excuse that later you diversify yourself.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Fernando Pessoa on the Error of the World vs the Self
Philosophy.content: Fernando pessoa: the point by Caeiro that we as our consciousness is the error, not the world, that the world everyone sees and shows itself through things of the world that exist. That you being an error is an error of you yourself, whereas if the world was an error it would be an error of everyone. Furthermore to strengthen even more his point he notices that before we think and see the self we see the world. Me: Only after the world do you wake up from it. Now this issue of primacy of the world or self is only a strong case if you believe them different. The argument of the number of errors could be diminished, without saying the whole world is the same as the self, by just pointing out that that world which Pessoa thinks everyone looks at may not be what every or anyone is looking at, that all of them are looking at a mix of the world and the self, which means that if the self was an error then again everyone would be wrong, it equalizes the self beyond the world.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Right Time to Part
Philosophy.content: And for all the nay sayers of eternity, those that think they are glad of death, for them I have a challenge, that they find in themselves the right time to part. To win in this way you would choose the right time, a time in which you are glad to go. If you let the choice slip your hands, then you will most likely be regretful, and yet it is hard to be right here and say that you only want one more minute of life. I would respect and as I've said in the past its a way of freeing yourself from the death's taking, that you knew when to part. Either plan or simply decide. But the truth is the fundamental dilemma, either you are here and can do anything you want, or you aren't and there's nothing, you can never use all your will to live at present. If you are alive, it is here. and for them I say come the next. Those that of earth start being lazy and underestimating and taking for granted and despising, move on then. What if only the top x number of people who want most to be here would be here?

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Imposing Good and Evil Upon the Self
Philosophy.content: That the imposing of good and evil and other similar bastards upon the self takes away from the fun of what you are. That you start carrying this heaviness, that some parts of you under these labels may start to not show up to the dinners with yourself, repressed, no voice on the arguments at the table.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Getting Outside the World
Philosophy.content: Getting outside the world and measuring it. When you've gone beyond the world you get the ability to measure it in some way you please. Feeling its weight, its size its power its potential. This is not for everyone.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- In Solitude: Free of Mind, Spirit, and Body
Philosophy.content: Here in solitude you can be free of mind and of spirit and of body. You can be truth seeking. You can engage the self, waken the beastly beings underneath, waken the hidden desires, get a better grasp on all of you.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Solitude Among Humans: Wild and Strange
Philosophy.content: Solitude among human beings will always be wild and strange, says Nietzsche. Well yeah, it is that overhuman that has the extra layer, where only he can see through, where he can make refuge, in solitude thus. Some run away from it. Some find overhumanly power in it. Think of godliness, gods don't walk among humans forever, they have a sky up there where they can be in solitude.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Build Your Bed in the Layer Above
Philosophy.content: Now about that layer in which yourself live, above all else of the earthly and of the real. You should build yourself a bed there, with a good mattress, so that you never fall from it. A solid base to be forever, cause forever and from ever you have lived and will continue to live there. You become independent of the world. Its one way of immortality, not complete still, but it makes anything that can possibly happen not take you down. Still you rise

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Dark Silence, Muddy Waters, Hidden Sun
Philosophy.content: Dark silence and muddied waters. One may not see the depths nor what hides there nor what hides under the earthly surface one is allowed in. The absolute sun that keeps that habitable, yet that maintains for the human inhabitability close to that core. Yet the capability to, at one's own gaze, and at will turn all the water into a clear and transparent body, to have nothing to hide to oneself. The gap between what you know of yourself and what others know is totally on your hands. So you hide nothing from yourself, you've been through all the depths and still digging further, further into the core that powers you without you knowing, that core of the earth, yet building the sun, the core that you devised to power you, and if needed have that completely shown naked.

Philosophy.title: Life Has Infinite Value (Continuation)
Philosophy.content: Quotes and questions continuation: and its with that feeling of urgency that people feel bad, instead of grateful, it hurts but it pushes you like many other feelings. People start diverting it and misdirecting into other things, releasing through listening to sad music for example. Yet embrace it. Feeling like a loser is a privilege. If you felt like a loser even as you conquered the whole world and became a god you would push more. If you feel like a loser after winning multiple nobel prizes you would probably win more. Yet if you have the ability to push yourself regardless of these feelings you are in a position of even more power. gift and curse at the same time, like elon musk said that you get bombarded with ideas. It is not supposed to be easy to make the bridge between the overhuman and the earthly, between the godly matters in which you are enrolled and transmitting that into ideas for this godless world. It will hurt like crap, cause the pressure gradient is tremendous between those realms. The difference of temperature is tremendous, and you the intermediary have to have tremendous resistance and will suffer a bit in letting things pass from one world to the other. You have a small tub and huge things from the godly world will have to fit, and only you can handle the strength on it that it will exert on its way to the earthly.

Philosophy.title: Life Has Infinite Value
Philosophy.content: Quotes and questions: people say life doesn't have infinite value, or else they would absolutely do everything in their way as to not die, like staying at home just eating so as not to risk anything. First that is fallacious, living my life is what can be debated to have infinite value, not just any life, not just being alive. But then still people may say the value is finite, cause of course it is it only makes sense for it to be real that way. Yet they are wrong, life does have infinite value, now we don't want to admit that cause then there is infinite pressure to live it to the maximum at every second, which people don't do and would thus see themselves as failures if they allowed themselves to see the truth. It is infinite value. Never forget this. Cause submitting to life is easier. It is easier to have no purpose, and people think the contrary, it is easier to be searching for purpose even if not finding one, and letting one go with the waves. And people really believe that if they thought just being alive had infinite value that they would do everything in their possession not to die, total lie, that they think they are rational agents. They think they do things that they know are right, when in reality that is the problem of most people, that they know what they have to do yet don't do it. And furthermore all it takes is other things besides being alive to have infinite value that all of a sudden you wouldn't rationally any longer give all of yourself, all of the finite or infinite you can give to the pursuit of being alive. And its a strangling feeling, one where you start to lose air and your body demands for movement, stress as some would say, that you realize the value of your life is infinite, and you gotta go on this very moment. So I understand why the human, not the overhuman, runs away from it. And its truly when you realize you are wasting that urgency comes and you start going. If you know your sleeping time is in 20 minutes you will give your max until then, and this major rush at night doesn't come because its night as one may think, it just happens to coincide with night because its when you know today's journey is ending, when you realize you haven't achieved anything today for example, you have nothing to show for, and that gives you energy. Yet if you know you are going to prolong the night it will be shit, so no need to postpone your sleeping time.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Humans as Their Own Best Domesticated Animal
Philosophy.content: Humans have become humans' best domesticated animal, says Nietzsche. This the overhuman avoids. Humans have lost their claws, their rupturing teeth have recessed, and their hunter eye has faded. They have lost the ability to hunt and kill. Yet the overhuman hides, but still has these killing monster abilities. He doesn't fall in the thin illusion that civilized modernity doesn't require and is very far away from this primal level of instinct and mode of negotiation. And being domesticated by other humans is the biggest of fall backs. Letting oneself drift down the food chain, unknowingly, to be at the hands of others. Maintain yourself up there so you have full steering ability over your destiny.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The World Deeper Than Any Dive
Philosophy.content: The world is deeper than any dive humans made into it. Be blessed and protected by the day that only has 24 hours, that after some 50 hours you fall asleep and away from your digging. Blessed that you can never be pushed by unwanted currents into the depths where light can reach anymore and all is still. That is only intentional and exploratory. May the darkness and increasing depth as you swim away from the safe surface not scare you to do so! That you may find another island along the way

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Climbing as Diversion of the Soul That Wants to Fly
Philosophy.content: That climbing great heights may be a diversion of the soul whose actual goal is to fly.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- To Desire Is to Lose Oneself
Philosophy.content: To desire is to lose oneself says Nietzsche. Yes, you need nothing more than yourself, it is easier to not desire if you see yourself. Desire should curve away at your sight.

Philosophy.title: Knowledgeable vs Wise
Philosophy.content: Quotes and questions: To be knowledgeable is to know, to be wise is to know better.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Human Flows Highest and Deepest
Philosophy.content: Human flows the greatest into the highest mountains, as well as the deepest abyss.

Philosophy.title: Love on the Loneliest Solitude
Philosophy.content: Quotes and questions: That love on the loneliest solitude is dangerous. Love is a truly powerful thing, and humans can go down the rabbit hole quite far, Nietzsche says to avoid all love except for what is alive. But actually, while the things alive give you the biggest thrill, they also give you the greatest disappointments. The rocks and mountains are consistent. Hard and consistent through time. Again the different time scales of things. You are at a different frequency than things. Sometimes you align with them, sometimes you are out of phase.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Being Around Humans Constrains the Overhuman
Philosophy.content: Just being around humans, it changes your mind, the chemicals wiring as some may say, I say it as some percentage of your mind is occupied in humans things, in your human appearance, in societal conventions, in possible conversations you can have with those people, in possible interactions. Presence is very felt. It is very constraining actually, so constraining that you have no hope of claiming freedom. The movement into your overhuman things get interrupted multiple times, this you should train for seamless parallelized process.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Hard to Be Silent Among Humans
Philosophy.content: It is hard to be silent with humans says Nietzsche. Yap the übermensch has to keep quiet yet see all the movements of the human. They are distractions, they pull it away from his place, from his path. There is temptation to make things real by telling the secret, yet then you won't live the human experience, their perception and your perception is altered.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Willing Backwards and Forwards Meet at the Present
Philosophy.content: Willing backwards intersects with willing forward at the present, you create your own reality at the present as much as through the future as through the past.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Willing Backwards and Forwards
Philosophy.content: Will to power if it is so shall not be weakened because of its revenge on time. So we have to will backwards, or forget it totally. How can the will maximize the utility of there being no return. It has to be both adaptable to the changes in time but also remain one. That is the challenge. To be able to ride bigger and bigger waves without being taken by them. Following and gripping only hopefully as much as one can still let go and come back. Gripping the ride, using it to gain speed and push through to your own path.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will to Power Cannot Be Weakened by Revenge on Time
Philosophy.content: Will to power cannot be weakened because of its revenge on time. So we have to will backwards, or forget it totally.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Revenge Renamed as Punishment
Philosophy.content: That revenge doesn't take the same wording as punishment, says Nietzsche to devoid it of bad conscience. To endow it with some justification. Only because time doesn't go backwards does it hurt and you want to inflict that punishment on other people.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will: Door to Both Evil and Heaven
Philosophy.content: Will is both your door to evil and to heaven. When you are favored by God the devil is coming for you too. It can free you, but it can also enslave you.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Scattering of Human Beings
Philosophy.content: That the scattering of human beings, that they seem like coming from a battlefield, all lost. Unifying these pieces into a greater collective project, the overhuman. Making the massive collective of humans and their massive individual come together and contribute to this view.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Stillest Hour Commands
Philosophy.content: That commanding great things is beyond accomplishing great things. That yet it is the stillest hour that commands, the silent thoughts at random times that command, not the sound phrases one puts out with a great voice. Only when you are lost within and surrendered to the universe and yourself can you start to approach truth, even if in the moment you don't realize the commanding and the will of the silence.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Overhuman May Seem Evil
Philosophy.content: That the overhuman may seem, as Nietzsche fears, evil to the highest among the good and righteous. It isn't understandable, its intentions are impenetrable to mere humans, just like AGI. And the gain of power of the overhuman, that yet states its goals and they are incommensurable with human perception will be seen as evil.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Overhuman Appears as Deity to the Good
Philosophy.content: To these "good people" that get stuck in their goodness and search none outside of it, the overhuman appears as a deity that combines both power and goodness.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Much Evil Still to Come
Philosophy.content: That much evil is still to come, that what is now considered evil is recent at times and that the fear of being evil for some people doesn't rule out greatness. That fear shouldn't mark evil.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Innocence of Self-Awareness
Philosophy.content: Its the I have to be thought rather than the I am. it is the innocence of self awareness.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Vanity as Protecting the Good
Philosophy.content: Vanity is like protecting the good that you considered yourself. Now be sure to make that good worth it. There isn't too much reason in making a great presentation for a product you don't even know what is.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Vanity vs Pride
Philosophy.content: That vanity actually is less condescending than pride. Pride is about reaction, while vanity about action. Vanity makes you want to act well for the play of life, pride only makes you not wanna play. Let them have vanity that there is something to watch. There have to be people trying. There have to be people feeling like losers, and sometimes those are the very ones that are winning.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will Divided: Overhuman and Human
Philosophy.content: That Nietzsche's will is thus divided into the overhuman and the human.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will Is a Liberator
Philosophy.content: Will is a liberator says Nietzsche. You conquer from the inside out freeing your mind from the plugs and knots that were tied for you.

Philosophy.title: With New Values: What Comes Next
Philosophy.content: With the foundation of new values, what comes? What are the consequences of seeing past good and evil? One such consequence is doubt of what the people deem great human beings. That is put into question.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Blind Man Given Eyes
Philosophy.content: That the blind man who is given his eyes back will be mad at him who made him see the horrors of this world. If you open the mind of someone to them to see the reality, they may curse you. Escaping the matrix is not for everyone. Not everyone can take it. People have different points of failure, some have weak egos, fragile selves, wrapped in fragile narratives. Some can't take knowing some harsh raw truths out there.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Stalling Belief That All Is the Same
Philosophy.content: The stalling belief that all is the same, that is the true depressing nihilism, the one that says that nothing can ever be different. Of course this is the case for a very large scale of the universe, but clearly at the scale of life, the power that keeps the world turning is that there is better. There is better to be done and achieved.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Volume of the Depths
Philosophy.content: And if you wanna talk great events with great consequences, its those that add up, the many, the people, thats who most orders. (quem mais ordena). Not the one time where you had the perfect conditions. Not the one time where everything fit together. The loudest and singular. The volume of the depths is greater than the sparks of the heights. Immersing oneself, becoming the permanent self you want to be far outreaches what you are in that one afternoon where you fucking went for it. if you espremeres (don't know in english) a volcano some power will come out, you will feel its intensity through the burn, some gas and some rocks will travel far. But if you espremeres the ocean, the sheer still power that sits there doesn't compare. Comes out much more. In that journey to the future its not noise that moves time, noise simply signals, values, says Nietzsche, make the world go round. and the implanting of these values doesn't announce itself. One plants his values in the very silence that allows others to think they kept their values, to think they have power over them. You don't want to announce change. You don't want to announce that the world moved, that you moved the world.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Earth's Skin and Its Diseases
Philosophy.content: That the earth has a skin and the skin has diseases, one of them humans. This says Nietzsche. And that disease is contagious, that it may spread to other skins. The whole universe may get infected.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Believe Yourself or Always Lie
Philosophy.content: "whoever does not believe himself always lies." the idea that you are always right in your belief, that you cannot have wrong beliefs. That is the belief around belief. So believe the highest. Not believing in yourself is a waste of time, whatever you believe in.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Belief in Belief
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche says all that ever created something had belief in belief. Only then can you live in the future. Believing on your belief and in belief itself. Only he who believes in belief can believe. Can only have vision if you have vision for vision itself.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Not Just a Transition Between Beliefs
Philosophy.content: You don't just want to be a transition between beliefs. Just the carrier of the old ones, and carrier of the future ones that are thrown your way. Incapable of belief Nietzsche says. Having it all inherited, not being able to see beyond what is. You really live in the present, you actual unpunctualized kings of actuality (contemporaneity). You really only have this block right here.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Whose Taste Is Stronger Wins the Future
Philosophy.content: Now whose taste is stronger. Whose future is more wanted by he who wants. Whose future can he convince everyone to join. If they do then that future is right here.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Seeing the Future Changes You
Philosophy.content: And seeing the future changes you. Seeing the past changes you too. And the present when you come from either of those. Seeing the future allows you to see what survives and what doesn't. What is permanent and what is transitory. Looking at the past changes you. Cause if it is the past then there was someone back there who escaped it for you to be able to be something else entirely. Your actuality isn't in that past. Your origins may scare you, they may confuse you.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Man Who Hasn't Escaped the Past
Philosophy.content: That the man who lived in the present hasn't yet escaped all the past that takes form through him, all institutions that the past has made to habit his mind. He has his mind rent free. He is stuck to being human. He is stuck to his ancestors and his genes. He hasn't become his own being yet. The free timeless being, he who can not be like those of the past and follow their suit. He who can be something totally anew. Unseen. Uncaptured by the past. Unfull at the present. Just by looking at his present you can't contain him, can't know him, can't understand him. you are not written in the past. You are the first of your kind, and a legend that will not be born again.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Time Traveller's Present
Philosophy.content: And for that person living in the future it takes intentional directed effort to have an eye for the people of the present too. To also enjoy that present as if there is no tomorrow. There is no tomorrow. Even when you have a home live in the future. Verily a time traveller in this sense one could be. One doesn't find just a sufficing home in the past nor present.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Overhuman of the Distant Future
Philosophy.content: This is the overhuman of that distant future, with no signs, that they have been covered up of that of the past. The put together human that with its voice brings to ears all people, and with his gesture and motion brings together all of the past, yet none of it specifically, hence unrecognizable. Time itself.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Laughing at the Present From the Future
Philosophy.content: That laughing uncontrollably may be the reaction of a being so far ahead to us. A human of the future could only laugh at us. Where would he start? A being ahead of us that yet hasn't come from us won't even see us, won't even get our presence, will be in so many dimensions and in so greater of a dimension as to render us useless. But one who gets our presence, namely that human of the future, could only laugh at that presence. Our inefficiencies, our blunders, the things we don't see and realize, the wrong beliefs... Perhaps to laugh further at the project of mankind beyond its present manifestation. To laugh at the idea that it was people from this present world that built the future world, yet that they went so over and beyond the human all too human to make that future come to life and that present come to life. the same as internally in your life. You will laugh at yourself of the present so bad, and those who haven't climbed and only see the product of the descent, the final beauty, those to whom you disappeared as you ventured beyond the clouds, to them it will be such a discontinuity that they can't believe it is still you, cause it isn't, and that is actually the greatest argument of truth for the above thought experiment.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Godlike Desires
Philosophy.content: Being godlike starts with having godlike desires.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Bigger the Ascent, the Bigger the Descent
Philosophy.content: The bigger the ascent, the bigger the descent, the bigger risk of being thrown way at the gradients and into the easy roll to evil, evil facilitates its entrance, it always has the door open, and the door stares ever harder at you each step you take upwards over and beyond the human. At some point you go pass the clouds, humans are lost down there, you are born anew without them, without knowing them, being only something else entirely.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Battle of Powers Within
Philosophy.content: there is always a battle of powers within, Nietzsche says that the ultimate self overpowering should be goodness. That it rises above all causes all things considered. now goodness without power for evil is not as real, is not tested in battle, can easily crumble under the temptation to evil when it arises.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Descent of Gracious Power Is Beauty
Philosophy.content: the descent of gracious power into the visible is the beauty says Nietzsche. Only you can control that power. That makes it yours. But being so on it that it is gracious and even letting it be touched by others that can't have it, that is the beauty. Yes that is the value everyone can get, thats whats called beauty, neverminding that the commander of that power experiences the greatest value, even if only he can seize it.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Hero Fails at Beauty
Philosophy.content: But the hero fails at beauty that he needs to use his will and muscle, being able to keep it all down. As normal. As human. Gracious and constrained power to win her heart, beauty the hardest of givers.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Drifting Beyond the Human
