Philosophy.content: And beyond the human all too human you move. Bit by bit we drift away from being human, further into doing things humans didn't thought possible

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Beauty From Tamed Beasts
Philosophy.content: Beauty one can argue comes when the greatest beasts are tamed. Still is made out of the fervent waters.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Memoryless World, Memoryless You
Philosophy.content: Memoryless this world is and memoryless should you be, not letting yourself be shadowed by your deed, nor your eye by the vision you developed.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Not Self-Made, Made by God
Philosophy.content: Not self made. Made by god. I read. You don't yet see the whole picture, but place no limits on whats possible with what you don't know.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Illusions About Time
Philosophy.content: Have no illusions about your time here on earth and otherwise. If something got delayed you didn't win time, you may have even lost it if you adapt yourself to that new time. The time on earth is finite and if you lose days you are doing just that, losing, the count on the days here won't increase, it never does.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Withdrawn: Believers in Time's Extension
Philosophy.content: Withdrawn are many a people, deprived by themselves, that they believe in extensions of time. That they believe something like the past exists, that they believe they have much power over themselves in the future. Withdrawn from their fullest expression. There is only to add, nothing can ever diminish you now or ever.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Tastes vs Wills
Philosophy.content: Tastes as one knows can be much more superficial yet unconscious than wills, those intentional beasts, that yet can run so deep into your being. Taste decides for you many things, or rather your taste of something gets many of its features before you even taste it. Free from taste means you've seen it all that your mind comprehends, having no part of your mind left uncomprehended, so that no projection from above or rising of a shadow from the depths could corrupt the experience. But actually this is the part that you comprehend you comprehend. I feel one shouldn't shy away from, which again brings the self referential paradox, having one part of the mind totally unknown, that is part of wanting to know more, is that you have something that doesn't quite make the whole thing perfect. There must be always something getting on the way, a little mosquito that ruins the moment yet gives you space to taste it right there and there about, that doesn't quite allow you to say you've got it all. That keeps you moving.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Becoming Free of Shadow
Philosophy.content: Become free of shadow. No shadow for you have lived with it and now it is nothing, it is part of you, it is not any longer a shadow of variable size that can seem like a monster when its a tiny rabbit. That you turn away from yourself and conquer the dark lands of your shadow, not just the unconscious, but the conscious shadow, that which you consciously put away, that which can't stand and you thus send to the far and cold, brief and loud domain of the shadows. You conquer those lands and nothing from it can now sparkle unrest. Fear it you may, but fundamental confidence you have to walk those your terrains. A step to free from wills is to be free from tastes. To grab them all and in the same way let them go. To become spectator to them, in control. Rationalize them, as in they are no more than a fact you confirm or disconfirm, not a thing that sparkles your need of them, oo the power of normalization. Some people give halos to normalization, normalizing your victories, moving on fast. Yet it is much too, that is also much, about leaving no stone unturned, and no what ifs unrealized. Knowing the possibilities, not casting some shadow or some divine light at some things.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Only After Shattering: Highest Good and Worst Evil
Philosophy.content: Only then after shattering all good and evil do you have the possibility for the highest good and also the worst evil. No great evil was ever put forth that didn't come from within. Yet this journey must be trespassed into he who seeks the greatest good. He must confront and say no to the evil he meets along the way.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Shatter Good and Evil, Rebuild Anew
Philosophy.content: You first shatter any notion of good and evil that has been imposed on you, to then build your values anew and stronger.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Permanent Good and Evil
Philosophy.content: There is no permanent good and evil, there is no persistence in this world, what is of the moment must fight to keep be and must overcome itself to keep being meaningful. You will never strike something that will work for you forever. There is no happy eras. There is no non zero measure thing that stays with you without you overcoming it and it overcoming itself. There is nothing that without intention will live on. Will to power necessary to keep living. Will for overcoming necessary for change.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will to Power, Not Just to Live
Philosophy.content: Only life can will, and will to power it is that it wills says Nietzsche. I say will to power even if it manifests as will to keep living, for that is power. And many powers does life value more than keep living. Will to the superpowers!

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Will of Existence
Philosophy.content: That there can't be will of existence, cause those that could will it that they don't exist can't will it. Only wills higher than existence can thus be willed, will to truth and will to power among them. Only those wills to something that one can or cannot have can be willed. Will can only will to win thus.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Love Something Enough to Free It
Philosophy.content: For if you love something sufficiently enough you will bless it with freedom, you will perhaps assist with not knowing where it came from, that it may not shed shadow on where it can grow. Your most precious creations you don't want to put your fingerprint on not to damage them. Thus god raised the universe and disappeared at once.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- One Is Struggle and Conflict of Purposes
Philosophy.content: One is struggle and thus others struggle to understand you. One is a struggle but a becoming says Nietzsche. Yet one is also a conflict of purposes he adds. That your soul stretches to many sides makes you more indecipherable. They will never know which principle you will invoke. You are always anew. If you had a clear and public or mappable traceable purpose it could be used against you. Thus in the unspoken ununderstood way to over and beyond you are unpredictable. And purely random, in the limit, so absolutely impregnable, intractable, unattackable, do you become when you become free from wills. You are yourself more than anyone is themselves for your whole is all you are, you are unmistakable exactly for your untraceability. Either you are or you are not.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Life Induces the Overcoming
Philosophy.content: Perhaps just maybe its the other way around. Life that has the overcoming by nature and induces the ordering on all that plays its game.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Life Not Yet Overcome
Philosophy.content: Life has spread itself, it has made itself after and from itself, but still there hasn't lied its overbearing, still remains to be done and undone life, that the overlife may come about.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Overcoming Life Itself
Philosophy.content: That even the greatest which is master to the greatest Nietzsche had mentioned, which is life, itself can thus be overcome in the singularity. Overcoming itself through getting out of itself through what it gave, the overlife

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Playing with Death: Beyond Life
Philosophy.content: And so the greatest which has risen through and throughout, he plays with death, for everything is left is to escape life. People have this conception of death as dying and waking up in another place. That is the kind of that that he who has mastered all but life can now master. Free from wills. People don't know if he who was born outta life can go beyond it, can be independent of it, nobody knows whether really. They don't know if a god can be made outta man. They don't know if this life of ours can be escaped. If one can stop playing the game of life without that killing him not just in life but everywhere. Can one play another game. Can one detach from mother nature that brought him.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Induced Order for Progression
Philosophy.content: This is the induced order from only which a progression can be made out of. One thus axiomatically builds the requisites of life.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Many Won't Want to Command
Philosophy.content: And many a people won't want to command, won't want to let go of that which enslaves them. A sort of stockholm syndrome on a smaller scale. Many people are just fine with obeying, even if others, let alone commanding, even the more others.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Power, Responsibility, and Good Will
Philosophy.content: Have no power and much responsibility, and your responsibility will be overpowered and rendered useless, have much power and no responsibility and you will be reckless, have both and your good will pass on

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Willpower on the Will to Power
Philosophy.content: your power to will is determined by your willpower on the will to power.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Enslaved by Everything to Be Enslaved by Nothing
Philosophy.content: So the way out of slavery that we are all slaves to something is in the way also of being enslaved by everything so that you can no longer be enslaved by anything, so that the things that can enslave you cannot any longer. Join this to the freedom from it that you choose what enslaves you, and you climb the ladder once more.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Free from Wills: Loving Your Creations to Freedom
Philosophy.content: Free from wills means free from the love you have towards what you create. Your creations become independent even though you created. Dirac delta's start flying everywhere, that you so much love something to make it come alive, thus you bless it with the game of life, yet so fast you overcome it and not let it stall you, thus you both escape and play the game of life.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Slave Only to Your Own Will
Philosophy.content: So it seems the solution to this every man is a slave to something issue is precisely being a slave only and only of your own will. One and only with your will. So losing all your freedom to the escape to your freedom. That the way of the living is a way of obedience. This aligns with there being no free will. It means that there is always a will, whether yours or another's. You can will your wills but not will what you will, said Schopenhauer. There is no free man in the strong sense perhaps, let there be at least in the weak sense that you choose your will, and let it be yours. so Gil's hierarchy of freedom- you can choose your will among the available, then you can choose your will in that you make it, so you go from deciding a posteriori to a priori, and then you are free from wills, something that Nietzsche hasn't mentioned and is my task to develop. For that last transition you gotta first be owner of all wills, slave to none. Only then can you live without them that they have made you rise over the free human. The over free human. Free from wills, here we dive.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Living Are Stuck to Their Gift
Philosophy.content: Yet the living are in a sense stuck. They got the gift of existence, they are bound to it and its creator.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Language as the Huxley Tape
Philosophy.content: Just so you understand with language alone you could win the world and express and experience everything. The huxley machine, the huxley tape could be made out of language. This duality of the convergence of the limit is at the base of simulacrum and simulation, red pill and blue pill.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Language as Sufficient for Freedom
Philosophy.content: For some the world is big enough and possibly language big enough that all it takes for them is that they keep experiencing new things, which can be expressed with the farther reaches of that language which expands the usual. novelty can be obtained alone from language. And is that not freedom that it lets us express the greatest and greater things. It lets us experience that. And a shared world that we were condemned to is better shared if we can also share, through language, all that we can express about it. If we make that language public people will meet you at that level. For some joy choice and progress is obtained through language alone. Just so you understand that if language was all there ever was we'd be good to go and win the world

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Read Beyond Words
Philosophy.content: Look at all that people tell you, not just what comes out of their mouth. I mean this and the less ambitious version that you really see what they tell you, what they communicate explicitly, not just implicitly through actions, but also explicitly through their eye and expression. One hell of a potent cheat code that you could expand your language, for hearing the way of the world and for expressing your way. The world opens to you if you know how to talk to it. Yet through this master the art of while having the language of everything not collapsing stuff into it, having the ability to thus hear what is not even there, that is an even greater step. And make your language evolve in the process that you read something that your language doesn't have, which implies that you got it outside of that language. if language can dictate thinking then you want the filter to be as little disruptive as possible, you want to be able to accommodate in your language all that there is yet be able to see it outside of your language. Thus you converge at reality from the direction of Seeing itself pure with no words to put on what you see, and put words ever closer to the pure that you destroyed at the start.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Way of All Living
Philosophy.content: The way of all living and the all way of living. The latter's understanding is a way of reaching the former, in its full understanding. intuition built a la deep learning, namely with representation, and little tidbits of symbolic aspects that you attach to it allows you to see things before even working them out, you already saw the existence of the idea before you made it out. I noticed this happened with writing and also with algebra as in seeing a possible way of writing a complex expression.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Your Will to Power Is Your Danger
Philosophy.content: Your will to power is your danger, for it is undeniable and unstoppable and real. That which cannot exhaust, that which isn't finite or bound, limited or encapsulated. That which no box can cover, no tingling can't turn around, that for which no price can be attributed or negotiated, that which the finite, the bound and limited, the encapsulated, cannot corrupt.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Make Every Will Descend from Yours
Philosophy.content: Make every will descendent of your mother will their master! Make the boats thus go behind you that go with that much more force now. Make every will of the world be such to strengthen yours. It should be that you believe all of those wills are here just to push yours forward, just so you develop yours the longer, and see for it a greater dimension than intuition alone would dare imagine. Conquer them then overcome them, incorporate every will that has already been worked out by those of the past into your arsenal. Master

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Worship Only Your Own Will
Philosophy.content: And if you are going to worship something, please be it not the will of another human, you that believe in the ultimate power of your will that you would never do that. You that believe in your spirit own your ship as much as you own the whole ocean in which all ships sail forth

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will More and It Shall Be
Philosophy.content: And will to power comes from a who who wills it. Will it more and it shall be.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Your Will on the River of Becoming
Philosophy.content: "Your will and your values have you placed on the river of becoming." you bet your values on yourself. You bet your power on your power. You are a mixture of what it is that you bet in and what it is that those who bet on you are.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Valuations as Arbitrary Self-Fulfilling Principles
Philosophy.content: That valuations as arbitrary as the self fulfilling principles of power sometimes carry people away, they thus drift by the hands of power of else who's.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Stretching the Soul Through Extreme Drives
Philosophy.content: Through extreme drives and opposites stretching the soul Pushing its limits. So that one may produce beyond what was already produced. The more these elastic drives are pushed together the greater the potential and the greater the flight when they get released.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Having Seen It All: Light and Free
Philosophy.content: When one has seen it all and one has been beyond the ideal and the real, when one has digested the ugly and the beautiful, one lives light, unafraid of what may come next, for there is nothing that one has been hiding from. There is nothing one has kept in the back of his mind feeding of the attention it doesn't get. There is no surprises, no secret, no cards under the table. One has no worry but to live. Powerful he can be and directive and assertive and directing and precise he can be with his allocation of his power and spirit. Forward he can move at every moment, not ever having to look back. Uncertainty is the terrain of this overhuman that is characterized by the juggling of all contradictions and drives that opposed one another yet through him coexist. Only through him. God of superposition that he handles it all and has none of it truly figured out, is able to withstand that he doesn't have anything figured out, that maybe only at the end does it all come together, yet that his path there can now traverse many dimensions unavailable to those who couldn't resist the growing of the bubble that tightens them up in their own system. That he can find things where others can't. Relations that others can't. Compartmentalization reduces RAM usage but massively lowers potential of output. this is the blessing of dimensionality, as opposed to the curse of dimensionality..

Philosophy.title: On Life Affirmation as Persistent Quality
Philosophy.content: Beyond the reality of the world this affirmation of life and the self and the world and the yes saying to all that exists, all that all the more is but a spark, rather a persistent quality, persistent qualities are scarce in a world that at every moment takes from you all that you had that very moment.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Paradoxical Ungratefulness of Fiction
Philosophy.content: The paradoxical ungratefulness of humans beyond their capacity of fiction, that they should be immensely grateful to reality that they can see beyond it, that they can venture far and forward, yet some become salty of their reality not being that which they can see which they haven't brought about.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Lost in Fiction, Against Reality
Philosophy.content: One can then get lost in his way out of reality, in his fiction man loses himself, and his fiction talks to him so gently he even turns against the very reality that brought him there. One can start to despise the reality that now takes the form of shadow of a greater cause.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Easy Ways Out of Reality II
Philosophy.content: There are easy ways out of reality, you can lose it, you can lose grasp of it, hard is to hold all drives still and hold all options open and feel the full power of superposition that your true power implies. Most people would rather collapse their whole into something easier something more graspable by themselves and others, something which can be written, something understandable, something rational, justifiable, not a raw untamed wild beast that goes beyond the projections the mind uses to exercise control, goes beyond the very finite limits tractable to our mind, beyond any language and beyond any program, beyond concepts and conceptions, beyond idea and experience.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Easy Ways Out of Reality
Philosophy.content: There are easy ways out of reality, you can lose it, you can lose grasp of it, hard is to hold all drives still and hold all options open and feel the full power of superposition that your true power implies. Most people would rather collapse their whole into something easier something more graspable by themselves and others, something which can be written, something understandable, something rational, justifiable, not a raw untamed wild beast that goes beyond the projections the mind uses to exercise control, goes beyond the very finite limits tractable to our mind, beyond any language and beyond any program, beyond concepts and conceptions, beyond idea and experience.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Unwise Outsource Their World
Philosophy.content: The unwise see life like a river that they ride for fortune, they see it eternal, they underestimate beyond the maximum of the space of daring beliefs their role in the river, their importance, their necessity for its flow, they guard themselves of responsibility, hiding behind the naturalness that the eternal they imagine seems to have. They outsource their world.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Create the World That You Obey
Philosophy.content: You want in every odd step to create the world that you obey by virtue of its obedience. It supersedes yourself so much that you find selflessness through it that was made out of the ultimate selfishness.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Overcome: Two Meanings
Philosophy.content: You gotta overcome the world to overcome it. Overcome has two meanings that the language till now driven by the thought of the people wasn't considerate and strong enough to distinguish. Overcome in the aspect of overcoming, as in working through it, to get to the place of overcoming that comes after when you precisely don't work through it.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will Beyond All Overcomings
Philosophy.content: See this will beyond all overcomings which you face before it. Beyond good and evil, beyond valuations that others will to the detriment of your almighty will to power. If there is subjective it shall be yours. It shall be your way all that isn't predefined, and that is all, for you near god define everything now.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The World as Big as Your Will
Philosophy.content: The world as will and representation. As your entire will. The world is as big as your will allows it to be, it is as big as you will it. It shall be big and yours.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- All Matter as Mirror of Spirit
Philosophy.content: All matter shall be mirror and reflected image of the spirit, says Nietzsche. Mirror for you shall see yourself in it everywhere. Reflected for it shall see you and be according to you.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Believe in the Power of Your Reason
Philosophy.content: You shall thus believe harder in the power of your reason, that your reason be so great it is understood and propelled by every living piece of computronium that this world has to offer and in general in your spirit, the power of your power.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Belief, Will, Action, and Reality
Philosophy.content: Things are more possibly real the more they are believed. They become the more real the more they are willed. They are the most real the more they are acted on.

Philosophy.title: The Competition of Wills and Beliefs
Philosophy.content: Again the world is a competition of wills, as much as it is a competition of ideas and beliefs. Who wills it stronger will have it willed. What he wills Stronger will be. Thus will is the passport to freedom. And yet to the privileged slavery of having to act to avoid deprivation. Preachers of equality don't notice the absolute equality of will and its access.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Godlike Belief, Will, and Action
Philosophy.content: Call your will to become that of a God. Let your belief and will and action thus carry themselves to that of a God. Godlike belief with Godlike will and no action is deprivation. Godlike belief with no will is waste.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will Commands the Way
Philosophy.content: Will commands the way. If truth you seek truth you can truth you will, yet truth is in the act of the will in the overmoment. You can make truth not in the sense that there is no truth but in that there is truth and you can make it, the best of both worlds, should you dare to believe it. Thus we start at the overbelief.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will to Truth and Freedom
Philosophy.content: Will to truth, that is yet unfounded doesn't it come from the naturality of the newborn overhuman. No one who isn't free can be driven by the will to truth to truth.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Ancient Wheel Deserving Rediscovery
Philosophy.content: Ancient wheel that needs and deserves rediscovery, deserve completeness. Deserves to see the light of day. Deserves to see itself become what it is. Destiny it is to bring about the reality of the most real.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Will Needs Fulfillment
Philosophy.content: Silently thus the will pass by still unchanged. It isn't complete. It needs fulfillment, and it projects its reality onto every other lesser.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Will That Survives All Seasons
Philosophy.content: The will survives. The will is the all equalizer greatest common divisor. Concurrent and parallel and whole and single. The will stays unentered and only affected on the exterior past the seasons and the weathers. It phases the seasons and seasons the weathers and weathers the storms. Will to life. Will to power. Will of spirit. For too long have we repressed the unholy our will. We have forgotten to live by it. To follow it. We have come to learn to believe more in unreal thing over the ever real will. We need more reality. Find the will. Let it free. Unleash the dangerous controlled will. I will my will, and I will my life and my life wills it.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Death of Natural Life
Philosophy.content: At ultimatum, the death of the natural life. The death of the dance that one has when one is children with the world. For other adorations unknown to life and nature and wisdom we have come to be convinced by. Visions and hopes that don't live to see another day, that stayed there where the moment left and lost its spirit, its life of life.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Dance Between Life and Wisdom
Philosophy.content: The dance and the day and Zarathustra and his lover life, wisdom his little secret that life may be worried about. Wisdom and life so similar, thus competing.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Dilemma of the Overhuman
Philosophy.content: The dilemma of the Zarathustra, the dilemma of an overhuman, that is solitary, yet isn't understood by the regular human, and the other overhumans solitary too, hardly will the conditions follow that they illuminate one another if they exist. Yet to go to the untruthful human as a famous wise man, or be truthful and crazy.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Embracing Incoherence and Open Doors
Philosophy.content: You that aren't afraid of being incoherent, you that embrace the pain of not having it all figured out, you that live with all unfigured out. Maybe only you overhuman can figure out everything everywhere all at once. You never cut yourself too short. You always allow room, which is a burden for one to never see the end of things, it is harder than it seems to leave all doors open. One can be consumed by one's freedom, by one's will and reach. One's sense of ignorance and smallness can take over. You thus walk the path of no return, of no returns for you never finish anything, you never erase the possibility of it being necessary in the final great reconstruction and unification of all that you have, or haven't, figured out. the path of progression, of choice, joy you make outta those two, just to complete Ben Goertzel's three principles for life. There is the possibility that the puzzle will tame all doors you opened to be solved, that it takes everything everywhere all at once.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Saying a Word Ruins the Moment
Philosophy.content: There is some very very real sense in which saying a word ruins the moment. You have the temptation to say it to show others you are all on the same page, but it just ruins it. One just had to follow this temptation and label the moment. Not allowing it to grow, not allowing it to be something else, what it wants to be, what it is. Pure is the moment, that it is uncaptured, unfigured out, unsolved in your heart, your heart, you now free spirit, free from the temptation to categorize, free from conquering instead of discovering, the former can always be done, the latter only a seeker, a truthful with a greater will than the last who dared honestly and truthfully to go as far as anyone, you raw likewise resonant coupling untamed beast that no other can put into words or he'll miss you, you now live over the human.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Spirit as Life of Life
Philosophy.content: Spirit as the life of life. The aliveness of your aliveness. The reality of your reality. The power of your power.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Redemption from Adorations
Philosophy.content: Redemption from adorations. This was the aspect of the free spirit master of deserts, will of a lion seeker, the truthful, that most spiked me. Redeem yourself from things that you love and are addicted to unintentionally. Learn to want to want what you want. All those things that you caught onto, don't let them have power over you. Use them

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Values as Signs of Overcomings
Philosophy.content: Values like good and evil, like signs of overcomings that the human still has to go through, says Nietzsche. There are such clashing signs of overcomings in one's life. If something still occupies your thought, you haven't overcome it, and if you haven't won it, you also haven't overcame it.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- From Diversity to Unification
Philosophy.content: The human is built on diversity, in diversification. The overhuman, on unification, the full cycle of what the seed that was planted as humans came to be had to give. From that on, its no longer human. Here we go.

Philosophy.title: On Enforcing Sameness
Philosophy.content: the preacher of equality may not just look for equality of justice but making everyone have the same condition as him, enforcing that others be the same as him.

Philosophy.title: On the Preacher of Equality
Philosophy.content: Preacher of equality of all another great idealists. Untrue to the nature of the world. Incapable of accepting the unfairness of the world sure, but incapable to adapt to win and win in that world? That is inefficient. The concept of justice is blind to the possibility that the present is all there is, and thus is fond of a revenge sentiment buildup in the soul. One thus forges within these forces of narrative, these unreal motives that drive one away from. The at every moment least action path. One runs the danger of dwelling on the past and thus trying to force equality. One can be thrown away from the path of victory for some past centered idea that instead could be made of more use by channeling of its energy into will or even, at times, of total withdrawal from memory.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Masses Must Be Wrong
Philosophy.content: Untrue the masses's spirit has to be, for there is still no overhuman. Wrong they have to be in some ways.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Virtue Sold Cheaply
Philosophy.content: So cheap everyone sells virtue to themselves. So many virtues lying around, but so little selfs to carry their weight. Thus so lightly they invest to their virtue. So transitory their virtue is, rather a momentaneous ego booster than an unshakable tree that has grown out of them

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Virtue as Individuality
Philosophy.content: That perhaps your virtue is your individuality, thus putting yourself in front guide of your ship so you go your way. Everyone is an expert at good and evil, points Nietzsche out, yet there is no overhuman yet. All that is created by humans can likewise be created by yourself.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Priests as Victims of Delusion
Philosophy.content: The picture here painted by Nietzsche is that priests are victims of delusion. They filled the gaps their spirit had with God, thus becoming a simulacrum of a good soul, whose base crumbles at touch. Lackless they think themselves to be, yet lackful, via unaddressing, they are if they are wrong. They put themselves through pain, cause pain is the most real feeling, to start to believe in their own narrative, they thus invest and dive head on to their voluntary movement, and sometimes may forget that it is a voluntariness, that in the worst case there was nothing to be gained by that undergoing. It is in one's interest of maximizing life that one believes that each moment should be maximized, that one owes nothing to anyone or any story. There is no memory. We live a local life. The moment is all there is. Thus at any moment one can be lived anew. One can totally change what one is. Don't let yourself be slave of your past plans, of what you thought back then or what someone else did. If you are so stuck in the framework of your church, think that the god you preach would beyond all have wanted you to maximize your short stay at his hotel.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Prisoners of Their Own Delusion
Philosophy.content: Prisoners of their delusion that their reality is not just theirs, even if they are right about it, one may go as far as to say. Giving away your story, what you so dearly deared real, having it be squandered, its tough. The realization that one may have wasted life away is as daunting as it is irrational. Cause the momentum builds up, and if one is brought down from the height of the world he has created, if that doubt starts creeping and tearing it, so that one can no longer even enjoy it, so that one cannot not just remember, but live with it, that it brings the highest of men to his knees. That one in this descent can thus fall far below the level of the ground. There is such a thing as sensitive information. One can easily overestimate his power against words and information, the overhuman overcomes this, so that he may die for words, yet live all the same for those he wishes. Most don't dare weather this storm. There is really such a thing as knowledge of something that can make you have no drive to live, lose all your will. I must go beyond this if I am to go further. The world is cruel, and just like we don't really grasp infinity, or the value of life, we don't grasp either what it feels like to know some things. We are but one human. How much can one human take. It is as easy to let something have no effect and let it kill you. A true 50\50, the fairest of things

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Hero Who Must Not Become Priest
Philosophy.content: For a hero to not fall in the trap of the priest, he must come back around and be at peace with all that of hero was he. Know deep down inasmuch vigor that one has found the best path and that one must be content with knowing that alone, needing not to show others his vision thus. If one has suffered, know that it was voluntary, accept in the extreme case that all suffering one has yielded was voluntary and wasn't needed to get where one's got. Thus freeing oneself from the temptation of imposing that to others, acting as much part of the people as any other inverse of hero has done. Swallowing it whole. Not becoming priest of being a hero. For that imposes form and structure, form and structure that, as much as one has given his life to perfect, one has to accept the futility of it beyond anything that transcends the self. That any other will not understand this vision one preaches, that any other, alone, yet parallel to all others that move beyond the masses and rise to become themselves, will have to find his own form and structure. Thus one paradoxically reaches a state where one has the most to say, yet none of it can be heard. For example, after a long tribulous yet self actualizing career one is in the position to relate it all, yet it can't produce, that narration cannot bring further value than that career has produced. Only after doing all the climb does one know the way to climb. Only after all the work can one get the view that could have previously led him up. Thus the hero fools himself one last time reaching and expressing all his heroicity, not being able to contain it within.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Irrelevancy and the Ghost's Freedom
Philosophy.content: The greatest unfairness, says Nietzsche, sickness I called it before, is not to be repugnant to someone, but him meaning nothing. Irrelevancy is the most disarming of experiences. Yet it brings you a certain distance away from the humans, gets you away from the noise. Gives you a certain freedom. Being a foreigner everywhere and having no one that knows you or cares about you is still freeing. You have no obligation towards anyone, you live in third person, as if watching humans, as a ghost. You can walk around with no pressure, no standard to meet, no form to take. Having nothing to hold onto, having no home, still gives a hell of a ride.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On Shame and the Right Reasons
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche says shame is the history of the human. yet it seems we are ashamed all for the wrong reasons. We should be ashamed when you see someone suffering more than us, someone working harder than us, someone learning more than us, doing more than us, and above all living more life than us, loving living more than us, or living loving more than us, or wanting more than us, or dreaming higher than us, or believing greater than us. Pitying rather than being ashamed Nietzsche says, those bastards pitiful bastards.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Shadow of the Overhuman
Philosophy.content: That we are still a shadow of the overhuman. A projection. A collapse. A reduction in dimension. In volume. A reduction. Harnessing the power of our higher fuller selves on our way to self actualization and self overcoming. and yet the overhuman is inasmuch the shadow of us. Its non expression comes as our repression. It is our shadow that we fear manifest. Its in there somewhere in the depths of our minds those forces that could elevate us over and beyond, after, like Zarathustra, undergoing. Unleashing the dormant part of ourselves to being the most alive

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Power as the Bestowing Virtue
Philosophy.content: Power it is the bestowing virtue. Seeking power the way of elevation will to power. My virtue. Nietzsche preaches the use of this power of virtue into the real, not letting this virtue get shattered by the unreality of the eternal, rather have it very present your virtue, be it very present.

Philosophy.title: The Bestowing Virtue and Transcending Levels
Philosophy.content: The bestowing virtue is an elevation over and beyond. A conquest of the world and yourself, having battled it out, having been through it all, you emerge through the clouds not to be bothered again by those opponents you defeated and took as one of your own. One bridges to the overhuman through expansion of the human all too human. The human is something that is to be overcome. One doesn't progress the quality of life by playing in ever more fields, but by playing in higher fields. By transcending the levels.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Bestowing Virtue and Predator of Values
Philosophy.content: In search of the bestowing virtue. A predator of values one becomes, but whole and holy calls Nietzsche this mission. To pile up all riches in the soul he says.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Value of Uselessness and Immortality
Philosophy.content: That the highest value is conferred to gold for its scarcity, shine and uselessness. By the last one can't measure its value, it is valueless and thereby can be given any value. It is visible by everybody, and few can have it. So the most valuable thing is what everyone sees, what no one can't have, and is useful. But this only confers value. One can define value through uselessness. Immortality confirms itself as the highest value thing if it was wrapped into an object like figure. Control of time. Of the Universe. What would be the gold of the Universe then, for all worlds out there. Perhaps the inside of a black hole? No for it can't be seen. Dark matter isn't luminous enough and perhaps can be useful. Perhaps all useless things are much less known because of that, and so its hard to even name one.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- A Life Affirming Death
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche paints the picture that your dying should be that of a life affirming being. Your energy should glow into love of the earthly and human. Free for and free from dying. Do not waste your death, and that means make people ripe from of your life. May your dying pass on the value of your living to others. May you thus let space for life to be lived, that life that you brought about. Lead the life, and die for the students to live theirs. A leader must then give space to his followers so they are their own followers.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On Free Death
Philosophy.content: On free death is the title if you allow your death to come free, my friend you're not. And you pay the price on this loss of control. it's as if a basketball player didn't retire but rather wait for the day where his knees fell off. We like to mark ends to things, except our life. Or ending a movie or a podcast when the battery of the camera died out.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Loving Yourself: Polishing the Vision
Philosophy.content: Loving yourself becomes much about polishing and cleaning the dust off your vision, always having it fresh, embracing the moment in the light of the overhuman presence you seek to realize.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Individuality Before the Group
Philosophy.content: One should then realize this before handing oneself to the neighbor. Not letting your individuality get faded to the group, that you be letting go of control, but rather augmenting it through it.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Solitude as All You Need
Philosophy.content: Perhaps the straddled principle across these lately ideas, that solitude is all you should ever need. You can live with you and your cause. More than living humans, loving causes he preaches. You love in the other what you envy he says. Be yourself a cause and thus have the highest love for yourself. I am the cause. I am the revolution. If you can't endure being with yourself, you are doomed to keep searching and lurking and looting and looking. What you look to find you should bring about from yourself. You should create that person you love talking to, and the one who does things the way you love. Your perception is your perception not others'

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Tablet of Good and Evil as Prediction Tool
Philosophy.content: Perhaps obvious but never by me said that this tablet of good and evil in every person which dress their will to power is the single most valuable thing in understanding someone and predict them.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Good and Evil as Signs of Will to Power
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche seems to imply that everyone's good and evil tells us about their overcomings and their will to power.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Friends as Mirrors of What to Overcome
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche here perhaps insinuating that in your friends you see you that must be overcome. Your friend show you how you must improve, for you see in them what you look like and what you have to improve.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- At Least Be My Enemy
Philosophy.content: At least be my enemy if you can't be my friend, says Nietzsche in somewhat these words. If no one is your enemy then you perhaps don't really matter, or don't do something big. Heard this too from Andrew Tate, and from polarizability in general, people want to see your action, whether they are friends and want to see you rise or enemies and want to see you crumble. There is of course bullshit and stupidity in Andrew Tate, but there is also things to be taken, value to be taken by going where others don't dare perhaps for the deprecation of those around them. Perhaps being dangerous and choosing to work as a team is a build of solid friendship. Making your friendship valuable through mutual knowing of each others capabilities to end it.

Philosophy.title: On Solitude and the Inner Dialogue
Philosophy.content: For the solitary one person around is too many, Nietzsche says, that it prevents the two of the solitary, him and his mind, to take the moment into greater depths. Thus I say company is enemy of truth. If right now what you wish is truth, all it takes and it takes all, is you alone. To be able to journey within into depths you are afraid to show another. There is no single one that knows you beyond the depths you are willing to go if you are alone, or rather with anyone you are not willing to go beyond certain depths. Truly being alone, that means being with nobody, not talking to Nietzsche, or who you are talking to in some of your free time, not having the company of society's mass produced memes. Truly yourself. Here can you search the big questions, and can you be free to do so. How much can you do that you can't do while with others? How many conversations there are no ears for in this world except your mind. How many places can you go only you comprehend. In essence how many limits the world places on your mind, that is what can be overcome as you stand with yourself. How much can you discover beyond the usual field of play you've been bound to. What would you actually do were you handed solitude for some amount of time? Those are some interesting questions. What voices are being muted by each bounding factor. What thoughts are better vocalized and more eloquently expressed when you are by yourself? This one particularly hits home for me. I'm much better able to produce ideas by writing or thinking by myself than when asked from people with people

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On the Dilution of Authority and Values
Philosophy.content: There is a real sense in which the dilution of authority, responsibility, into the collective being, brings about a certain confusion as to the problems you face, and honorable and more generally value driven people would abstain from playing this game. They'd rather die in battle than live with the mess of modern problems of which they have no clarity. They don't want to live for free if that means giving away their individual values. Thus one sacrifices even his life, thus one seeks the clear rules of into the wild a la Kaczynski.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Creators of Values vs Actors of the Show
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche says the way of the world is that it revolves around the creators of new values, but people revolve around the play actors and the show.

Philosophy.title: On Irreplicable Essence
Philosophy.content: Quotes and questions: "They can get the recipe. But they can't make the sauce." this idea that it is very clear to see what makes things work, yet being unable to get the ingredients. Thing that can't be bought. The fiber that only you have, can't be taken from you (it's yours) and can't be taken away (it's only yours). Stuff that is truly valuable, beyond any clothing and surface one puts over it. there is this great aspect of seeing someone you know do something great you didn't know they could do. Like they not telling you and then you discovering independently they did something. That is essence.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Mud on the Throne
Philosophy.content: Perhaps that the abstraction of the state allows for further development of abstractions, more space for imagination and all of a sudden lesser real goals take place, all of a sudden power over the confused and spread state is what People drive for. That there is only mud on the throne and the throne sits on top of mud, Nietzsche says

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Death Preachers and the State's Entropy
Philosophy.content: Perhaps the preachers of death finding refuge in the superior way of the state, yet only making confuse good and evil, whereas before it was bound and tamed by a people, now it is spread everywhere, as most things tend to be through entropy. One can no longer point blame or bear responsibility, one can no longer own achievement, a giant cloud of mess submerged into the supremacy of the state, stealer of all that was something. Nietzsche even goes further in suggesting the going back to the old ways when one could place all power and good and evil in one person. Thus there are too many he says.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The State Against Natural Order
Philosophy.content: there is perhaps also an argument against the state as going against the natural order of people and herds, taking power away from the people. perhaps there is an inacceptance via the perceived unfairness of the mental nature of the natural social dynamics of humans. Just maybe the state hides that from our sight. Nowadays people refuse human the animal, human with its natural hierarchies. Perhaps better named a shying away from the irrational roots that brought you here.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Human to Be Overcome
Philosophy.content: "The human is something that is to be overcome" the whole message of the book. It seems hard to overcome the human as a human, but it is in me to get it done. I as of now gaze at the fire, was it invented or discovered? My wise bit, my uncle, said it was captured, seen in nature and tamed. This form of life made totally functional, constricted. Only allowed to burn a certain much, that is the most annoying. Having to work but not being able to give it your best. No warrior wants to live long or be spared. One wants a great war, may the best thought come out, may one change for the better, and may peace be short lived. But what is it else that hasn't been captured by human, tamed. Now the overhuman shall tame the human, tame all that's human, all too human, this is a way I see to unlock the next chapter in our experience. Destroy it all the alike and rise the new. so that the overhuman isn't contaminated by human foundations, tho it builds on 'em.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The State That Substitutes the People
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche reckons the state substitutes the people. That it pretends to be the people, but no longer is there people and followers, there is an abstraction that engulfs the two, perhaps so by virtue of losing reality, we'll see where he goes with this.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- War of Ideas
Philosophy.content: War of ideas. That the other lover of ideas love us from the ground up, yet are also our own worst enemy. That in the name of keeping this war alive you should love from the ground up your position, yet seek your worst enemy. Learn the most. Make it a mess of a thing of a ramble of ideas competing for your attention, not uniform. Warrior no soldier. A whole army moving freely you are my friend.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On Time and the Child's Honesty
Philosophy.content: "falta uma hora, 60 minutos, é tanto, é tanto" here in the train. Wow how I miss that, how I have become distant to that. Always asking how much time left, indifferent to my opinions, we babies know what we want, or rather what we don't. And we are sure to try to get one over the other, even if it means getting mad and feeling bad about the moment, we don't pretend we have what we want. Real

Philosophy.title: The Selfish Gene: Blind Faith and Genes as Living Structures
Philosophy.content: "A fé cega pode justificar tudo." Acho que todos somos míopes, e se descermos a um nível de análise suficientemente profundo percebemos que aí somos cegos. "Os genes deveriam ser encarados como estruturas vivas, não só metaforicamente, mas tecnicamente." Quando é para atacar, é para atacar.

Philosophy.title: The Selfish Gene: Universal Application of Replicators
Philosophy.content: A ideia de Dawkins de que o seu argumento filosófico (que qualquer vida no universo ocorre por agentes replicadores, segundo o próprio) aplica-se ao universo inteiro, enquanto que as experiências de laboratório só nos ensinam sobre a vida como ela é aqui. Ou seja, a filosofia, e por exemplo a matemática, poderá aplicar-se a tudo, e daí poder não aplicar-se a nada. Ou seja, como não tem uma aplicação definida, é completamente maleável ao ponto de se aplicar em diversas áreas.

Philosophy.title: The Selfish Gene: Tit for Tat and Rebelling Against Replicators
Philosophy.content: Temos o poder de nos revoltar contra os nossos genes replicadores e os nossos memes egoístas. "Só nós, na Terra, temos o poder de nos revoltar contra a tirania dos replicadores egoístas." Ainda que as melhores estratégias dependam das estratégias dos outros, a estratégia "olho por olho" parece ser bem sucedida. Esta diz para seres altruísta em primeira instância e depois copiar o movimento anterior do oponente. Se ele trair, trai; se ele cooperar, então coopera. Ou seja, se ele trair, ataca brutalmente, num golpe rápido e eficaz. Mas depois não te deixes ficar com rancor para sempre. Se o tiveres, é para ser usado para te motivar a ser melhor. E não tenhas arrependimentos.

Philosophy.title: The Selfish Gene: The Memetic Pool and Comfortable Illusions
Philosophy.content: No pool memético, na minha opinião, sobrevivem as ideias que nos fazem confortáveis. Se são as ideias de Deus, por exemplo, ficamos confortáveis em tapar os buracos da nossa existência com uma camada de vidro. O mesmo para as piadas e isso. O que nos incomoda normalmente são as verdades (algumas), pelo que perceber a verdade é ir contra a corrente. Às vezes, mesmo as verdades mais científicas só servem para ficarmos confortáveis em pensar que temos a verdade.

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Gricean Communication and the User Illusion
Philosophy.content: Segundo Grice, no ato de comunicação tem de haver intenção de informar outra pessoa, tem de se assumir a responsabilidade de ter a intenção (tem de deixar que a outra pessoa saiba que tem a intenção de a informar) e tem de ser o reconhecimento da pessoa que, pelo menos em parte, cause a sua resposta, crença. "S1 pretende que A(2) reconheça que S(3) pretende que A(4) acredite em P". Dennett sugere que: know your memes, know the world in which you know you live. Dennett diz que a domesticação dos memes é passar da "seleção inconsciente para a seleção metódica", o que para mim é um passo mais próximo do livre arbítrio. "Everything that you can do, I can do meta" - Doug Hofstadter. Esta frase mostra que a consciência permite usar palavras para falar sobre palavras, teorias que expliquem teorias... "Há uma obrigação de termos razões que possam explicar um comportamento." "O primeiro dever na vida é ser tão artificial quanto possível" - Oscar Wilde. "A linguagem foi dada aos homens para que pudessem esconder os seus pensamentos" - Charles-Maurice de Talleyrand. Dennett vai então dizer que a consciência é uma ilusão do utilizador, a ilusão do eu, feita pela comunicação, pelo facto de deitarmos cá para fora os nossos pensamentos. Diz que construímos uma narrativa à volta da comunicação, em que o "eu" é uma parte da narrativa. O cérebro edita a narrativa para ser simples e coerente, não nos mostrando a complexidade do processo. "A experiência consciente é a forma como as nossas mentes retiram das suas operações para nós, o seu funcionamento real." - Daniel Wegner. Parece-me muito interessante este conceito da narrativa editada pelo nosso cérebro, porque mantém-nos distraídos da sua própria natureza. Em vez de andarmos todos confusos sobre como funcionamos, pegamos na narrativa e não questionamos. Claro que a ciência agora virou-se contra o próprio cérebro, porque está a construir a sua própria narrativa que faz parte por sua vez de uma narrativa do cérebro dos cientistas.

Philosophy.title: Dennett: The Manifest Image, Scientific Image, and Patterns
Philosophy.content: Dennett definiu as razões para que hajam propriedades do design que parecem ser inúteis como razões de livre flutuação. Dennett concorda com outra pessoa em que a filosofia é o negócio entre a imagem manifesta e a imagem científica. Há aqueles que acham que a ciência é uma ficção útil, há os do cientismo... Dennett diz que os padrões são informações que nos permitem generalizar, para que tenhamos uma hipótese de precisão maior do que se estivéssemos perante um sistema completamente aleatório.

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Chomsky's Merge and the Evolution of Language
Philosophy.content: "As palavras são os melhores memes, mas não os primeiros memes." A língua é vista como uma forma de produtividade e utilidade para expressar significados, que permite a comunicação sobre tudo, com um número relativamente pequeno de palavras. A meu ver, em vez de atribuir um som a uma coisa, construímos essa coisa com base em palavras estruturais, que ligam as palavras entre si para construir o significado. Imagine-se a trabalheira que era dar um nome novo à manteiga de amendoim, ou manteiga de cacau, em vez de simplesmente dizer manteiga e depois adicionar o restritivo. O nosso sistema de linguagem evoluiu a partir da acumulação gradual de pressões imediatas. "Existe uma correlação estatística negativa entre a complexidade morfológica de uma língua e a quantidade de pessoas que a falam." - Hurford. Chomsky, na sua revisão à sua teoria, pensa num operador, um merger, que tem recursividade, o que origina a língua. O merge une as partes da frase. É a operação lógica que nos separa dos outros animais. As línguas originaram por um processo de seleção natural, vindo da protolíngua, que por divergência evolutiva deu origem às outras. Esta divergência foi originada por exemplo por drift "genético", porque quando as populações se separaram e deixaram de comunicar umas com as outras, a sua cultura foi-se afastando cada vez mais, até que chegou a uma altura em que deixaram de se perceber uns aos outros. Tornaram-se espécies diferentes. Uma teoria da origem da linguagem nos humanos diz que passou das mãos (gestos) para a cara e depois para a boca. "That confident, I-dare-you-to-knock-this-chip-off-my-shoulder tone has never left Chomsky. He still sounds like Euclid justifying the axioms of plane geometry. Evidence, I don’t need no evidence for these assumptions, but you do if you want to challenge them." Chomsky diz que a linguagem é, antes de uma forma de comunicação, uma ferramenta de pensamento, que permitiu aos indivíduos que a usaram uma vantagem. Chomsky basicamente não quer saber dos comportamentos, dos produtos, mas sim dos mecanismos. Quanto aos comportamentos, se forem úteis para explicar o mecanismo boa, senão caga. Segundo ele, e já vindo de Descartes, a linguagem é diferente dos outros processos da filosofia mecânica. A linguagem não tem limites e é indeterminada.

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Memes as Symbionts and the Evolution of Logic
Philosophy.content: As palavras são domesticadas pelos bebés. Transformam o que encontram na natureza (ouvir os outros a falar) em algo que incluem na sua imagem manifesta, mesmo que ainda não o saibam. "A compreensão e aprovação humana não é necessária nem suficiente para a fixação de um meme numa cultura." Os memes têm aptidão própria, e são coisas informativas. A teoria tradicional da cultura é de que se baseia em ideias e crenças. Mas nem sempre a cultura evolui conscientemente. Podemos adotar memes sem sequer nos apercebermos disso. A expressão "that's nasty" não foi propositada, aliás, nem faz muito sentido tendo em conta o seu significado, mas mesmo assim foi propagada. "As palavras e memes que infectam os ancestrais são simbiontes valiosos cujo valor não dependia de serem reconhecidos ou mesmo apreciados pelos seus hospedeiros." Alguns consideram os memes meros parasitas, não fornecendo contribuição para fenómenos culturais complexos e "permanentes" como a religião. "Somos a única espécie que conseguiu encontrar uma perspetiva que desloca a aptidão genética como o propósito mais elevado, o summum bonum da vida." "Somos a única espécie que descobriu outras coisas pelas quais vale a pena morrer: liberdade, VERDADE (...)" Há quem proponha que a origem da lógica e do nosso res cogitans é no facto de ser melhor em termos evolucionários ganhar debates, mesmo que pelas razões erradas ou com razões erradas. "O nosso talento subjacente favorece decisões que são fáceis de justificar mas não necessariamente melhores."

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Type vs Token and the Consciousness Spectrum
Philosophy.content: As palavras não são tão específicas como se pode pensar. Podem ser escritas, ouvidas, pensadas no cérebro. E até pode dar-se o caso de termos ideias sem sequer ter noção das palavras que estamos a empregar. Por vezes as palavras deixam-nos pendurados, mas por vezes não necessitamos delas para pensar nas ideias. Dennett fala da distinção entre tipo (type) e ocorrência (token). Embora o Word (programa de computadores) do meu computador seja uma ocorrência, quando eu falo do Word estou a falar do tipo. Todas as ocorrências, de uma forma ou de outra, num formato ou noutro, acrescenta, são réplicas. Isto é, na minha opinião, uma alusão ao mundo das ideias platónico. Dennett acha que a consciência é um processo gradual, num espetro, e não uma coisa que divide o universo em dois. "Tal como o despertar de um sono profundo é muitas vezes realizado gradualmente".

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Memetic Evolution and Lamarckian Words
Philosophy.content: Estas criaturas, de uma maneira ou de outra, extraem a informação do ambiente, tornando-se mais eficazes "graças à informação que agora podem utilizar para fazer todo o tipo de coisas novas, incluindo o desenvolvimento de novas formas de se informarem ainda mais. Os ricos ficam mais ricos. (...) usando a sua informação para refinar a informação que obtêm através dos sistemas que concebem para melhorar a informação de que dispõem, quando se propõem a conceber algo." "Os dólares (versão física do dinheiro) são como muletas ontológicas". Objeção aos memes: dizem que são "distintos" e fielmente transmitidos, mas as evoluções culturais não mostram sinais disto. Noto agora que quando se diz que uma música, por exemplo, é intemporal, isto significa que não é local no espaço-tempo. A passagem do testemunho entre humanos foi tão eficaz que a música se espalhou pelo bloco do espaço-tempo. Quanto à replicação memética, em níveis superiores disto é necessária compreensão. Para passar de uma língua a outra é preciso compreensão, mas, depois de passado esse obstáculo, a competência sem compreensão volta a dominar. Nestes obstáculos perde-se a nitidez, a fidelidade dos memes. Os NFT são o local perfeito para os engenheiros meméticos. "Pode-se muitas vezes distinguir os bons dos não tão bons sem saber exatamente porque é que os bons são bons." As palavras, num certo sentido, são lamarckianas, porque quanto mais usamos, mais descendentes têm e mais usamos. Quanto à origem da língua, Dennett faz uma pergunta que nem ocorreria à representativa quantidade de pessoas: quem foram os maiores beneficiários: as palavras ou os falantes? "Usa uma palavra 3 vezes e é tua". "As palavras, quando chegam a outro cérebro podem entrar por um ouvido e sair por outro, ou podem, ocasionalmente, criar raízes." "As palavras são moradoras preeminentes da nossa imagem manifesta."

Philosophy.title: Dennett: The Decentralized Brain and Unconscious Neurons
Philosophy.content: Todo o brilhantismo e compreensão no mundo surgem, em última análise, a partir de competências incompreensíveis, compostas ao longo do tempo em sistemas cada vez mais complexos. Dennett parece tocar individualmente numa ideia: eu acho que o cérebro é descentralizado. Não como ele diz, aquela coisa do gestor central, a célula chefe, é uma ilusão. A meu ver, isto é a descentralização. E acontece deste modo porque a única maneira de células intrinsecamente egoístas cooperarem é se todas cooperarem, sem exceção; se uma faz batota, as outras poderão retaliar, e de um momento ao outro, num efeito dominó cataclísmico instaura-se o caos. Se houvesse um chefe que tivesse todo o poder, não abdicaria do seu principal objetivo: descendência. Não resistiria à força dos genes que lhe causavam a tentação de roubar a energia das células circundantes e usá-la para os seus próprios objetivos. Depois, a evolução foi "inteligente" em não ser inteligente. Os neurónios não fazem puto de ideia do que estão a fazer, não têm consciência, e por isso não são céticos. Simplesmente fazem o que lhes é pedido. Como os neurónios são ingénuos quanto ao seu objetivo e são obrigados a não ceder às suas tentações próprias (não se reproduzem devido ao período G0). Ou seja, a evolução evitou a consciência dos neurónios para que trabalhassem juntos, em equipa e sem reclamar, para que dessem origem a um ser mais complexo, com consciência. A evolução, nas suas tentativas e erros, manteve o seguinte princípio: não deixes criar consciência na base, senão somos descobertos e o nosso plano é destruído "from the inside". E, no entanto, nas suas tentativas e erros não percebeu que evitando a consciência originou consciência macroscópica. E cá estamos nós a desvendar o plano maléfico da evolução.

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Deductivism and Generating Comprehension
Philosophy.content: O que Darwin e Turing fizeram foi prever a versão mais extrema deste ponto: todo o brilhantismo e compreensão no mundo surgem, em última análise, a partir de competências incompreensíveis, compostas ao longo do tempo em sistemas cada vez mais competentes - e, portanto, compreensivos. Dennett não fala nisto, mas reparei que na passagem do indutivismo científico para o dedutivismo científico de Popper, por exemplo, há uma inversão de raciocínio. No indutivismo, pegamos nas observações e generalizamos, criando uma teoria. No dedutivismo, começamos pela teoria e só depois testamo-la experimentalmente. Ou seja, somos competentes (fazemos a teoria), antes sequer de perceber se faz sentido, antes de obter a compreensão acerca do mundo a posteriori. É novamente um processo de competência sem compreensão até porque, depois dos testes experimentais, sujeitamos as teorias à seleção natural, sobrevivendo as mais adequadas. We generate comprehension through the fact that we had competence to formulate good theories and less good ones. As cópias evolutivas devem ser altamente fiéis, mas não perfeitas, para deixarem espaço a mutações vantajosas. Ou seja, o sinal evolutivo tem de se sobrepor ao ruído, mantendo-se basicamente intacto ao longo do tempo para a seleção natural poder fazer o seu trabalho. A evolução tem de ocorrer gradualmente, em pequenos passos, para haver estabilidade. Depois, as aptidões selecionadas devem depender de propriedades intrínsecas. Não deve ser 100 por cento sorte.

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Darwinian, Skinnerian, Popperian, and Gregorian Creatures
Philosophy.content: Dennett distingue quatro tipos de criaturas: Darwinianas: as mais simples, são dogmáticas, não olham para o ambiente em que estão, simplesmente cumprem as regras que os genes lhes impõem. Deixam que a seleção natural decida o seu destino, não mudam, não adaptam, apostam as fichas todas nas suas características e fazem all-in. Skinnerianas: são as próximas, têm plasticidade no seu comportamento, vão fazendo as coisas por tentativa e erro, melhorando as suas hipóteses de, em condições desfavoráveis, sobreviverem. Eles próprios moldam os seus comportamentos para satisfazerem a seleção natural; no entanto, por fazerem simplesmente as coisas por tentativa e erro, são criaturas completamente à posteriori. Fazem, se der certo continuam e se der errado na próxima não fazem. Mas pode não haver próxima, devido ao seu erro. Sujeitam-se a todas as possibilidades e sofrem a consequência de cada uma delas. As Darwinianas fazem all-in numa tática na sua vida inteira. As Skinnerianas fazem all-in numa tática durante um momento. E as Popperianas? Fazem tudo à posteriori no domínio à priori das coisas. Experimentam mentalmente, morrem mentalmente, e depois fazem o que devem no mundo real. As Gregorianas, como nós, fazem uso de instrumentos. Os gregorianos MUDAM não só a si mas também ao mundo, para que a seleção natural se adapte a seu favor.

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Language Evolution and the Feeling of Meaning
Philosophy.content: "A linguagem evoluiu para se ajustar ao cérebro antes de o cérebro evoluir para melhor acomodar a linguagem." "Sócrates ficou intrigado com a forma como as pessoas conseguiam descobrir a definição de uma palavra a partir de uma simples conversa entre elas. Se não soubessem já o significado da palavra, de que é que serviria consultarem-se uns aos outros, e se já soubessem o seu significado, porquê dar-se ao trabalho de a definir (...) compreender uma palavra não é o mesmo que ter adquirido uma definição da mesma". A meu ver, o significado é um "sentimento", sente-se o significado. E, já agora, neste processo de aprender o significado a partir de uma conversa, é como se estivéssemos a detetar padrões, fazendo ciência, formulando hipóteses e tentando comprová-las da próxima vez. É engraçado que podemos todos dizer uma palavra e sentir coisas completamente diferentes quanto ao seu significado. Quanto à definição, definir assume que já temos a compreensão de outras palavras. É parecido com o "qual primeiro: galinha ou ovo?" e parece que os hominídeos ancestrais, não tendo definições, tiveram de dizer uma palavra e simplesmente senti-la com base nas suas consequências, ou no seu contexto. E a partir daí começaram a usá-la para determinada coisa. Implica isto que ambos têm uma confiança em que o outro pensa o mesmo acerca da dada palavra. "No que diz respeito à compreensão, todos nós dependemos de algo como uma divisão de trabalho: contamos com especialistas para ter uma compreensão profunda e 'completa' de conceitos difíceis, em que confiamos todos os dias, compreendendo-os apenas por metade. Esta é de facto, como veremos, uma das principais contribuições da linguagem para a inteligência da nossa espécie: a capacidade de transmitir, fielmente, informações que pouco entendemos."

Philosophy.title: Dennett: The De-Darwinization of Human Culture
Philosophy.content: "A afirmação que defendo é que a cultura humana começou profundamente darwinista, com competências sem compreensão que produziram várias estruturas relevantes, mais ou menos da mesma forma como as térmitas constroem os seus formigueiros e depois, gradualmente, desdarwinizou-se, obtendo cada vez mais compreensão, cada vez mais capacidade de organização de cima para baixo, cada vez mais eficiência nas suas formas de procurar o espaço do design." Basicamente, acho que a evolução genética criou a evolução cultural que se revolta contra a evolução genética, não só percebendo-a mas também contrariando o darwinismo. "Picasso uma vez disse: 'eu não procuro, eu encontro'. Esta é uma perfeita vanglória de génio."

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Darwin's Inversion of Reasoning and Emergent Telos
Philosophy.content: A evolução do "como" (o processo narrativo de explicar as coisas) para o "para quê" (o telos, o propósito da coisa). Dennett argumenta que não é o telos que origina o como, mas sim o contrário. Não é o designer inteligente que cria o universo e dá o propósito a tudo e depois há uma narrativa do universo que podemos descodificar. Mas sim, há uma narrativa do universo que, pela sua natureza (a biologia funciona com evolução darwinista), gera um telos emergente. "Uma boa ação pode ser mal executada e até falhar na sua finalidade, enquanto uma boa ferramenta pode ser um dispositivo de tortura eficiente ou uma arma maléfica. Podemos ver o mesmo contraste em casos negativos, na distinção entre maldoso e estúpido. As pessoas podem castigar alguém por ser maldoso, na sua opinião, mas a própria natureza pode castigá-lo despropositadamente por ser estúpido (...) A valorização da razão é um produto da evolução posterior, mais avançado que as razões." Na teoria com que temos de lidar, a ignorância absoluta é o artífice: "para fazer uma máquina perfeita e bela, não é necessário saber como fazê-la. Esta proposta será encontrada, após cuidadoso exame, para expressar em poucas palavras todo o significado do Sr. Darwin. Que, por uma estranha inversão de raciocínio, parece pensar a ignorância absoluta como totalmente qualificada para tomar o lugar da sabedoria absoluta em todas as realizações de habilidade criativa." (Beverley, 1868)

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Competence Without Comprehension
Philosophy.content: "Tudo o que é da forma que é, é porque atingiu essa forma." - D'Arcy Thompson "Esta é uma pergunta retórica. E tentar responder a perguntas retóricas em vez de ficar intimidado por elas é um bom hábito a cultivar." "A evolução é mais inteligente que tu." - Francis Crick "O nosso ceticismo sobre a competência sem compreensão tem causas, não razões." Ou seja, a causa não é a razão. É, por exemplo, a mania de ser teimoso. "Um elevador automatizado funciona bem porque tem a ontologia certa. Ou seja, as coisas que sabe são as coisas que interessam para funcionar bem." Utiliza variáveis que acompanham todas as características do mundo que importam para fazer o seu trabalho e é alheio a tudo o resto. Quando as pessoas são cegas quanto às implicações do que fazem, gera-se uma dinâmica de competência sem compreensão. Se muitas pessoas estiverem a trabalhar para o bem maior e não o compreenderem, podem ter avanços fabulosos, sem compreensão.

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Emotions, Culture, and the Magic Trick of the Mind
Philosophy.content: "A irritação (como outras reações emocionais agudas), são uma espécie de sublinhado na nossa memória, tornando-se menos suscetíveis de ser esquecidas." "Veem a cultura humana como algo transcendente, o dom milagroso que nos distingue dos animais, o último baluarte contra o reducionismo crescente, contra o determinismo genético, contra o filistinismo que pensam ver na ciência moderna." "Muitos dos truques de magia mais desconcertantes dependem de o público não imaginar os limites ridiculamente estapafúrdios a que os mágicos chegam para obter esse efeito desconcertante."

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Cartesian Gravity and the Illusion of Expertise
Philosophy.content: "Sim, temos alma, mas é feita de tantos pequenos robots." Sobre a consciência: "Além disso, neste assunto toda a gente é especialista. As pessoas recebem calmamente informações sobre as propriedades químicas do cálcio ou os detalhes microbiológicos do cancro, mas pensam que têm uma autoridade pessoal particular sobre a natureza das suas próprias experiências conscientes que lhes permite oporem-se a qualquer hipótese que achem inaceitável." Ferida cartesiana: o paradigma que separa a ciência moderna, do dualismo alma-corpo. Gravidade cartesiana: uma força de distorção que distorce a nossa imaginação, faz com que vejamos que as coisas se começam a encaixar de uma nova maneira, lançando o nosso pensamento em novas órbitas. Podemos escolher protegermo-nos ou explorá-las. "A gravidade cartesiana atua sobre ideias, ou representações das coisas, não em proporção à massa do objeto (Newton), mas em proporção à sua proximidade de conteúdo com outras ideias, que desempenham papéis privilegiados na manutenção de um ser vivo."

Philosophy.title: Dennett: Evolution of Minds and Solved Mysteries
Philosophy.content: "Como podem existir mentes? E como é possível que essas mentes perguntem e respondam a essa pergunta? A resposta curta é que as mentes evoluíram e criaram ferramentas de pensamento que, em determinada altura, lhes permitiram saber como evoluíram e até saber como essas ferramentas permitiram que elas soubessem o que são mentes." "É um mistério impenetrável!" ou "foi Deus que fez". No fim de contas, eles podem estar certos, é claro, mas dada a fabulosa riqueza de ferramentas de pensamento que temos à nossa disposição, e até agora pouco utilizadas, esta é uma rendição manifestamente prematura. "Algumas pessoas gostariam de persuadir os curiosos a manter as mãos afastadas dos mistérios queridos, não percebendo que um mistério resolvido é ainda mais arrebatador que as fantasias ignorantes que ele substitui."

Philosophy.title: The Selfish Gene: Altruism as an Egoistic Strategy
Philosophy.content: Os nossos genes são tão egoístas que às vezes até são altruístas só para satisfazer os seus objetivos individualistas. "Tentamos ensinar o altruísmo e generosidade porque nascemos egoístas." "Na luta pela sobrevivência são as diferenças que importam." "As máquinas de sobrevivência que podem simular o futuro estão um passo à frente das que podem aprender apenas com base na tentativa e erro." Cooperação, egoísmo, altruísmo.

Philosophy.title: The Selfish Gene: Order, Chaos, and Hierarchical Stability
Philosophy.content: It seems to me that as populações tentam instaurar a ordem. A ordem, até certo ponto, ou seja, o equilíbrio "correto" entre ordem e caos é uma estratégia evolutivamente estável. Isto porque Dawkins fala na hierarquização e na territorialidade. Isto são separações arbitrárias mas que formam um sistema. Embora seja "injusto" para os que estão no topo da hierarquia e os que estão debaixo, há um sistema que é respeitado (teoricamente), que conduz a estabilidade provisória.

Philosophy.title: The Selfish Gene: Competition and the Survival of the Stable
