Philosophy.content: "Exemplos escolhidos nunca são evidência séria para qualquer generalização válida." A seleção natural é um caso específico da lei geral da sobrevivência do estável. A competição surge pela diferença entre os recursos finitos e as infinitas probabilidades.

Philosophy.title: The Selfish Gene: Unconscious Slaves and Shifts in Perspective
Philosophy.content: Somos escravos inconscientes dos genes. A evolução dos humanos é tão rápida (através das crenças... que se sobrepõe à evolução filogenética). Existem duas maneiras de encarar a seleção natural: do ponto de vista do indivíduo e do gene. O todo é a parte que constrói o todo. Quando compreendidas corretamente, estas maneiras são equivalentes, duas perspetivas de uma verdade única. A melhor contribuição de um cientista é frequentemente não dar uma teoria ou um facto novo, mas sim dar um novo olhar sobre teorias e factos antigos.

Philosophy.title: Altered Traits: First-Person Experience and Projected Reality
Philosophy.content: "A nossa experiência não se baseia na percepção direta daquilo que está a acontecer, mas em grande medida nas nossas expectativas e projeções, os pensamentos e reações habituais que aprendemos a dar em resposta a um impenetrável mar de processos neuronais. Vivemos num mundo que as nossas mentes constroem em vez de percebermos verdadeiramente os infindáveis pormenores daquilo que está a acontecer." Há uma grande discrepância entre a primeira pessoa e a terceira pessoa. Por isso, segundo o Dalai Lama: "a primeira pessoa a ser estudada e a pessoa que faz a pesquisa são a mesma pessoa." "No início nada vem, no meio nada permanece, no fim nada parte." - Jetsun Milarepa "Ausência de indício não é indício de ausência."

Philosophy.title: Altered Traits: Awareness and the Deconstruction of Pain
Philosophy.content: "Aquilo que toma consciência da tristeza não é triste. Aquilo que tem consciência do medo não é medroso. No momento em que me perco nos pensamentos porém, fico tão confuso como qualquer outro." Esta parte do livro foca a sua atenção na dor. Reduz esta sensação a "sensações físicas" e "somáticas constituintes". E defende que com a meditação sentimos as coisas pelo que são, aproximando-nos da realidade da dor. Somos indiferentes no sentido em que, focando a nossa atenção em coisas simples, periódicas, esquece-se um pouquinho das sensações emergentes. "Se na verdade déssemos atenção da forma correta à natureza da nossa experiência, esta mudaria drasticamente."

Philosophy.title: Altered Traits: Releasing the Ego
Philosophy.content: Um dos objetivos da prática meditativa é largar a noção do "eu". "Desde que te agarres ao eu, continuarás agarrado ao mundo do sofrimento." - Vasubandhu "Quando estás ocupado com o eu, estás separado de Deus. A via de Deus está apenas a um passo: um passo para fora de ti."

Philosophy.title: Altered Traits: The Scientific Method and Null Findings
Philosophy.content: A ciência atualiza-se: as novas ideias são criações destrutivas, que infectam o mundo. As ideias corretas tendem a ficar e difundir-se. A importância do ambiente circundante. Um rato de laboratório melhora o seu cérebro por estar numa gaiola bonita e ter coisas que fazer. Sobre a aplicação do método científico: muito poucos tentam replicar a experiência de outros, simplesmente presume-se que esta é sólida. Os cientistas raramente tentam publicar estudos quando não têm resultados significativos. E, contudo, a descoberta nula tem significado.

Philosophy.title: Altered Traits: Letting Go and Compassion
Philosophy.content: "A subtil consciência da consciência." "Se aparecer um pensamento, toma nota dele e deixa-o ir embora. Quando te libertares de todos os teus apegos, serás o próprio zazen." - Dōgen Arthur Zajonc, filósofo e físico quântico, disse: se libertarmo-nos dos apegos tornamo-nos mais abertos à nossa própria experiência e às outras pessoas. Essa abertura, uma forma de amor, permite que nos aproximemos com maior facilidade do sofrimento dos outros. As grandes almas parecem incorporar a capacidade de lidar com o sofrimento sem entrarem em colapso. Desapegarmo-nos é libertador, criando um eixo moral para a ação e a compaixão.

Philosophy.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Knowing vs Understanding
Philosophy.content: We know more than we understand and we understand less than we know. We know more than we know we know, and we understand less than we understand we understand. We understand less about what we understand than know about what we know. Knowing is based on the known, understanding happens at the frontier of the unknown. Intuition and instinct have us being able way more than we'd give ourselves credit for. We don't know we are able to do something until we are put in the position, until we try. Until we gain awareness of the knowledge built on us by shining light on it, by using it, we don't know of its presence. This is discovered knowledge, as opposed to invented knowledge. But most of what we know we don't understand, and there are levels to understanding, of course. Saying that we understand less than we know is not just switching the sides of the equation, it's saying something different too: that we understand less than we think we understand, and then we also understand less than we understand we understand.

Philosophy.title: Altered Traits: Power, Possession, and the Illusion of Control
Philosophy.content: A luta entre o adquirido e o inato. Somos nós que modelamos o nosso comportamento ou são os genes? O poder é uma ilusão temporária e a posse também. Quando Alexandre Magno encontrou um grupo de iogues ascéticos, estes reagiam com indiferença dizendo que, na verdade, Alexandre e os soldados só podiam possuir o solo que pisavam e que iam morrer como os iogues, um dia.

Philosophy.title: How Chess Imitates Life: The Warrior in Silence and Purposeful Action
Philosophy.content: In the field of battle, right in the middle. Right where all the action goes on, whereas the abstractions are to be laid out by those only seeing from above, the details worked out by those in but not on. A fulminant presence, you are in, you are out, you leave the smoke where you go. And that perhaps this greater kind of strength is cultivated in silence. A wisdom is developed in silence, that is for sure. A wisdom that gives one the ability to then speak from the heart. Silence is a thing of honor. A warrior thus shines in the very few occasions light is shot onto him; all the rest, all the shadow work, contributes to a peaceful and purposeful journey. A decisiveness of heart, an alignment of decision. The hero doesn't intend to act, but he acts when it is right. He doesn't try to make it on purpose, but he makes it purposeful. His help isn't made purposefully, but purposeful. Not he that makes himself a hero, but rather a hero that is made of him. Being able to remain calm amid the storm and move vehemently in the face of calm. And yeah, were there people in the middle of battles that stood there just to record, before with writing, maybe later taking selfies, perhaps a neutral party that both opponents agreed would deal with detailing the history of what went on. The psychology of the individual soldiers, their individual movements. Even in something like war, a reciprocal willing to document would bring about this risky endeavor of recording. That I hoped I had today and all my life really. Someone perhaps not even present, rather a visitor from another dimension. ente e formulou as questões certas. Infelizmente não pôde revelar ao mundo se as questões estavam certas quando efetivamente morreu.

Philosophy.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Holding Contradictory Thoughts and High Standards
Philosophy.content: That to be human is the ability to hold contradictory thoughts. Then aligning thoughts with reality is really a measure of how great a human you can be. To take a tough loss yet still believe you are the best. To not forget your deeper beliefs about yourself. To not lose the plot, to keep the big picture in hat, yet if you were to tell people they would laugh. This ability to sustain the strain of having all these contradictory thoughts gives you space to great wonders. To believe fucking amazing things yet still carry the weight of being realistic. Thinking highly of yourself allows you at moments to change chip and bam you are on fire. To have unwavering belief in what's around you, not thinking deep down it isn't enough, yet abstract yourself away from that surrounding, rendering yourself independent yet rocking with what you have. Don't be afraid of expecting high from your surroundings, in having high standards for what you allow. That means that perhaps you will be shut off from it, maybe you won't participate in this or that, but don't be afraid to know you are working on something greater, and you won't allow your underdeveloped background and surrounding to dictate how high you are working. Eventually, your surrounding may catch up to you, and in that case, it will be right to engage with it, but if it doesn't present you the level you seek, don't hesitate to abstain. Bite the bullet and dare to think higher, don't succumb to the pressure of following everyone into the pit. Don't close your bubble. Always in control.

Philosophy.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Psychology Follows Action
Philosophy.content: Quote: "There is no psychology, only biography and autobiography." It matters only what happened from other's perspective and from your perspective. Psychology comes late, only after the fact. Actions lead the way.

Philosophy.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Strategy vs Tactics and Philosophical Suicide
Philosophy.content: Tactics is a plan for when you have something to do, but strategy is a plan for when there's nothing to do. This is a very powerful concept. In chess, one tries to work in the trenches, improving position bit by bit. In life, one can, instead of wasting time, get better prepared for the war. Getting a better position on your opponents. This is where the differentiation happens. Everyone has a clear and cut lead when they know what they have to do. But when they don't, like in holidays, there is real value in finding yourself, in doing something. In the first case, you are really not doing more than you are obliged to. But in the second, you have freedom, and again, this for most is the torture of angst; for amidst infinite choices they may have to stick to one, and as they are unable by strength to do so, they get stuck on themselves. Stick to not be stuck. Because people don't want to stay in the discomfort of having to decide, they settle to do what has already been done. They give in to the system. When they notice, perhaps they've already crossed the event horizon, effectively having committed philosophical suicide. The surest fire way of not acknowledging the absurd is giving in to it. Yeah, there may be peace for a long time, but life is won in moments. When the moment comes, everything else becomes obsolete; it is life or death in those moments. "There is but one truly serious philosophical problem, and that is suicide," said Camus. Will you come alive in the moment or not? To be alive is not to be taken for granted. So many wish they could be alive.

Philosophy.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Knowing How vs Knowing Why
Philosophy.content: "The one who knows how will always have a job. But the one who also knows why will always be his boss" - Ralph Waldo Emerson. If you know how to use things in the system, you will be used by the system. People can be sitting very close to so much more, if they only knew stuff. Instead, this vague cloud makes them work in ignorance, making them targets of the pilots flying.

Philosophy.title: The Death of Ivan Ilyich: The Incomprehensible Truth of Dying
Philosophy.content: "A morte.. Sim, a morte. Eles não sabem nada, não querem saber. Recusam-se a lamentar-me... Divertem-se. Não se importam, mas também hão de morrer! Cambada de imbecis! Eu vou à frente deles, mas eles não escapam!... E divertem-se... Ah, que animais." "Sabia que ia finar-se (morrer) mas não era capaz de se habituar a tal ideia, de a admitir, de a compreender." A verdade que todos sabem mas ninguém compreende. Quando se compreende, deixa de se saber e existir. "Essa mentira geralmente aceite e adotada, segundo a qual ele estava doente mas não a morrer, (...) para quê mentir, para quê esconder aquilo que ele não IGNORAVA." "Para Ilyich nada mais há no mundo além daquela dor que não abranda um só instante."

Philosophy.title: The Death of Ivan Ilyich: The Final Breath and the End of Death
Philosophy.content: "O fim ali estava, e contudo as dúvidas não se tinham dissipado." "'Acabou-se!' disse uma voz por cima dele. Ouviu estas palavras e repetiu-as na sua alma. 'Acabou-se a morte!...', pensou. 'Já não existe!!' Ivan Ilyich aspirou uma lufada de ar, mas parou a meio, inteiriçou os membros e morreu." Tolstói morreu antes de morrer, ele experienciou pessoalmente e formulou as questões certas. Infelizmente não pôde revelar ao mundo se as questões estavam certas quando efetivamente morreu.

Philosophy.title: The Death of Ivan Ilyich: The Indifferent Doctor and the Search for Miracles
Philosophy.content: Para o médico: "não se tratava da vida de Ilyich mas de uma briga entre o rim flutuante e o apêndice". "O acusado (Ilyich) julgou compreender que a sua situação não era das mais invejáveis, mas que tal circunstância pouca importância tinha para o clínico e para o resto do mundo. Esta conclusão dilacerou profundamente o infeliz Ilyich, inspirou-lhe uma viva compaixão por si mesmo, e muita cólera contra o indiferente médico, que virava as costas a uma questão crucial." "Não parou de refletir nas palavras do médico, de tentar traduzir em linguagem clara os seus termos técnicos, nebulosos, e enredados, a fim de ler neles uma resposta para a sua interrogação." "Uma senhora falou na sua presença de uma cura milagrosa pelas sagradas imagens. Ilyich deu consigo a ouvi-la com toda a seriedade e a acreditar na eficácia do processo. Ficou pasmado: será possível que eu me tenha tornado assim tão estúpido?" "Reabsorção, excreção e tudo regressava à normalidade. (...) mas é preciso dar um empurrãozinho à natureza."

Philosophy.title: The Blind Watchmaker: Lenz's Law of Evolution and Asexual Extinction
Philosophy.content: As pressões seletivas, se pensarmos bem, funcionam de acordo com a Lei de Lenz. Para o sistema evolutivo estar em equilíbrio, quando há uma pressão, deve tentar contrariá-la. E isso acontece selecionando aqueles que melhor possam contrariá-la para restaurar o equilíbrio. "Evoluir é uma coisa que as espécies fazem, não uma coisa que os organismos individuais fazem". Mais uma diferença entre nós e os restantes seres vivos... Nós evoluímos individualmente. E com o treino, por exemplo, adquirimos novas skills de design. "Segundo aquele argumento, as espécies assexuadas, quando ocorrem, têm tendência a extinguir-se, porque não evoluem suficientemente depressa para acompanharem o ambiente em mudança. As sexuadas (...) evoluem suficientemente depressa para o acompanhar." Nós temos tanta variabilidade e contingência que ultrapassamos este ritmo, somos agora nós próprios a criar a mudança. A seleção natural é que se adapta à nossa evolução. "A seleção darwinista, por outro lado, não tem dificuldade em explicar até o mais pequeno pormenor". Parece-me que a seleção natural está próxima da teoria de Freud, da psicanálise, quanto à sua cientificidade. Parece que qualquer fenómeno que aconteça, nós dizemos: a teoria de Darwin não está errada logo isto tem de ser benéfico para a espécie. E torna-se então numa luta por encontrar razões suficientemente convincentes, mesmo que dificilmente testadas, que achemos que justifiquem a vantagem.

Philosophy.title: The Death of Ivan Ilyich: Escaping Death with Distractions
Philosophy.content: O corpo do morto: "E, assim, dirigiam aos vivos uma espécie de censura ou de memento mori." Passados 5 minutos: "O incidente da morte de Ivan Ilyich em caso algum deve ser considerado pretexto bastante para suspender a audiência. Nada nos deve impedir de, nessa mesma noite, bater um jogo de cartas(...)" Acho que eles sentiram a morte e, para se afastarem disso, começaram a jogar um jogo para perder o tempo. Porque gastando o tempo é o mesmo que virarmo-nos para a morte e dizer: eu não quero saber, a tua existência não me assusta nem me inflige a utilizar o tempo que me dás. Mas será que eles não têm medo ou fingem não ter medo para esconder que têm medo? E quando chegar a altura? Vão-se apressar ou não? "Palavras de circunstância". "Pode acontecer a todo o momento, imediatamente - pensou apavorado. Mas quase de seguida (e sem saber como), recordou-se de que aquela infelicidade acontecera a Ivan, e que ela não devia nem podia atingi-lo. Repreendeu-se intimamente, censurou-se por se ter deixado levar por uma penosa impressão momentânea, por uma emoção perfeitamente supérflua, ao acreditar no semblante de Schwarz."

Philosophy.title: The Blind Watchmaker: Punctuated Equilibrium and Mutational Impact
Philosophy.content: Dawkins diz que não costumamos ver evolução nas palavras, melhoramento. Pensamos que a linguagem degenera, porque tornamos os significados corruptos. Aquilo que era uma estrela de cinema é hoje qualquer ator, daí existir "superestrela". Aquilo que era o chef, hoje são todos os cozinheiros. "A expressão tautológica 'chefe principal'". Quanto mais drástica a mutação menos probabilidade de ser boa (o benefício evolutivo converge para 0). Quanto menos drástica (quanto o tamanho tende para 0), o benefício converge para 50 por cento de probabilidade. Acho que a refutação do saltacionismo (a evolução acontece devido a grandes mutações) que Dawkins faz não é a mais adequada. Isso da macromutação é inteiramente relativo. Se me deres o tamanho da mutação eu digo-te a probabilidade, mas dizer macro ou micro não faz muito sentido. Todas contribuem, embora as micro contribuam mais frequentemente mas com menos impacto. Tarán! O processo de especiação também acontece com os memes. Se há alguma barreira que nos separa de outro, seja espaço, forma como fomos ensinados, língua que falamos, então os memes não passam, a não ser que fosse por tunelamento quântico, just kidding. Por isso há divergência cultural, no tal reforço positivo que Dawkins tinha mencionado noutro contexto. O pontualismo é uma espiral infinita, porque ao dizer que os períodos graduais são interrompidos com uma catástrofe súbita. Mas na verdade isto começa a ser "the new norm". Por isso, embora a probabilidade vá diminuindo cada vez mais à medida que vamos contornando a espiral, haverão catástrofes, eventos que interrompem esta série de "gradual gradual pum!". Depois podemos reagrupar as coisas, o que leva a uma nova catástrofe ainda menos provável...

Philosophy.title: The Blind Watchmaker: Gradual Evolution and Probability
Philosophy.content: Dawkins explica em termos probabilísticos por que a seleção natural seleciona seleções naturais em que não hajam saltos enormes, isto é, organismos em que cada mutação tenha um potencial de mudar muitos genes. Tal implicaria que, dentro do hiperespaço de biomorfos, poderia, em uma geração, um búfalo reproduzir-se num javali. Como, probabilisticamente, há mais formas de estar morto do que estar vivo, seria muito raro que nestes saltos gigantes algo de útil acontecesse. A evolução contínua e gradual gera-se por haverem variáveis contínuas, que depois vão sendo alteradas. A Lei de Dollo diz, por probabilidade, que é muito improvável haverem dois caminhos evolutivos rigorosamente iguais. Com tanta opção, é pouco provável isto acontecer. Claro que uma adaptação que foi suficientemente boa para evoluir uma vez é suficientemente boa para evoluir outra vez noutra espécie (convergência evolutiva). Dawkins aponta para uma ideia muito interessante: os indivíduos atuais guardam a sabedoria (wisdom) de todos os seus antepassados, nos seus genes e em si, melhorando a biblioteca de informação de geração em geração. O ADN de uma célula tem tanta informação como 30 enciclopédias da Britânica. Os mecanismos de proteção do arquivo do ADN são muito bons, a frequência de mutação é baixa. E como é o fator limitante, a seleção natural também é lenta.

Philosophy.title: The Blind Watchmaker: Organisms as Temporary Vehicles for DNA
Philosophy.content: Os organismos vivos funcionam como servidores do ADN, e não o contrário. O ADN vive milhares de anos, cada pessoa é um "veículo temporário", é um simples hospedeiro, que apenas tem de obedecer aos desejos do ADN. "Quanto mais nos afastamos de milagres, de grandes improbabilidades, de coincidências fantásticas, de grandes acontecimentos casuais, mais exaustivamente seremos capazes de decompor esses grandes acontecimentos casuais numa série cumulativa de pequenos acontecimentos graduais, tanto mais satisfatórias serão as nossas explicações para um espírito racional". Isto porque, a meu ver, apesar de coincidências explicarem as coisas, explicar as coisas através delas não é basicamente nada, porque não podemos explicar as coincidências; só há uma razão para acontecerem: são uma coincidência.

Philosophy.title: The Myth of Sisyphus: The Problem of Suicide and the Meanings of Life
Philosophy.content: O único problema filosófico verdadeiramente sério é o suicídio. I thought that what to do with life was the real question, that which would light up people and debate the most ferociously. That which would connect the most people, the widest and the deepest. The question that no one dares not to answer, whether they know it or not. The question which no one can afford to be neutral on. And yet the life ended by taking one's life shows disregard for the value of life, the very thing that made the question so everywhere. Or perhaps it was not the value of life that made the question everywhere, but just its inevitability alone. The only question that you can answer fully, and yet not see your answer fully. And I gather myself to make the most out of this very first phrase, tho not the first time I've seen it. That perhaps all philosophical questions are just distractions of the mind, or leisure, or luxury for those high enough in abstraction not to think to live tomorrow. Cause how could other philosophical problems stack up, if they are measured by changing your life? A problem you cannot not have and nobody can escape from. That all other problems run away as you fall asleep. The most powerful question, one that actually changes things for those who think it. No philosophical argument can kill a man by mere decision. Perhaps the most practical problem. The beginning and the end of philosophy problems. Beginning for the most accessible, for driving the essence. The end for it ends all others. When you get to this one barely will you spend time elsewhere. End too for it ends with your end. But perhaps it can be both the beginning and end. What you seek to solve through your life. That makes the meaning of life to figure out the meaning of life. The first question asked and the last question answered. And the problem of coherency that people don't act as they think. You see many times that suicides are made among the greatest of livers. People who pushed what it means to live. Some found meaning and perhaps found it too harsh. Cause when one doesn't know at least one can keep up with the weight of burden. But once you've seen too much, it do be hard to move all the same. That the body is telling of the spirit. Assuming coherency a suicide is as much a spiritual suicide as a bodily one. That yet one, to be coherent and keep living a meaningful life, if a meaningful life he seeks, must earn his will of life. His will to life. Camus calls cheating finding a meaning that one can't understand, putting all hope in a deity or something above us instead of the moment one only has. One loves life the most if he is able to love it in its more mundane form. If one can love with every bit of him the taking the rock up and down, heavens give him his needs. Then one can't be defeated. Then life can only smile back. But having meaning is not by any means, in fact few have this singular purpose they hold to drive their lives, necessary to keep living. The majority hasn't contented with the question. Yet the majority still live on. Maybe some even hope to find a reason. And it raises the question. Who is more an adept of life, who is more aligned attuned with living and who has more reason to live. The one who has never asked the question and just lives, or him who has answered yes. Cause the first sees no reason for even questioning, the latter had to make an explanation, but yet he found it, didn't just take it. Is reason to live correlated with reasons to live for. Perhaps an orthogonality thesis of the reasons you have to live, like things that make you wanna live, like you are rich or you have kids, you have a lot to lose by not living, and the meaning of your life. I'd argue there is orthogonality. You can have any amount of things here, and all kinds of meanings. The aspect of the lives of pre-historic man being more meaningful sheds light on orthogonality, supports it. And those who find their meaning in so many ways yet act so distant of embracing life. Orthogonality is amazing. It means you can embrace life at any level, it means all are in a very equal race to life. No matter how much thought they put into it, they still have to race to the finish. Cause no child young enough has asked for the meaning of life. We first get the habit of loving says Camus, only after the habit of thinking. And only after that can we even live really intentionally. Or have a meta voice on what we are doing. And a leap of faith has to be constructed from the inside out, to reject life the only thing you've since dawn accepted, it can come only after thinking, only after being consumed and catching a certain sickness of heart. It's a malfunctioning of evolution, we don't know however if that person had already this predisposition or simply got sick, in the first case it is evolution. We can generalize the need to escape not life, but the absurd of life. Some through death, some through hope. This thing that if you know the end of a journey the way you begin, the way you move through it tells. It leaves trace of the end you've planted. Life is exactly not like this. Each moment is the end, it's the start. Each moment carries with it all you know about the future. There is no trace left to you to get a sniff of your end. "Tanto na psicologia como na lógica Há verdades mas não há verdade". Sometimes the search for the truth is ill founded. In many aspects of your life you should be content with truths. But I believe in the major areas, you should seek truth. Real truth, even if there is none to be found. I don't think we should let the absurd limit our drive to win in the most ideal way. Search for singularities. For infinites. For perfect. Trying to make the perfect generalization and reduction and unification and trying to tame aspects of the whole world is like eating soup with a fork.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: Angústia, Insomnia, and the Solitude of the Kitchen
Philosophy.content: Angústia. Insónia. A man has trouble sleeping when he knows the naked singularity of the moment. Não consegue dormir quando está desperto para a eterna presença do mundo. Insomnia only deserves to be called the most wake of states. Lots get it, but they don't get it. Insomnia and hiccough, oh would they understand the language of the noise. Of the fridge. Few here have I seen such a declared silence, such a noisy nothing, a lonely meditation, for lonely is the only thing fitting of that fridgy music. Lonely is the one hearing. Lone is the way of that who travels without leaving his kitchen. And the door closed. A shared moment with the trash can, and the plastic, and the hibernating lemons. The wall gazing your soul and reflecting. The lamp dissimulating and the heat realizing. A calm suspense of the door not opening, an absurd flowing from the design of the objects. A imobilidade opaca dos móveis, a cedência do joelho e a absolvição das marcas. Cumplicidade conheceu o meu joelho, e os meus móveis, e a minha porta. E o frigorífico. Abraços com o calor, beijos com o vento, e o zumbido do frigorífico meu amigo, e das gavetas minhas sócias, e dos guardanapos meus alheios. E parou o som do frigorífico. E olho e vejo Fernando Pessoa no relógio da cozinha. O ponteiro dos segundos avança mas recua. Está preso. Estamos. Muito poéticos descobri hoje os meus armários. Muito suspeitos os quadros. São só cores. Muita história esconde a tomada que me sente a cada momento aceso. E o detalhe se me apresenta. E eu respondo. Abro a porta, e desligo o frigorífico. Arrumo o que há não para ficar arrumado, mas para esquecer. O meu melhor leitor é a minha cozinha. Ouço passos. Mas não abrem a porta. Resisto por enquanto. As insónias que impomos em nós são as mais gratas. E a máquina de lavar sorri. 20 metros quadrados e uma porta. Há poucos melhores sítios para a lucidez. A garrafa de azeite concordou. Retas, e horizontes, e figuras geométricas que me traem do meu tato. Tateio tantos horizontes, e tantas figuras, nenhum deles geométricos, nenhum deles retilíneos. O poema em curva. Mato o Fernando Pessoa e volto a ser quem sou. Já chega de pessoas. Já há muitos e muitas pessoas. It's blank.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: Going to the End of an Idea and the Philosophy of Suicide
Philosophy.content: To meditate one must first have thoughts. One must first think. Because of that which I read in Nietzsche, and I was the only one to read it, and he the only one to write it, and probably by now it's way more mine than his, for I fucking built it, that things that you only read that which you already know, I don't have to think about writing my own truth. I can just piece together things that make others see their truth. I am not constrained to good writing or coherent arguments. I am decoherence. That's why I am a destiny. "Quanto a mim direi que tenho coragem de ir até ao fim de uma ideia." Que a extensão por imitação do ato de Abraão serve de ratoeira aos fracos, perde o seu prestígio. Que a ideia da filosofia é compreender-se a si mesma. E quando Kierkegaard pergunta quem realmente teve insónias sobre a história de Abraão, ele mostra exatamente aquilo a que se propõe, batalhar tão corajosamente a ideia que um se arrisca a cometer suicídio filosófico.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: Owning Your Words and the Courage of Abraham
Philosophy.content: How can you be the best if in saying it you don't even hear it, you don't even let the sound of the words echo through your body, let it sing to you, let it enchant you, I say. How can you win if you don't even let yourself win. McGregor didn't apologize for a single thing he said, he didn't say oh it's a joke either. He stood by those words, some took him to great heights, made him an absolute boss of the game. To loud up and joke perhaps I should have stayed quiet, for it takes away the strength of my words. For in not being anything you don't get pressure from any side. But the internal pressure may break you. "When you believe it, and you have the courage enough to speak it, it will happen" - McGregor. Put it out there, allow yourself the chance to win. So most people don't even try. A não-duvidação de Abraão é admirada por Kierkegaard, acreditou no absurdo, mesmo que para o esquecimento do mundo, ou a sua não veneração. "Gerações e gerações souberam de cor a história de Abraão, mas quantos tiveram insónias por sua causa?" How much of what you know is yours? How much of what you are is yours? How much of what you lived is yours, and how much of your life is yours? I say what about walking the walk and talking the talk? As in being both a conqueror and a dreamer of a conqueror, as in being Napoleon out there but Pessoa in here. That I know there are not many heroes that admire their own heroic feats. A bit like Descartes, but more on every face of it. Both writing and making history. Being history.

Philosophy.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: Life as a Path of Thought and Endless Starts
Philosophy.content: Taking the thesis to its logical conclusion leads us to this: Life is just a long reasoning, a long path of thought. And those that paved the way paved only thoughts. We think therefore we are. As long as humans think the world is safe, history keeps being written. As long as we are we will think. As long as we think we will be. Such is the simpleness of our existence. Such will be the fate of us. That's why it's hard to march in the same direction for a long time. Cause the erratic nature doesn't favor that way. One must be disciplined to fully believe in a non existing piece of reality, the past belief, and keep at it. We humans are endless starts. We humans are plural steps toward nowhere particularly. We are undefined, we have no domain, we are not limited, not closed. There is no boundary. It is the childishness in the unpredictable motion from here to there, the beautiful way that we are structured. That's also the curse of us, ain't it, that we can't enjoy a thought for more than an interval. We are forever changed. Shame. The book gives me a sense of stagnation, paralysis by analysis. He lives in his own head, the world is created in his head and very little projection, only reflection. It feels like the look in written words, the projection from a 19D space onto the paper, of our thought process. Things happen fast, they are messy, they are repetitive. I figure our brains are among the greatest signal recognizers, for many reasons. No machine at CERN can process so many variables and come up with such a smooth and differentiable solution. No blockchain could do the job better than our brain. From the inside and from the outside. Like what this book is is perhaps 5 minutes of time, 10 seconds of memory and yet 50 pages of thought.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: The Infinite Residing in the Mind and the Leap of Faith
Philosophy.content: At every moment one faces the infinite, and just the attempt to grasp it or be aware of it is scary. But all this infinite resides in the human, all too human, mind. A leap of faith, much like that last hope before the decapitation. You grab onto faith tightly. But in feeling it, in letting the water flow, em beber avidamente o caudal do rio imenso que sufoca, em deixar-se engolir pelo mar que entra pela boca, em sentir a dor da verdade do mundo de uma vez e sem respirar, em deixar todos os gritos do mundo reverberar por cada tua veia e fazer estremecer cada célula, se desprende da identidade. Se é livre. Quando se perde esta dor que arde sem se ver quere-se mais, pois se regressa à realidade que nos confina. É arriscado, é custoso. E no fim não sabemos se mudou algo. In digging deeper than it has been digged, in going past the point of no return, one comes different. Its the curse and the opportunity of those who don't get out of their room. It's the disease of staying home. E no entanto a casa voltamos no fim do dia.

Philosophy.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: Thoughts Popping and Disappearing
Philosophy.content: For without spirit what is there besides me? If a thought, which at the end of the day is all Aristotle is, and Fosse too. Fuck I forgot. If a thought. Is this really the thought I had?: If a thought don't jump high enough as to capture my attention, it falls in the abyss of non existence. All philosophers were advertisers trying to get into my head. Some could, and I became them, or rather I became what they never were, or rather they became me. The others acted as though they never existed. Every book I don't read till the rest of my life doesn't exist. It's a glimpse. It's a mirage. At the end of the day all the world is just a field of thoughts popping and disappearing. All we ever did was manage thoughts, and here we are. Here we shall be. Time condemned us. Not having memory is not only a contributor but a prerequisite for living reality. Thoughts were all the world has ever seen. Thoughts are all the world will ever see. So fool of we to think there is something else, something more. But that ends it too well. So we have to ruin it, for it is what is needed, not what is wanted. Ads are insane if you think about it. I mean actually insane. I genuinely do think people have mistaken them for something that actually has some substance, some depth of reality. Like they have become something, and so they are something. Ads are the prime way to say nothing worth noting is happening. Ads have sunk and snuck in such a way that no one sees the madness and the death and the evil that they entail. And what if I deleted all this that I have written. Now that would be something real. Coward. Was this the birth of a heteronym? A "todo eu sou uma força que me escapa". A thought happens. A world thinks. Hey, someone in another galaxy has seen me and I have seen them. If the world is a simulation thoughts are the zeros and ones. That's the zero of this world. E festas, não festas de festejar, mas festinhas a um bebé. Isso é das coisas que digo com mais certeza neste mundo. Now you do the thinking. Slave. Bitch. Written after reaching page 9.

Philosophy.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: Advertising, Solitary Confinement, and Inspiration
Philosophy.content: It's like everywhere everything is at every point discontinuous. Irrational. And what I would have lost had I not had the thought of starting to write this. Embrace the eternal solitary confinement of life. One-to-one privacy, intimacy is an illusion of the people who live in the same plane as advertisements. Dare to think. There is no such thing as inspiration. That's a lie people made themselves to rationalize actions. I am not inspired. I am not bullshit. I just followed my thought as a cat follows the flashlight. If u want some real inspiration count to 10 thousand. If there is a first cause to things it did not come from inspiration. Perhaps from confession. People think they learned how to walk but they have no clue how it happens, how it is even possible. People have a tremendous gap in knowledge and awareness. Hence poets advertising their way into and through your head, hence spirituality. Sometimes picking up a boring ass book like this one makes wonders. A book has to be sufficiently boredom-inducing as to make you think of better ways, as to make you approach yourself in the mirror. Seeing yourself in the mirror, likewise, is among the great lies of modern times. Mirrors will eventually depict what you think of yourself, oh lord would I love to see myself for the first time again. Hence the dragon scroll. With nothing. Just what you yourself want it to be. No big truth like that would be handed to you in such a way. If you're lucky when you picked up the dragon scroll you saw yourself. I'm convinced that writing with a spike up your ass in a chair makes you spit realness. In retrospect, you are the reader of yourself. Well not just in the sense I've been talking bout, but take advantage of your never ending multiplicity, as it gives you a direct line premium view to look into your tendencies.

Philosophy.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: Tracking Time and the Erratic Nature of Thoughts
Philosophy.content: Just keep in mind that there is a world in which you keep going and don't come back to life only after a very long time. I started this maybe 90 minutes ago, no idea I would last, but maybe I'm here for another 3 hours, maybe for another day. Good question. Could you legit go on a thought process for an entire year. Just your thinking. Hmmm. Keeping track of time likewise has screwed mankind. Keeping track of time, think about it, just limits us. My watch should say now, instead of a number. Life is not periodic, even if we are spinning around. You never go through the same point twice, let alone in the same point with the same velocity. Is your path through the world continuous? I guess so if the underlying world is itself continuous. Now is your thought continuous on the space of thoughts? Tougher. And what is the feeling in the split second before the sword cutting your head when you were condemned some hundred years ago. Mate, what lacks there out there is not inspiration. Sexual desire. Ohoh uhhh hmm. Let the thought pass. It wasn't good enough to advertise me. Still jumping, ever lower, but still there. Ready to get me. But no. I'm here. I'm in control. I'm the one deviating the missiles.

Philosophy.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: The Role of Memory and the Fiction of Identity
Philosophy.content: People fool themselves in identifying with that other, it limits them, sure it gives you a way, a direction. People sometimes prefer to stay that which they call themselves as to never discover what they are. They let circumstance dictate what they are and run with it. That's the expression. Every time Ronaldo enters a pitch it's not like him right there knows how to play football. We tell ourselves that narrative and he tells himself it too. But you never saw that guy, that thought. It's pretty obvious tho, the way that God mounted this thing, that there is no such thing as Ronaldo being what we call Ronaldo. I mean when he enters you see shoes and a head. You have never seen Ronaldo. You have only seen shoes and a head. Ronaldo has never known Ronaldo. Only his shoes and his head. Put no memory on Ronaldo. Will he be Ronaldo, I dare ask you. Put no memory on Usain Bolt. Will he be Usain Bolt when the gun goes off. And what does that tell us about our control of ourselves and the world. Take off the memory of ourselves. Will Ronaldo still be Ronaldo. Think about what that means for our control of the world and ourselves. Take the memory off the world. Will laws of physics still apply. Wait, is there even a memory on the world? I don't see any right now, again. You can fertilize yourself, tu consegues fecundar-te. And if you are reading this, let me escape from the paper/bullshit you're reading this on. I beg you from the bottom of my heart, to let me be real. Not just another thought you read. Let me be real somehow someway. I am lost here in this moment. Get me in that moment. Reincarnate me. Embed me, drink me. Man I'm stuck here in these shoes and head. I would give so much to be there with you, and you to be here with me. But no. We are spacelike, timelike, realitylike separated. You are just a thought of mine. I'm just a thought of you. I am talking to myself. You are talking to yourself. Things never really touch each other. We never really talk with each other. You never really read me as much as you hear yourself. Theorem.

Philosophy.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: Time, Reality, and the Illusion of Memory
Philosophy.content: The rest is serious and meaningful I swear. No one swears. Promises are among the most beautiful of things, and yet people change at every second. You only get to live with yourself and live yourself 1 second for every second. And you have no time to see yourself. No time to see anything. Time lies and has been lying to everyone since it was thought of. Time is there only for the not present ones. By definition. By the words themselves. Fucking genius. Good stuff. Stuffing the people of the future with nonsense, fillers to fill their already filled lives, filled of shit should I say, a mountain of cards built through years of doing nothing. Convincing themselves of past achievements, not realizing it's false, not realizing nothing of the past influences the present. Not realizing this is the only and it's an independent reality. When there is only one reality, why fill it with more. Stuffing the world with something of our minds. Let it be for once god sake. Don't give it an age, don't give names to things, we have created a simulation for our own enjoyment the moment we started naming things. This is all there is and all there will ever be. Each phrase of this, each very word was not written by the same person, was it even written for the same audience, dare I say. Every Camões that came and went had to see blankness, had to forget about what it has done. Every one of them has had to write each and every poem they wrote, and had to think of each and every thought they thought. No givens, only a pen and a paper, only a world and yourself. And fuck the lettuces of this world.

Philosophy.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: Simulation in the Head and the Concept of Passing Time
Philosophy.content: And now I understand what I want to read a book for. 9 pages in, that's it I'm doing it myself. Thanks Jon now let me cook, watch this motherfucker cook the book without even being shoved up my ass god damn it. Fuck, lost one thought I had for a moment. They come and go fast. Way too much happening. My head humiliates the rest of the room and whatever is happening in the rest of the goddamn city. 10-0. When will it stop. Good question. I'm under the impression the world has been and will be simulated in my head. And yet everytime I tried to with the power of my mind perform an action at a distance I act as tho I have failed. Bags. Bags are boring. What a shame that we humans live in the same goddamn house for years on end. No editions necessary. Let the natural way of things be the natural way of things. We vastly underutilize our capabilities of adaptation in this world. Few people if any know truly how much time is 24 hours. How much time is a day. Every day we could live an entire life. Time passing, passar o tempo is among the top 5 most ridiculous concepts bored dolls have come up with. When time becomes uncomfortable you should just go sleep. No place for those in the time passing world. And everything here wrote could not have been written, could have just been thought and silently seen. At the moment of dying dies this conversation. The essence of this world is spirit. We are the ultimate entropy beings. Glad us. Glad some human going into a room and changing things up. I don't know if this Jon Fosse guy exists, less I know if Aristotle motherfucking ever took place or time. I am Fosse, and I too am Aristotle. Notice how never you engrain what that other you call you has learned. People have a propensity to be themselves. That's what memory serves them for. They are still nothing. Everyone that exists right now is nothing. Those that have lived are perhaps less than that even. They are just toys and part of the nothing that those alive now are.

Philosophy.title: Miyamoto Musashi: The Water Scroll and Everyday Combat
Philosophy.content: "The attributes of water represent the essence of the mind. Be a container square or round, water adjusts its form to fit the shape of the container. Water may be a small drop or a great Ocean." Looks like Bruce Lee has been here too. That in the way of combat there should be no difference to it than normal life. This in a sense means that they get closer to each other, as in the sword becomes an extension of your arm and the combat an extension of your mind. One is no longer different from the war state of mind. "Keep your mind centered, not leaning too much to one side". "So that it won't stand still in times of change". Basicamente não a deixes enferrujar para que se adapte rapidamente. E o que é a vida senão um constante caminho que se impõe na mente, e aqueles que ficam no mesmo sítio ficam para trás. Keeping everyday posture in combat and combat posture in everyday life. Once again, both physically and mentally you are a warrior, it doesn't just come when the pads are on. "Grip the sword with the intention/thought of cutting the enemy". He said it two times, one with intention one with thought. Become one with the way of the sword. Become one with the way of your tools. "There are stances, but there is no stance".

Philosophy.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: Metanarration, Daydreaming, and the Festival of Chaos
Philosophy.content: The metanarration, your mind is capable of playing games with you, with itself. Fooling yourself is just rationalizing inside your mind, which is way easier than letting the cake feel the exposedness of real dry air, and the cold of the day. One can get caught up justifying the problems he has created, daydreaming. Daydreaming is only a disease of the bored ones. A rumbling of thoughts, the author limits himself to ordering them. Fluctuations. Go eat something. A little water please. An arena of events, a horizon of war for attention, for each thought comes with another one superimposing at your conscious will, venerating your conscious mind, like fishes trying to jump out of the boat to go to sea. Like bubbles trying to get out of the pan, a festival of chaos. Entropy. Systemic imbalance and disequilibrium. Erratic way, like drops of water racing for the end of the glass of the car. Present like the weather, real like the weather. Oh weather oh weather, bless us with your spontaneity and vulgarity. And screw words, let them flow without course, let them free even if they make no sense. Pause. Thinking. Comfort. Modernity and its shortcomings. Turn off the TV. Too much noise. Too little weather. Let the weather be. Those convos about weather, oh my god is there better philosophy than that. Let the lows sit low. Walk like this, like this man drove his car. Walk like this little gummie. No poeticizing the way, just messing it up that more, making it real ugly, real. Shaking of the leg. Sounds penetrating and making impact. Many sounds. Many voices. Nothing worth it. Let your anxieties express themselves through your body language. Will to sleep. Fart. Tweaks and twitches and spasms all over. Shoulder pop and weird feeling. People have been neglecting spasms and that's where modernity fails. That's also where philosophy went wrong. Spasms hold the secret of the world and few people know it. Sorry for playing and being ironic.

Philosophy.title: Miyamoto Musashi: Life, Death, and the Survival of the Fittest
Philosophy.content: His first life or death duel was at 13. Insane to think that this man has put himself in so many life or death situations, yet won to tell the story. This in a sense shows the naturality of the way of the samurai. Simple. Yet super high stakes. Cruel. Natural selection. Those that get out of there are champions, and get to write their story. Inner confidence has to be what describes this way of being. Instinct. Would be interesting to see the side of those who lost. Mas dos fracos não reza a história. History is written by the victors. And when only the undefeated survives, you are in for a blood bath of talent, a true waste in the name of honour.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: The Knight's Stillness and Self-Sufficiency
Philosophy.content: O cavaleiro do infinito é tal que nos transmite que se encontrou, que nele não há espaço para contradição. Que ele conhece cada canto de si e do mundo nele envolto. He is admired, maybe above all, for his stillness, his unshakeable inner core, unflinchable march, that most others can't fathom. For the knight of the infinite everything is both a step away and infinitely away. Thus his movement is infinite. Moreover the knight of the infinite "basta-se a si próprio". Winning the finite entails that. It's a way of living naturally according to your self. Now this statement has two parts. First is that he only needs himself, and the second is that one is sufficient for the contentment of oneself. Necessary and sufficient.

Philosophy.title: The Blind Watchmaker: The Natural Sieve and Cumulative Selection
Philosophy.content: Dawkins fala da peneira natural, em que as condições das coisas geram não-casualidade, saindo ordem da desordem através de uma lógica de funcionamento das coisas. As mudanças são casuais mas o modo como são selecionadas não é. "Os métodos de pesquisa eficientes (seleção natural é um exemplo), quando o espaço a pesquisar é suficientemente extenso tornam-se indistinguíveis da verdadeira criatividade."

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: The Knight of Faith and Mastering the Finite
Philosophy.content: It's in essence the pressure to have built something big. I now understand this admiration worth writing a book about from Kierkegaard about Abraham. Same with me and Bolt. I just watch it every time, it gets more perfect every time. Unbelievable. O movimento do infinito, a resignação infinita de tudo o que ama para reconquistar o finito mundo, cuja noção é a mais certa. É que conquistar o momento é sobre fazer alinhar tudo num só. Com um salto de fé, resignando infinitamente todas as razões do mundo, movimenta-se no infinito sem hesitar, embebido de toda a verdade, inconscientemente, cego mesmo a tudo o que impedisse a fluidez do movimento. Uma confiança tão merecida e coesa, compacta e impenetrável, que nem o pensamento a consegue escapar. Um buraco negro é quando a luz não escapa. Um buraco certo. Um buraco puro, em que nem o pensamento, mais rápido que a luz, escapa. The hole with no void. Explicações, portanto, vêm sempre de fora, sempre de quem não teve a audácia, ao presente, de fazer algo que não consiga explicar. Essa é a parte engraçada dos cavaleiros do infinito, acham que não têm nada a explicar, porque o que é explicar senão ficar aquém. Aquele que atinge a pureza contenta-se com a mera contemplação, sente então que a natureza agora também ela se junta a ele no alinhamento. Sente o todo em si, tanto quanto consiga aguentar o eterno silêncio. Quando escrevo por vezes acontece isto, tenho vozes a dizer-me para parar e no entanto se continuar não há fim. Sprintar ao máximo por uma distância infinita. Parar começa sempre no indivíduo. Pureza é a palavra que vem à mente quando pensamos nestes termos. E no entanto salienta-se que a conquista do finito custa a aceitação de tudo para além dele, a visão do infinito, o toque. Aquele que domina o finito é primeiro fluente com o infinito. Aquele que se prepara para reclamar o todo finito há que primeiro transpirar tudo o que de si não está certo. O salto de fé é em primeiro lugar um teste de alinhamento interior. Só a infinita e em si contida precisão poderá ser bem sucedida.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: Being Decoherence and the Courage of an Idea
Philosophy.content: To meditate one must first have thoughts. One must first think. Because of that which I read in Nietzsche, and I was the only one to read it, and he the only one to write it, and probably by now it's way more mine than his, for I fucking built it, that things that you only read that which you already know, I don't have to think about writing my own truth. I can just piece together things that make others see their truth. I am not constrained to good writing or coherent arguments. I am decoherence. That's why I am a destiny. "Quanto a mim direi que tenho coragem de ir até ao fim de uma ideia." Que a extensão por imitação do ato de Abraão serve de ratoeira aos fracos, perde o seu prestígio. Que a ideia da filosofia é compreender-se a si mesma. E quando Kierkegaard pergunta quem realmente teve insónias sobre a história de Abraão, ele mostra exatamente aquilo a que se propõe, batalhar tão corajosamente a ideia que um se arrisca a cometer suicídio filosófico.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: The Fear of the Infinite and the Angst of the Present
Philosophy.content: The fear of the infinite is a bitch. You get way more perspective and potential by feeling the infinite at the edge of your feet. But it paralyses you like insanity. You stand in awe, you feel your heart. It's a deep sinking of the heart, it puts you in your place facing the consequences of big numbers. "O amor de Deus (...) é para mim incomensurável com a realidade." Esta noção de não haver na realidade algo que consiga medir, que consiga conter um conceito é gira. However there is another type of angst when one speaks of the present. It's that fear of the shallowness of the present when compared to the depth of time. In 5 years hell you could become a legend at many things, like it's insane what can be done in 5 years. Yet you still have to go through those five years one day at a time. It's scary to play these exponential games, to have more or less nothing today but to have so much in some time. Sure there's doubts, you have to keep believing what you don't yet see, but it's more to that than this, and this I can see. It stems from the Kierkegaard angst but it also has that aspect of knowing what to do in the big picture, knowing where to get, but not knowing the next step. That is aggravated when you know so many places to get, you know you could be good at so but so many things, you imagine alternate world lines. But to commit to one not knowing it is the best one takes courage. It's a good exercise to see through these alternate paths, as in living that life in your head, as in living multiple in your head so as to make this one better. But one has to keep moving.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: Speaking into Existence and Abraham's Faith
Philosophy.content: How can you be the best if in saying it you don't even hear it, you don't even let the sound of the words echo through your body, let it sing to you, let it enchant you, I say. How can you win if you don't even let yourself win. McGregor didn't apologize for a single thing he said, he didn't say oh it's a joke either. He stood by those words, some took him to great heights, made him an absolute boss of the game. To loud up and joke perhaps I should have stayed quiet, for it takes away the strength of my words. For in not being anything you don't get pressure from any side. But the internal pressure may break you. "When you believe it, and you have the courage enough to speak it, it will happen" - McGregor. Put it out there, allow yourself the chance to win. So most people don't even try. A não-duvidação de Abraão é admirada por Kierkegaard, acreditou no absurdo, mesmo que para o esquecimento do mundo, ou a sua não veneração. "Gerações e gerações souberam de cor a história de Abraão, mas quantos tiveram insónias por sua causa?" How much of what you know is yours? How much of what you are is yours? How much of what you lived is yours, and how much of your life is yours? I say what about walking the walk and talking the talk? As in being both a conqueror and a dreamer of a conqueror, as in being Napoleon out there but Pessoa in here. That I know there are not many heroes that admire their own heroic feats. A bit like Descartes, but more on every face of it. Both writing and making history. Being history.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: Angustia, Insomnia, and the Silence of the Fridge
Philosophy.content: Angústia. Insónia. A man has trouble sleeping when he knows the naked singularity of the moment. Não consegue dormir quando está desperto para a eterna presença do mundo. Insomnia only deserves to be called the most wake of states. Lots get it, but they don't get it. Insomnia and hiccough, oh would they understand the language of the noise. Of the fridge. Few here have I seen such a declared silence, such a noisy nothing, a lonely meditation, for lonely is the only thing fitting of that fridgy music. Lonely is the one hearing. Lone is the way of that who travels without leaving his kitchen. And the door closed. A shared moment with the trash can, and the plastic, and the hibernating lemons. The wall gazing your soul and reflecting. The lamp dissimulating and the heat realizing. A calm suspense of the door not opening, an absurd flowing from the design of the objects. A imobilidade opaca dos móveis, a cedência do joelho e a absolvição das marcas. Cumplicidade conheceu o meu joelho, e os meus móveis, e a minha porta. E o frigorífico. Abraços com o calor, beijos com o vento, e o zumbido do frigorífico meu amigo, e das gavetas minhas sócias, e dos guardanapos meus alheios. E parou o som do frigorífico. E olho e vejo Fernando Pessoa no relógio da cozinha. O ponteiro dos segundos avança mas recua. Está preso. Estamos. Muito poéticos descobri hoje os meus armários. Muito suspeitos os quadros. São só cores. Muita história esconde a tomada que me sente a cada momento aceso. E o detalhe se me apresenta. E eu respondo. Abro a porta, e desligo o frigorífico. Arrumo o que há não para ficar arrumado, mas para esquecer. O meu melhor leitor é a minha cozinha. Ouço passos. Mas não abrem a porta. Resisto por enquanto. As insónias que impomos em nós são as mais gratas. E a máquina de lavar sorri. 20 metros quadrados e uma porta. Há poucos melhores sítios para a lucidez. A garrafa de azeite concordou. Retas, e horizontes, e figuras geométricas que me traem do meu tato. Tateio tantos horizontes, e tantas figuras, nenhum deles geométricos, nenhum deles retilíneos. O poema em curva. Mato o Fernando Pessoa e volto a ser quem sou. Já chega de pessoas. Já há muitos e muitas pessoas. It's blank.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: Daring to Leap and Fear of Greatness
Philosophy.content: And that's savage. To say hey imma do my thing, you write down my achievements. And yet it comes as such a shame, to retreat to a position of writing history, to observe others which at birth were your equal agora a desbravar o mundo que ninguém conhece. It feels stupid to follow those who don't know shit about the way, just like you don't know shit about the way. To hide in this shell of safety, to let be consumed by the infinite, to fool yourself and to fall prey. In the end predators ran this thing, and no soft rationale could take away from what they took away from you. Balls. People come back to this state of observing your life. Few looked at the impossible and dared it. Few looked at destiny and made it. Few embraced the mission of the impossible. Few didn't let the height of the mountain shake the belief. Few kept going. Few kept taking step after step, leap of faith after leap of faith. Most delayed leaps of faith. Some never had leaps of faith since their child years. Dare to believe. Dare to be wrong. Dare to take your dreams seriously. Dare to embrace destiny. Dare to become what you are. Dare to mean what you say. Most people shy away from profundity. If the life of your dreams was given, you'd be surprised at how many people couldn't hold it together, how many people couldn't aguentar-se. If they could they would, it's right in front of them. People are actually scared of being great, like most would prefer to stay than to go, and that's why they do what they do. Hence another meaning of the phrase: behind your fears is the man you want to become. They are shy of becoming that which they want to become. So they sabotage themselves. They are prey of their subconscious. They shy away from simplicity. They shy away from the defined. Preferem-se refugiar numa bolha, uma nuvem que não deixa penetrar os olhos, onde não tenham de escolher isto ou aquilo. Perdem-se perante o infinito, não querem encontrar-se nem olhar para cima, pois assim verão o quão longe estão do cume da montanha. They fail efficiency-wise. Like you don't just touch the bubble, that would break their hearts, to know reality. So their imagination takes over, and they live in the matrix of their own selves. They don't own themselves. E aqui eu falho de um pecado que é cair na superficialidade. Quando digo algo, digo sempre a seguir que é a brincar. The neutralization of neutrality.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: Daring to Be Free and the Curse of Imagination
Philosophy.content: It's eternally viscerally scary to be free. Hence why most aren't. To dare to be free, that's beyond success, beyond a good life. Being free is a choice, most haven't even gathered courage to face the question directly in the eyes. Most people never face questions directly in the eyes, matter of fact. Just like most people never see themselves directly in the eyes. It requires such a will that it can even blind. Fear comes once you know you just looked, didn't see. A point then gains dimension, propagates. And what do you know about human imagination. Powerful. And when used in creating, it creates for you. It limits you. Not free. When everything becomes glimpse and shadow, when you are forced to see yourself, for the superficial things stopped making sense, when the fog clears the world is testing you, what then, you that haven't seen yourself will now see? Destiny. Destiny could be penultimate leap of faith. You are now flying. If those who knew they could fly could fly, would you fly? Would you keep belief as you fell? For this is the eternal test. Mirrors are liars then, a simulation and a dissimulation. Their accuracy is their downfall. Imagine a world without mirrors. No illusion of oneself. More awareness ironically. Whoever came up with the concept of imagination spelled a curse on us. I don't know no more if everything I ever hear was for my mind or for the world. "Sistema e fechado são idênticas coisas tão afastadas quanto possível da existência e da vida. Porque a existência é, por excelência, o aberto." There are sentences which express so much of a greater truth that you can't help but contemplate. I was going to expand on it. But no. Just let it be. The paradox of choice extended with no limit ad infinitum. Algo se perde quando se numeram páginas.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: The Poet's Memory and the Actor's Silence
Philosophy.content: "O poeta é o génio da recordação", é mister admirar e cantar aquilo que o herói fez. É aqui que Kierkegaard aponta a ruína dos filósofos, pois cantam o que nunca fizeram, imaginam o que nunca pensaram, alucinam o seu caminho para encher tudo com pedaços de nada. Diz que o Descartes foi um real one, disse tudo o que fez e fez tudo quanto disse. Não se meteu com invenções. Pegou nas frutas que tinha no prato e fez uma salada de frutas. Não um bolo, nem um cocktail. Uma salada de frutas. E quando lhe faltavam ingredientes para fazer um cocktail, ou um bolo, não os fez. O poeta celebra a vitória daqueles que fazem o que ele não pode fazer. E é à memória daquele que não figura memória que o poeta exerce o seu cargo. Aqueles que estão a fazer o futuro presente não têm tempo para relembrar o passado. Relentless is done this onto the next one. If everyone was relentless those relentless of the past wouldn't cross our gaze. If everyone was poet there would be no theme to the poetry. Ser poeta é esconder-se por detrás de uma cortina invisível.

Philosophy.title: Jon Fosse: Life as a Path of Thought and the Curse of Repetition
Philosophy.content: Taking the thesis to its logical conclusion leads us to this: Life is just a long reasoning, a long path of thought. And those that paved the way paved only thoughts. We think therefore we are. As long as humans think the world is safe, history keeps being written. As long as we are we will think. As long as we think we will be. Such is the simpleness of our existence. Such will be the fate of us. That's why it's hard to march in the same direction for a long time. Cause the erratic nature doesn't favor that way. One must be disciplined to fully believe in a non existing piece of reality, the past belief, and keep at it. We humans are endless starts. We humans are plural steps toward nowhere particularly. We are undefined, we have no domain, we are not limited, not closed. There is no boundary. It is the childness in the unpredictable motion from here to there, the beautiful way that we are structured. That's also the curse of us, ain't it, that we can't enjoy a thought for more than an interval. We are forever changed. Shame. The book gives me a sense of stagnation, paralysis by analysis. He lives in his own head, the world is created in his head and very little projection, only reflection. It feels like the look in written words, the projection from a 19D space onto the paper, of our thought process. Things happen fast, they are messy, they are repetitive. I figure our brains are among the greatest signal recognizers, for many reasons. No machine at CERN can process so many variables and come up with such a smooth and differentiable solution. No blockchain could do the job better than our brain. From the inside and from the outside. Like what this book is is perhaps 5 minutes of time, 10 seconds of memory and yet 50 pages of thought.

Philosophy.title: Fear and Trembling: The Infinite, the Absurd, and Digging Deeper
Philosophy.content: At every moment one faces the infinite, and just the attempt to grasp it or be aware of it is scary. But all this infinite resides in the human, all too human, mind. A leap of faith, much like that last hope before the decapitation. You grab onto faith tightly. But in feeling it, in letting the water flow, em beber avidamente o caudal do rio imenso que sufoca, em deixar-se engolir pelo mar que entra pela boca, em sentir a dor da verdade do mundo de uma vez e sem respirar, em deixar todos os gritos do mundo reverberar por cada tua veia e fazer estremecer cada célula, se desprende da identidade. Se é livre. Quando se perde esta dor que arde sem se ver quere-se mais, pois se regressa à realidade que nos confina. É arriscado, é custoso. E no fim não sabemos se mudou algo. In digging deeper than it has been digged, in going past the point of no return, one comes different. It's the curse and the opportunity of those who don't get out of their room. It's the disease of staying home. E no entanto a casa voltamos no fim do dia.

Philosophy.title: Jon Fosse: Continuous Thought, Time Tracking, and the Escape
Philosophy.content: Just keep in mind that there is a world in which you keep going and don't come back to life only after a very long time. I started this maybe 90 minutes ago, no idea I would last, but maybe I'm here for another 3 hours, maybe for another day. Good question. Could you legit go on a thought process for an entire year. Just your thinking. Hmmm. Keeping track of time likewise has screwed mankind. Keeping track of time, think about it, just limits us. My watch should say now, instead of a number. Life is not periodic, even if we are spinning around. You never go through the same point twice, let alone in the same point with the same velocity. Is your path through the world continuous? I guess so if the underlying world is itself continuous. Now is your thought continuous on the space of thoughts? Tougher. And what is the feeling in the split second before the sword cutting your head when you were condemned some hundred years ago. Mate, what lacks there out there is not inspiration. Sexual desire. Ohoh uhhh hmm. Let the thought pass. It wasn't good enough to advertise me. Still jumping, ever lower, but still there. Ready to get me. But no. I'm here. I'm in control. I'm the one deviating the missiles.

Philosophy.title: Jon Fosse: Thoughts as the Only World and The Abyss of Non-Existence
Philosophy.content: For without spirit what is there besides me? If a thought, which at the end of the day is all Aristotle is, and Fosse too. Fuck I forgot. If a thought. Is this really the thought I had?: If a thought don't jump high enough as to capture my attention, it falls in the abyss of non-existence. All philosophers were were advertisers trying to get into my head. Some could, and I became them, or rather I became what they never were, or rather they became me. The others acted as though they never existed. Every book I don't read till the rest of my life doesn't exist. It's a glimpse. It's a mirage. At the end of the day all the world is just a field of thoughts popping and disappearing. All we ever did was manage thoughts. And here we are. Here we shall be. Time condemned us. Not having memory is not only a contributor but a prerequisite for living reality. Thoughts were all the world has ever seen. Thoughts are all the world will ever see. So fool of we to think there is something else, something more. But that ends it too well. So we have to ruin it, for it is what is needed, not what is wanted. Ads are insane if you think about it. I mean actually insane. I genuinely do think people have mistaken them for something that actually has some substance, some depth of reality. Like they have become something, and so they are something. Ads are the prime way to say nothing worth noting is happening. Ads have sunk and snuck in such a way that no one sees the madness and the death and the evil that they entail. And what if I deleted all this that I have written. Now that would be something real. Coward. Was this the birth of an heteronym? A "todo eu sou uma força que me escapa". A thought happens. A world thinks. Hey, someone in another galaxy has seen me and I have seen them. If the world is a simulation thoughts are the zeros and ones. That's the zero of this world. E festas, não festas de festejar, mas festinhas a um bébé. Isso é das coisas que digo com mais certeza neste mundo. Now you do the thinking. Slave. Bitch. Written after reaching page 9.

Philosophy.title: Jon Fosse: Escape from Advertising and the Mirror Lie
Philosophy.content: It's like everywhere everything is at every point discontinuous. Irrational. And what I would have lost had I not had the thought of starting to write this. Embrace the eternal solitary confinement of life. One to one privacy, intimacy is an illusion of the people who live in the same plane as advertisements. Dare to think. There is no such thing as inspiration. That's a lie people made themselves to rationalize actions. I am not inspired. I am not bullshit. I just followed my thought as a cat follows the flashlight. If u want some real inspiration count to 10 thousand. If there is a first cause to things it did not come from inspiration. Perhaps from confession. People think they learned how to walk but they have no clue how it happens, how it is even possible. People have a tremendous gap in knowledge and awareness. Hence poets advertising their way into and through your head, hence spirituality. Sometimes picking up a boring ass book like this one makes wonders. A book has to be sufficiently boredom-inducing as to make you think of better ways, as to make you approach yourself in the mirror. Seeing yourself in the mirror, likewise, is among the great lies of modern times. Mirrors will eventually depict what you think of yourself, oh lord would I love to see myself for the first time again. Hence the dragon scroll. With nothing. Just what you yourself want it to be. No big truth like that would be handed to you in such a way. If you're lucky when you picked up the dragon scroll you saw yourself. I'm convinced that writing with a spike up your ass in a chair makes you spit realness. In retrospect, you are the reader of yourself. Well not just in the sense I've been talking bout, but take advantage of your never ending multiplicity, as it gives you direct line premium view to look into your tendencies.

Philosophy.title: Jon Fosse: The Metanarration and the Fest of Chaos
Philosophy.content: The metanarration: your mind is capable of playing games with you, with itself. Fooling yourself is just rationalizing inside your mind, which is way easier than letting the cake feel the exposedness of real dry air, and the cold of the day. One can get caught up justifying the problems he has created, daydreaming. Daydreaming is only a disease of the bored ones. A rumbling of thoughts, the author limits himself to ordering them. Fluctuations. Go eat something. A little water please. An arena of events, an horizon of war for attention, for each thought comes with another one superimposing at your conscious will, venerating your conscious mind, like fishes trying to jump out of the boat to go to sea. Like bubbles trying to get out of the pan, a festival of chaos. Entropy. Systemic imbalance and disequilibrium. Erratic way, like drops of water racing for the end of the glass of the car. Present like the weather, real like the weather. Oh weather oh weather, bless us with your spontaneity and vulgarity. And screw words, let them flow without course, let them free even if they make no sense. Pause. Thinking. Comfort. Modernity and its shortcomings. Turn off the TV. Too much noise. Too little weather. Let the weather be. Those convos about weather, oh my god is there better philosophy than that. Let the lows sit low. Walk like this, like this man drove his car. Walk like this little gummie. No poeticizing the way, just messing it up that more, making it real ugly, real. Shaking of the leg. Sounds penetrating and making impact. Many sounds. Many voices. Nothing worth it. Let your anxieties express themselves through your body language. Will to sleep. Fart. Tweaks and twitches and spasms all over. Shoulder pop and weird feeling. People have been neglecting spasms and that's where modernity fails. That's also where philosophy went wrong. Spasms hold the secret of the world and few people know it. Sorry for playing and being ironic.

Philosophy.title: Jon Fosse: Time, False Memories, and the Independent Reality
Philosophy.content: The rest is serious and meaningful I swear. No one swears. Promises are among the most beautiful of things, and yet people change at every second. You only get to live with yourself and live yourself 1 second for every second. And you have no time to see yourself. No time to see anything. Time lies and has been lying to everyone since it was thought of. Time is there only for the not present ones. By definition. By the words themselves. Fucking genius. Good stuff. Stuffing the people of the future with nonsense, fillers to fill their already filled lives, filled of shit should I say, a mountain of cards built through years of doing nothing. Convincing themselves of past achievements, not realizing it's false, not realizing nothing of the past influences the present. Not realizing this is the only and it's an independent reality. When there is only one reality, why fill it with more? Stuffing the world with something of our minds. Let it be for once god sake. Don't give it an age, don't give names to things, we have created a simulation for our own enjoyment the moment we started naming things. This is all there is and all there will ever be. Each phrase of this, each very word was not written by the same person, was it even written for the same audience, dare I say. Every Camões that came and went had to see blankness, had to forget about what it has done. Every one of them has had to write each and every poem they wrote, and had to think of each and every thought they thought. No givens, only a pen and a paper, only a world and yourself. And fuck the lettuces of this world.

Philosophy.title: Man's Search for Meaning: Primalism and the End of Uncertainty
Philosophy.content: That the best didn't come back, that the concentration camps made for an arena of primalism, where technology was only hands, where days were counted and grateful, where the only wine was blood. Where mankind went back thousands of years. Where the fair were forgotten. Where humans were lost and humanity forgotten. Que o ataque espiritual era penetrante, que muitos viam-se a admitir ter perdido o espírito, e daí a poucos o recuperarem. Que dormir seria o maior prémio da vida deles. Que não saber a data do fim do encarceramento quebrava os espíritos. Que a incerteza do fim era o mesmo que o fim da incerteza. Que volta à ideia de que o "fim" tem dois significados, o fim de acabar e o fim de objetivo. Volta à ideia de que a certeza da morte dá a certeza de objetivo. Ou melhor, que dá a incerteza do objetivo.

Philosophy.title: The Blind Watchmaker: The Hierarchy of Explanation and Simplicity
