Philosophy.content: "Vamos percorrendo a hierarquia de subcomponente a subcomponente, até atingirmos unidades tão simples que, para fins práticos, já não tenhamos necessidade de fazer perguntas sobre elas." Basicamente, quando a explicação de um fenómeno torna-se simples o suficiente, não há necessidade de uma outra. A simplicidade da explicação é o que exausta a curiosidade humana, mas devido ao que normalmente acontece, nunca chegamos a um patamar inteiramente satisfatório de simplicidade, e é o que ateia fogo à curiosidade humana. Dawkins diz que os objetos biológicos distinguem-se dos objetos da física porque o seu arranjo é muito improvável, embora continuem a ser objetos causados e de acordo com as leis da física (no creator necessary). Dawkins fala do design excelente da ecolocalização, que tem todas as propriedades que um designer inteligente pensaria para conseguir a ecolocalização. Aquela espécie que usa melhor as leis da física mostra-nos como usar as leis da física. Se quisermos compreender melhor o design, primeiro formulamos leis da física e depois compreendemos os seres vivos, ou compreendendo os seres vivos compreendemos as leis da física? Penso que os dois.

Philosophy.title: Tao of Jeet Kune Do: Awareness, Flow, and Conscious Unconsciousness
Philosophy.content: There is a difference between concentration and awareness, one reductive the other preserving its isness, no exclusion. There is only this moment, the reality has no time, that's only when thought invades the empty free mind. When in danger of dying, there is only flow, everything ceases to exist, a real feeling, no method no means. To be in the circle with no circumference. Be a superposition of everything, collapsing when necessary, not being when needed. "Nirvana is to be consciously unconscious or to be unconsciously conscious". This way of being is Pessoa's ideal, the best of both worlds, the child and the thinker. It's the floating daring survival bubble. And so zen is to be an uncollapsed wave function, yet defined. "Float in totality". Be like water my friend, undefined at first look but all encompassing at last instance. Power over the mind, power of being able to learn, use what you learn and forget what you learned. Nirvana is immediate, natural, like a leaf falling from a tree. It is, in sum, to be one with the light. No time. Pure flow.

Philosophy.title: Tao of Jeet Kune Do: The Imbalance of Action
Philosophy.content: The synthesis. The propensity to action, he says, is the manifestation of inner imbalance. When at balance you are at rest, or changing with the change, so as to be resting relative to it. It's skewed to think action is balanced. When going out to the world that's imbalancing, when sleeping one is balanced. When one dreams or remembers his place one imposes the imbalance on himself.

Philosophy.title: The Unabomber Manifesto: The Cost of the Revolution
Philosophy.content: Ted wants to catch the system in a crisis to make a revolution, the counter ideal to tech in Nature. Maybe a beautiful way for mankind to go: evolve, reach the peak (which we would have to agree on what it is), and then come back, reverse evolve, take care of the problems that we created along the way, showing gratefulness for each step of the journey, a sort of spiritual acceptance. A satisfaction of finally knowing what we are, and where we came from, perhaps the most fundamental questions to be answered for mankind. This means accepting our end, something that again is not easy, not easy to give up and be satisfied, but maybe would be smarter to go out on our terms, leave space for other species that may go the same way as us, letting the Universe rest, instead of insisting, fighting and going out fighting. Tough choice, don't even know what I would choose. It comes down to: are you here for a long time or for a great time? And the thing too is that I'm not sure if humans who abdicated tech, knowingly, could live with their own failure, to cite Thanos. "As long as there are those who remember what was, there will always be those who are unable to accept what can be" - Thanos. Then comes the fundamentalist part, the revolutionaries would have to give their lives to the mission of destroying industrial technological society, they would reproduce abundantly so that more people thought against tech. I mean it just starts going crazy from now. Ted is suggesting without remorse or doubt that his ideas can turn out to be wrong, that it is in the mankind interest that a genocide occurs. But how is he so certain of his ideas? He's willing to risk mankind for the ideas that he developed in the book. Even going as far as saying to not only use tech to destroy tech, which I'm ok with, but training the mass reproduced children into an army against tech. So he would create a system of controlled behavior to take down the system of controlled behavior, take out freedom of the revolutionaries. Very tough to make it happen. The only dogma is the elimination of technology, the rest is empirical he says, as if the ideas of his book don't work, discard. He claims that the enthusiasm for progress is a modern thing, and so the new society wouldn't want to progress. Man here I disagree totally. And he roasts the hell out of leftists, beginning and ending his book with it. Maybe I should do a mini revolution, but in my life, so moving away from technology, that is also a major part of people today getting depressed and what not.

Philosophy.title: The Unabomber Manifesto: Modern Helplessness vs Universal Control
Philosophy.content: Searched up on Google to see if there are some inspirations and a lot of people noticed this too. Says that today's man is much more helpless than the primitive. Hmm I'm not sure about that. Sure in the sense that we described before that is the case. But the path of today's man leads him to be the master of the world, so he controls everything. Primitive man has no idea how the sun goes up and down, no idea of weather, animal behavior, so in a sense modern man makes nature. Maybe there is an issue with this reasoning, maybe it's that the man representative of the collective gets more and more control, but each individual along the way to build this overman has less control. Ted then errs on the side of psychological security, psychological health. Psychological well being is the best thing to have, instead of living 200 years healthy but living a natural (but worse in this aspect) life. It comes back to "ignorance is bliss". If cavemen were unaware of how insignificant they were in the Universe, thinking that their 10x10 mile square of land is the world, sure they would feel more like gods than us. I mean what would you rather live like? The workaround that Ted does is "oh but man could accept the things that he couldn't control stoically", whereas today they are man-made.

Philosophy.title: The Unabomber Manifesto: The System as a Self-Preserving Entity
Philosophy.content: So he goes on to make these principles for how history works, and how reforms won't be sufficient for the system to stop its momentum; it would take a Thanos-like courage to restart where it has gone wrong, and accept that there might be unpredictable radical consequences. He points out problems of the system, what I want to see is how he proposes to solve them with his revolution. He talks about the system as a being, as an entity that keeps itself alive for example with propaganda, interesting. "If you think that big government interferes in your life too much, just wait till the government starts regulating the genetic constitution of your children." He seems to make some predictions which might very well turn out to be true; the question is whether that should be or shouldn't.

Philosophy.title: The Unabomber Manifesto: The Industrial System and Surrogate Activities
Philosophy.content: Ted talks about the system, the industrial system (I'm reading this book in parallel to reading Simulacra and Simulation). Ted wants a revolution against tech and the economic basis, not a political affair. He thinks we do things for tech, maybe that's the being that is feeding off of us, like what I hypothesized in Simulacra and Simulation. It seems that the way he is going with this is that life was meant to be life in a more primitive way, where what he calls the "power process" (having goals that require effort and achieving them) gives meaning to our lives, and was more present. Because when now you are part of a group so large that you contribute so little, it's not as fulfilling. So the collective advancement and collaboration among humans takes away from our fulfillment. I mean, it seems that he is saying that living primitively/biologically was better. And yeah, if some people want to engage in that, like restarting, it must be hella fun, almost like starting a new game with no resources and only themselves. Yeah that would be cool, but reductive of the potential of humans. On an individual basis one could say that is the way to go, but look at tech, it's amazing what we can do. Yea, we are not free, we are more contributing to the being that feeds off our growth than to ourselves, but it has been the decision of the majority of people up until now, whether they were aware or not. Where I'm failing to see is what the big problem is with this, why is it more than just a choice, why does Ted call for a revolution? Is mental health gonna crash at some point, are we gonna become ever more slaves?

Philosophy.title: The Unabomber Manifesto: The Power Process and Artificial Goals
Philosophy.content: He says that the natural power process has been disrupted in modern society, and explains that this society is so mentally down and depressed because whereas before it felt like you produced change, now it seems it is imposed upon us. I honestly think this is not my fucking problem, I don't understand that people have these states of mind, don't get why, yeah just not my problem. Says that the drives today of which victory is more tasty, those which take hard work, are artificial goals, "surrogate activities". Well yeah we evolved, we are not living according to our biology, yeah whatever let's escape free of our natural instinct. What I learned from this book till now is that actually maybe the cavemen were among the happiest, or let's say, the lives that you'd wanna live if you had to choose. Especially for old people in the mind: calm, same things over and over, simple, live/die, no attachment to dreams, no simulation, no hyperreality, just the food on the plate and raising the children. Maybe what it means is that whilst the victors get an increasingly better life, for the majority of people, the average people, the quality of life in this sense goes down. And yeah the artificialities of today are very real. Ted says that ads make us wanna buy things we don't like, and yeah today we don't do what we actually want, we enter into more and more nebulous and dubious and complex systems of values, we do things such that others like us... yeah eventually people realize that they were slaves to the system, and that what really mattered all along was family, for example. Fight Club is exactly this, making of a small group which engages in this primitive way of living and they seem to enjoy it, they are fulfilled.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Why People Find Beautiful Things Harmless
Philosophy.content: That is why people find things beautiful harmless, they see by definition only the toned down tamed gracious fire of the beast, they see nothing what it is capable of. They don't understand its power nor its strength, nor of it nor of its master.

Philosophy.title: The Blind Watchmaker: Death as the Surrender to Entropy
Philosophy.content: "Seja qual for a quantidade de formas de estar vivo que haja, é absolutamente certo que há uma quantidade amplamente maior de formas de estar morto, ou antes, não vivo." "Se as coisas vivas não trabalharem ativamente, fundir-se-iam com a sua envolvente e deixariam de existir como seres autónomos." Ou seja, a morte é a dissolução, é a desistência de lutar contra o avanço da simetria e entropia. My quote: It's not that they break the laws of physics, is that they know how to use them.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- All This World Is a Dispute of Trying
Philosophy.content: All this world is a dispute of trying and all your wins are about trying

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Power Is What Gives Graciousness Its Rarity
Philosophy.content: But with no power behind it to back it up, with no capability of violence and all evil, graciousness is default, there is no beast to tame, there is no scarcity, everyone can get it, no one can reach over and beyond.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Preacher of the Earthly and the Normal
Philosophy.content: And how advantageous is it to be a preacher of that which is all consistent, the earthly, the normal. No need for anything abnormal. You can rise over and beyond this world through all normality.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Fairness of the Lived Moment
Philosophy.content: If there is further fairness and equality in this world is in that the moment is lived. There is really no memory in that you may have crossed all 7 layers of hell right before being born into a moment, you are all the same. All the like the same. This can be taken advantage of, that you truly can go through these layers, head down, consciousness of if you so please and only awaken in the other side. You cannot trade now for later. Either you grab something now or never. Now or never, ever. If in doubt flat out. No saving now will increase your later output. That is always the same lie.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Taste as the Weight and Measure
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche says taste is the weight and what weighs. Is it the measure and the measured. The compared and the source of comparison. It is that by which you judge things, that they can be judged means they are tastes of some form, and at the same time the value of your own, that is only your own if it is your judgment, your taste.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Removing the Inherited Filters
Philosophy.content: There are still some filters to be taken out from the inside of your mind. Some conceptions, they call it preconceptions, because it wasn't this your being now present who decided upon them, it was thrown into you, whether by external things or even by previous versions of you. Free yourself from the stuff that previous versions of you used for their own sake. Do not confuse yourself with this, open your mind. Open your mind means opening the doors that you haven't opened, pressing the buttons that you haven't, and understanding your mind, picking apart its parts.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Winning the Wills of Others
Philosophy.content: And that winning the will of others is thus what can be won, for non willed things are totally public fair and square. they can't be allocated, deployed, gamed, hacked, yours.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- All Choice Is About Taste
Philosophy.content: And all choice is about taste and tasting after all.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Everyone Sees Themselves at the Center
Philosophy.content: Everyone sees the world through them being at the center. Everyone sees things in such a way that they who see it are the balanced ones. Thus when an author for example tries to add something to knowledge with his own thing, trying to fit it in the wheel and make it correct for him, even tho he tried to have the complete theory with him adding his own bit and yet having the balance to connect everything, people reading will want to associate him much more with just the bit he added, making him a champion of it, and they themselves as the balanced ones who now incorporate that into the whole.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- In Secret the Overhuman Is Born
Philosophy.content: Thus in secret the overhuman is born. But it shall not be explained for now. Singularity.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Vision Dictates How Much You Shatter
Philosophy.content: Your vision of how much there is still to build dictates how much you are willing to shatter.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Building the Greatest Good Within
Philosophy.content: And our greatest good that which we will build within ourselves to overcome ourselves.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Law of the Strongest and Life as Master
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche puts forward this explanation that the law of the strongest being the master of the weaker comes from everyone and not only the strongest let alone the stronger. That even the strongest yields to life itself its master. That all the both weaker and stronger to something serve the stronger and endorse this yielding so that they can be masters of the weaker. Thus one plays this game of life. Thus life is obeying, that Nietzsche puts great emphasis in having figured out. Even the greatest, and precisely because that coined term makes only sense for those who play this game, is still slave to something, hasn't escaped his slavery to life itself. On the one hand it makes some sense for the master of the game to keep playing the game by being itself a slave, but symmetrically to all others, thus the argument extends.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Purpose Through the Master-Slave Order
Philosophy.content: Maybe a purpose can only be introduced through the ordering of master slave. That thus master and slave need each other. They feed off each other. That through ordering one has place to go, namely over and beyond, and that can be the purpose of not our life but life itself.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Fortifying Beliefs
Philosophy.content: Among those beliefs that self fortify are the belief that power can be attained, can be good, and that all drives can be fulfilled. Fundamentally placing no limit in the power of your being, in how much you can juggle. Everything everywhere all at once. It is possible that it is possible to maximize every drive, and that in fact the pushing of any one drive is independent of any other, that they all add up that they all interfere constructively, however hard may it be.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Your Becoming or Someone Else's Being
Philosophy.content: You choose with whom you cross the river. With your becoming or someone else's being. You embark with your values or someone else's. You are either the master of your ship or you are in someone else's "frota"

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Becoming the Creator of Worlds
Philosophy.content: I am become life, the creator of worlds. You like a god of gods would want to create a world that is yet so much beyond himself that one may lay back and kneel in admiration. You overhuman shall bring the overuniverse. If you dare you can create beyond all that you have made, beyond yourself, your world. The cycle closes. You are already a god when the world is yours, so overgod must you be to make something even the bigger. Ultimately this world isn't enough for you, this life isn't enough to fill your appetite.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Humans as Masters of Making Things Understandable
Philosophy.content: Easy ways out are what humans thus have become masters of. We should marvel at the little world we have made for ourselves and our little heads and scopes, and sight and vision. Our greatest masterpiece as a species is that we made something understandable, we picked out the best piece of the world to be understood and relied on.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Willing the Thinkability of All Beings
Philosophy.content: Nietzsche suggests willing the thinkability of all beings, of all things. Can this help you? Well yeah for you can conquer their thoughts, you can spread yourself more, you can will the world to your will, thus your will wills it. You can will the world to your win and win the world to your will. More freedom more power more life. Your will shall in essence be universal, something that can talk with any being, something that can dance and resonate to the frequencies of the world your world. The world as will and representation.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will the Greatness of the Universe
Philosophy.content: So will the greatness and power of the will to bring about the overuniverse. The overmoment. The overlife and the overspirit. The overpower of your will. Believe thus that all the universe is watching your very will in action, all of it talks to you and between one another about you. That what you do in life echoes in eternity. Let every moment of your life echo in eternity and eternally in yourself. And every eternal life echoes in your moment. And the whole world resonates, thus progression and freedom.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Will Brings the Moment to Life
Philosophy.content: Will that brings that moment to life. That makes the moment eternal, that saves from the genocide, that transcends time, that outnatures the child.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Overcoming
Philosophy.content: Self overcoming, I am to be overcome, the human is something that is to be overcome.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Language as Tool, World as Experience Beyond It
Philosophy.content: Language is a tool of thought and communication, but the world as an invention is much older and perfected than it. Your eyes tell you so much that you underestimate just cause you can't put it into words. Your experience goes so much beyond what can even be projected onto and represented by language. If I couldn't write down and contribute to the cult of the overhuman through human's progress, if I couldn't document this journey of the overhuman, then I would just allow myself to experience. To experience reality better than anyone who is already too closed minded to think that the resonances and the coupling was to be made solely through language this new thing.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- First Hear, Then Resonate
Philosophy.content: First hear the sound of the world, only then can you vibrate to its frequency. Only then can you hope to Resonate with it.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Those Who Want to Punish Cannot Live in the Present
Philosophy.content: Those that want to punish cannot live in the present. They cannot believe in the unreality of the past. They seek power for the wrong reasons, to be at peace with the world they live in, not understanding neither that it isn't theirs nor that it has anything that needs rescue.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- No Human Sees the Power of Spirit
Philosophy.content: And that yet no human can see the power of spirit, what it can do.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- From Smallest to Greatest: Same Flesh
Philosophy.content: That continuous still is the transition from smallest to greatest human, topologically equivalent they are, naked they are but of the same flesh. There is no semblance of the overhuman yet

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Masses' Inherited Power
Philosophy.content: We shouldn't neglect the amount of power that the masses hold and pass generation to generation, how much they can shape all of yesterday and tomorrow, a la 1984, even if no single one of them can grasp all their impact, or even know why the spirit is a certain way. Those that started and knew their reasons may be far gone, yet their consequences can live on if they aren't intentionally taken care of. We can be stuck on obeying orders from those that don't even exist. the other side of the coin of collective building and standing on the shoulders of giants

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Hero's Last Self-Deception
Philosophy.content: Thus the hero fools himself one last time, now that he wants to express his heroicity. he doesn't understand all he did was for himself. All was voluntary. He starts to believe is mode a little too much, his narrative and vision start to become real to him, so much so that he wants to impose them onto others. He fails to realize that all that in him was hero is not anymore by this sharing. He cannot produce more than he has through making public all he went through to produce his stuff. structure

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Desiring Both Coherence and Self-Actualization
Philosophy.content: That one may desire both coherency and self actualization, that then one would find peace, owning both the model and reality, doing the right and having understood the reason to it. Paving the right journey and knowing its true meaning. Paving the path as much as one knows its story. Mostly we don't. We move for different motives than those that will be recounted further on by those who tell the tales of the heros. Heroes were thus never understood. again they couldn't be. The complicity alone between them should suffice for them.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Love and the Creation Beyond the Self
Philosophy.content: Love of yourself makes you despise yourself in going for the creation beyond yourself, makes you perish. Becoming the overhuman is being the overhuman perhaps.

Philosophy.title: On Silence, Frame Control, and True Questions
Philosophy.content: Quotes and questions: it is tendency to think that that thing which we lack so much, which I lack so much, which is keeping in mind the long run in the now, having that greatness instinct even when you are doing the most dull thing, that truthness that you forget and substitute thus for lesser things, thus you think you have nothing to do, when you have very much the very opposite. This you think is solved by throwing in stuff to the mind, like music, motivational videos or whatever, when in reality it is found in silence. Truth is better cultivated alone and alone of the rest. Doing nothing for 2 hours may bring back the truth you once recognized. It can furthermore make you see things your body and mind are perhaps trying to say to you of a long time, that yet didn't go through all the noise, couldn't make it to the surface amidst all the pressure that was put on the moment. More is less. The force builds in you clarity. You have a real question, and don't know the answer? You have a dilemma? Sit in silence and the answers will be clear. A la antiga a la kung fu panda, most of the training and time spent by the warrior is reflecting, looking at the water and seeing it look back. Most of the time is spent in the confinement of your mind, in the light of your shadow, finding yourself. The show that others see is in scope very limited to all you've blessed. It is only the very top of the iceberg that others can see, that others can understand. Your job is to make this blackbox of a thing come to life, take all that only you can understand all that's puzzling the self and figure it all out, all, and only you can, and make it come true in what is seen by others. people then ask why in movies they have such assuredness, why they do it like in movies, natural unwavering faith in their outcome. True main character stuff, that you go your way and your way is the way. But there it is they know damn better than others the world they want to be, and they make it, and they make others live in it. The stronger the frame the more likely it becomes everyone's frame. The more truthness and suredness the frame carries, the stronger it gets, The more undeniable, talk about frame control in the life man. this is what they have. What they hold. So much power is in the frame. I will, the masters of frame know. those who have learned to live without reality are the ones who come to find their reality and make it. Those who tune to the deeper frequencies of thought lower levels of questions. This way is not about feeling moving, but about finding the right direction to move to, then go for it. Finding the worthy opponents, the worthy bridges of overcoming, the worthy flags in top of the mountains, into the overhuman. There are few questions that you must answer in your life, most of people spend life, not enjoy it, actually spend it without facing the questions, perhaps because just off the top of their head they don't see clearly the answer, and they have no interest in seeking deeper. Life is mostly about getting away from noise and finding the gem in the sea. Because its huge, this is the way to go. Quality of decisions. Quality of output into the true great questions

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Elevation and the Godlike Will
Philosophy.content: Think about it. Elevation brings you closer to the sky, closer to being a god. Having will and being over, that is, overcoming the things gives you god like power. Do you think god worries or spends time on little human things. Rise thus, elevate yourself, gain control of all around you and god shall you be crowned. one step for me that I haven't taken is elevating beyond good and evil. This overcoming on the path to the overhuman is one Nietzsche dwells a lot with.

Philosophy.title: The Blind Watchmaker: Telos and Human Perception
Philosophy.content: "As palavras são nossos servidores, não nossos senhores." Acho que a finalidade das coisas (telos) não é parte da natureza, mas sim da nossa perceção. O telos faz parte do filme que fazemos do mundo. Nós buscamos o significado nas coisas, o porquê. O significado das coisas, tautologicamente, é o que dá significado às coisas.

Philosophy.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Solitude, the Highlight Era, and the Overhuman
Philosophy.content: That in solitude and from the exterior of the state may the overhuman rise and take over the mere human. the era of the highlight, that today things amount to nothing unless a show is made out of them, Nietzsche says. Image becomes an ever more important commodity, a necessary and sufficient medium of truth, of belief, of idea. Truly ever more things you did in your life are more irrelevant if they weren't filmed. And yet we that in this generation passed through both scenarios have still this privacy to us, that no one knows what we've been doing all our lives, it gives so much space to achieve so much without anyone knowing. I think we shouldn't neglect the brain drain that happens right inside our homes, with little children future owners of mankind watching tv shows that at times bless nothing onto them. Even worse for old people, that are allowed watching nothing binge-watched shows that drain life away. They in reality show that one doesn't need meaning to live, as the discussion of Camus made me think. One can just pass along not bothering much. When death becomes widely recognized as more avoidable I wonder what will be done to these people. Or to these shows rather.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Cold Sobriety and Changing Convictions
Philosophy.content: To have to carry out in the coldness and sobriety what you decided in the midst of passion, says Nietzsche, is one of the biggest burdens. This coldness and sobriety seems to me an inevitable non-zero measure interval of your experience. And in that you are not anymore backed by the naivity and relentlessness of the passion. Had you made too much ground when on your high, then perhaps you may find yourself bound when sobriety hits like a brick. Whether that means promising too much to others, in essence talking too much, lending yourself in fight for coherence over the trap you've made for yourself. Nietzsche argues that it shouldn't be a shame to change convictions midway, he wants us to furthermore become routine traitors of the convictions, so that we do not suffer unnecessarily from the burden of having to keep up with your past self's aspirations. I find however for me a lack of commitment to these past decisions. I have to much more respect these decisions and be bound to them, for the lack of pressure by rejecting them causes an implosion of the soul the like only cause of slowing down of the day. That an enemy of he is, he who pursues convictions with no method. A form of fanatical thinker that gets excited with the first explanation of the unexplained that he comes up with. He declares it his own and runs with it. Very many died for their convictions thinking they were sacrificing for truth.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Truth, Ego, and the Ignorance of Dying for a Cause
Philosophy.content: The human all too human enjoyer of life wants to have truth as long as it is sound with him, as long as he is the one reaching it. It takes humbleness in the devotion of truth, in that all you ever produced may suddenly have to be thrown away for something that another human did. You must then not be too bound to your works in the name of truth. I myself still do this quite a bit in philosophy, you get inspired and spit some great hypothesis but then you are hesitant in passing them through a rough filter in fear they may not come out as fit and worthy of truth. Truth can make your ego play limbo. "Out of passions grow opinions." That the limit of one who holds no convictions, has performed the infinite resignation movement, is to have only carefully calculated probabilities for each assertion. How could one then die for something? Dying for it implies the leap of faith that that which we are dying for is absolute truth. He who holds no more convictions cannot die for anything without acknowledging his mistake. Perhaps that is once more the bliss of the ignorant, that they can in all their right and innocence of mind actually believe they are dying for something bigger, that they are doing their part. They are by this means more likely to die.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Paths, Annoyance, and The Boring Truth
Philosophy.content: That it is the path that is taken that offers the most inertia instead of the destination. People are sure where they are going but too thinly know where they are headed. Don't underestimate the hardness of keeping a destination in mind through the paths. The hard part is not following through, but to keep in mind the end goal while doing so. Annoyance as a physical illness. Yep annoyance mostly boils down to the state of your mind at the moment. We however usually find a justification for it. Is our demand satisfied we find another one. The problem is in not realizing this independency, and so giving in to your momentary desires. Truth stays low not the most when it's dangerous, but rather when it's boring, Nietzsche says. Truth has this ruthless impartiality to it. It is or it isn't. Its search presents the least of guarantees to anything desirable. When truth is boring fewer want to tell its story. Its story gets lost in the ways of the imaginary things. People are there that given the truth of the world would never get out of the first stage - denial. It can get worse than there not being any truth, it can be boring to the point one can't find words or descriptions to elevate its status. The dragon scroll. It is thus required a harsher sense of direction. It is harder to stick to such an ending of possible disappointment. It is not rewarding in the usual sense. Opting out midway is in all likelihood the smartest choice. It requires a selfless soul of the kind that gives away his life to reach the untamed waters.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Nature, Comfort, and Questioning Reality
Philosophy.content: We say that we like nature because it doesn't have an opinion about us, says Nietzsche. For one, you definitely see this behaviour when people steal nature for themselves, talking to it and responding back in an attempt to believe what they want to. Such is summed up in the phrase "only you understand me", when talking to flowers or something. Thinking about it really points out how ridiculous it is that we do this routinely. We have to put some voice into something to hear what we wanna hear. May truth turn us into animals again, Nietzsche asks. May irrationality hold the truest way. Fight club. That we need consolation more so for that we were consoled easily than for the pain of death of a loved one. Nietzsche's appeal to some portion of the masses, is that he has these dangerous if followed statements. He shows us a very bad reality of what human beings do. By coming one with his words you perhaps satisfy your need to challenge the system. This is his way of covering truth, through the danger it represents. Much less popular would it be if he left it plainly and boringly. So he makes this trade, how raw does he really want the truth that composes his symphony be? And how many will dance to his music? What he then most likely hopes is that I see through the chords and into the fundamental melody, even if dissonant, that underlies it all. We adhere to opinions in the name of our ego too, sometimes. The first thought of the day, Nietzsche suggests should be whether we can give pleasure to at least one person in this day, instead of the usual prayer. The first thought of the day I believe should set the rest going. Win it and your day will become more meaningful. Win the first thoughts.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Worshipping Genius and The Annoyance of Normality
Philosophy.content: He points out that the mechanism of worshipping a genius is much similar to that of Gods, and that when one does it, one runs into the risk of seeing the basic masses as more inferior than he should. This worshipping has at its core that the worshipper doesn't understand the possibility of the genius/God. He is left with only faith as the resource for fathoming, commensurating with what is understood this beast of the thing that remains elusive to our take on the world. In fact there is also this effect of dismissing the genius when you know what he does. If you think you could be doing what is doing, if by another token he is similar to you in that he plays at your level, be it through leisure conversation, or watching a movie, then you feel he is normal, you feel he doesn't fit the image of the machine you have in your head that could be yourself. This image is what you perceive as the great man of the masses. What you know you could do had you just given everything. Had you acted at the limit of what you know you can act. The omnipresence of this image of the will gives rise to the admiration of the great man, as you think he has that will, even tho no one does, for he himself finds in others the genius that he isn't. So obviously if you spend all time with one person, and you see he doesn't meet your expectations of the genius, you dismiss him as "yeah he studies a lot but he isn't a genius", for example. There must be room for fantasy, for you to put your part into the story of the genius that lives in your head, his perverse body just a confirmation of his existence. By believing in the possibility of the genius, which is because you worship, you are disconsidering the masses who don't fit. You find them dull, you almost come to halt your belief that they are people equal to yourself. When you are so sure of the higher presence, normality becomes annoying, unrespectable, unforgivable. And here in all practicality you must maintain your gratitude and appreciation.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Happiness, Globalization, and Inner Strengths
Philosophy.content: That there isn't such a thing as happy eras. That happiness is property of the moment, and that it isn't extensible. However miserable you once were, no matter its duration, you are condemned to expect the present, and therefore not continue that sudden happiness. Happiness is elusive and has no regard for the past. When you imagine that you will be "happy forever" after doing something, like in fairy tales, you started with the wrong foot. If animated movies had a continuation, then after the narrator says "and they were happy ever since" we would see a zooming out and after a while, someone says I'm hungry. And the moment collapses on itself. There is this nationalistic way of wanting to preserve its people as representing the country, its boundaries being the colors or façons of their people. So avoiding mixing of races. If this is the view of some people, they find it disconcerting the consequences of globalization. The process of mixing happens slowly, but one day we may be one world, in fact it doesn't take that many generations for, with great probability, each of us being an equidistribution of the people's genes on earth. That will build the best humans. If these nationalistic gurus want to keep their sanity alive and well they will have to find other ways beyond language, or the façons of the people, to differentiate the citizens of the world. Perhaps one day there will be so much fluctuations that the approximation of a commensurable criteria that mostly separates the countrymen from the foreigners will fade, we will be left with the higher entropy state of everyone having a bit of everything. That war rages inner strengths the like we will only approximate with the surrogate activities we found enchanting of this very power. War makes you feel alive. For that reason it caught even the most exhausted ones with some will to push on. Most people are alive and can't tell it, and that, as now wars have halted for our good sake, will be inevitable don't we find our battles to fight. So war is about territory inasmuch as it is about satisfying our desire. War is as much a nightmare as a ritual for our soul. Put a group of humans together and they will find some means to fight, are they still ashamed of this our nature. Else they'll do it like one has sex. Another face of the ego. That we allocate more to criticizing that with which we don't agree than that to which we agree, the latter much more dangerous. We act in the way that maintains this view of our goodwill.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: The Depths of Evil and The Matrix of Suppression
Philosophy.content: That diving deep enough into something brings to light the bad in it. Thus it is hard to stay digging the same hole. Thus the deeper you go not only the less there are but also the more are coming back. Like a marriage, you may be advised to think twice before you know everything there is to know. In an unfair world like this at present there are some things you don't wanna know for your sake. In fact even those that we come across get sunk into our unconsciousness without ever seeing the light of the day. Things can get very disturbing out there. Staying on your lane is one of the few things we have learned in our walks. I bet there are things that would break your heart, break your youthful hopeful spirit. They would fill you with horror and disgust for what before found home. Going deeper may change you in ways you didn't want to. Maybe it's as harmless as about knowing the truth behind events in your life, gaining a new perspective on things that keeps you awake at night. Cause there is a lot that goes under this constructed surface. And the surface was made to protect you, not with the intention of lying to you. The collective being has repressed some of its wildest characters and forms. The rabbit hole goes deep, that I'm sure. Something tells me that as much work as we put into building this surface is what we put into closing the evils of this world in the bottom. Cause you need to go to sleep at night. All do. But there have been billions walking on this earth, each compounding on a giant evil we have walked along ourselves. Perhaps not some evil you want to fathom alone, this evil that was built by millions upon millions. You are in such a safe space you don't even conceive of the wild stuff that is happening out there. Even if you wanted to escape the matrix, maybe you don't really, when you see what's out there. There are levels to this shit. There is a lot of sickness in nature. Like sickness you don't wanna know. Part of maturing is getting further in this awareness. Disconcerting. This is another way in which the matrix is real. But we built it for ourselves. Again no creators, it was the development of the collective awareness that most people don't wish to go down there, most don't come back. It is one of the undercover genius inventions of our kind. It serves its purpose and we shall thank for not having to worry about the depths where the light can't reach choose we not to.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Judgement, Egos, and the Illusion of Mastery
Philosophy.content: On judgement. The effects of judgment ought to be thought as the manifestations of our big egos. We usually don't like the judgement of others, cause they don't know us completely, even if they know things that we don't know about ourselves. Because they don't know us completely we feel like their judgement is too down to earth. Our egos make us think that others will have moderate success while we will have tremendous success. Each of us lives in this bubble of self confidence and faith that we are the chosen ones for indeed our experience shows us we are for we are the only ones we can guarantee even exist. So throw down the results. We also like to convince ourselves that they haven't us figured out, that we still have something up our sleeve, that they miss the point, that we don't say nothing because we don't stop our enemy while he is making a mistake. Enemies however usually know something about us that the closer ones don't, and we are annoyed, as Nietzsche puts it, if they disclose to the world this that they have seen from the other angle. We are likewise annoyed if someone enhances greatly one of our qualities, for we feel like they are neglecting the others. Even if someone says what we like to hear about us, which is usually far from what they say, for we have this ego-inflated vision about us, we are suddenly demanded to behave like that, we have this extra pressure. For people who don't have contact with us, and thus are much less biased in their assertions, we find them annoying for their description feels reductive, all too objective. We also, because again of our ego, hate when we are said to be alike someone else. We ask who is it that we look like, and we always disagree. This realization is insane. That those that become masters at one subject probably are amateurs at all others, and yet are perceived as the reverse. Wow. The specialization of modern human is thus incentivized, range is not appreciated if not as a single thing in itself. The champions are these that mastered the subject. We think that because they can become great at something that they are great in general. This gives an advantage in low profiling on that which is becoming the masters of many things, if you can see through it and not give a fuck about this perception. The era of the highlight. Who you like and who you dislike, your acquaintances, distract the conversation from ever finding ground to connect rightly. No, don't make friends with someone because of other people, some you've never even known. Winning people in conversation through putting the ball up there to be dunked. Allow them the opportunity to be funny and great, and they will follow you.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Socialism, Justice, and the Power of the Oppressed
Philosophy.content: That socialism doesn't pose a problem of justice, but only of power. A problem of redistribution of power. We have nothing to say about the justice of the rise of the oppressed against their oppressors, which have made a business out of putting them down for quite some time in most cases. This rise however puts the first injustice part in question when the oppressed find a means of supercompensation to release their long-held anger onto the world, when they believe they now have the right to be put on top for some time as dividend of their downward ways. When the war they are fighting transgresses the oppressors. When they believe themselves as victims of the world, as fighting for their freedom, even if there are no oppressors to be seen. These figure the collective psychological consequences of poor treatment in stages of development, and can manifest as individual issues. This expansion of the war they fight into the neutral territories backfire, for a well-deserved hand was given but they are trying to take the whole arm. To blame far descendants of the oppressors is unjust. Justice has no past, no record of accumulating credit. Justice is about the now. There is no place for vengeance in a just present. Most importantly, we gotta move on to bigger issues. It's shameful to lose precious time on air, at home, at dinner, talking about woke, discussing these things that shouldn't be a thing in the first place. It's a problem that we created for ourselves, and how far can we really get by "solving" it?

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: The Ugly Soul, Unfiltered Art, and Continuous Becoming
Philosophy.content: FROM ANOTHER bus travel: That one would be limiting art too severely were him to reject the art of the "ugly soul", Nietzsche writes. That only the balanced, morally sound, achieved person doing art is boring, it's not spontaneous enough (wisdom of crowds I here add), it lacks in content, it lacks in authenticity, for it comes from a place of yes-saying, yes-doing, a place of wanting to say the right thing, a place of a lot to lose, and the censures implied by this status transgress into the affected freedom of thought. The erratic, from error, way, the nothing to lose attitude, it gives you the advantage of openness. The attitude that no matter how far you've gone, you are that beginner still, that student still, only transformed in time not in mind, that Poatan mindset that you are recurrently fighting for the championship, even if you already have it. And in writing, journalling, you understand that you come to think that at present you are a work in progress, and in the past you are shit. This is for the same reason that when you read others' material you find it boring if their points are already solved in your head. The only thought worth thinking is that for which you have to fight for. Same thing happens in dating, you don't want to understand too plainly the other individual, you don't want to feel like they are just a phase of you in the past. And The ugly part is the one that you remember. The one where you were sailing at rock bottom yet knowing you would fly, nonetheless exposing yourself to that moisty uncomfortable, bed-sleeping, feeling (falling to bed like a baby when you are working hard), which is facilitated by a hanging motion, a giving up action (as I have here and continue to lay out the philosophy of sleep.) The illusion of inspiration, as opposed to iteration of shit, and careful selection and mixing. Letting it out at once because you have been no fapping for long. Then one has the choice, to stay true to his art, even if no one is dancing to its sound. That art proceeds from the ignorance of the interior, and for that reason isn't reserved for physicists and philosophers. The self distancing of the status of genius, so that we don't have to compete within it, don't have to stand up to its height from our bottoms, for it descended from the "stars". "Perfect is admired. What is in evolution is underestimated." The era of the highlight. I realize that then the "to search" section of my whatever this is called, maybe log of ideas, is actually to search inside me. I know what I want to hear and I just have to let it come through, no need to get approval from others in the internet wherever. Maybe that's why it seems that when you tell people you are evolving in some fashion they are like "Yeah, sure, whatever", it is seen as an excuse. But then when you come anew, when everything you said would do you have done, when the chips have fallen your way, there is a sudden 10-fold boost in respect for you. Don't assume they will just respect just cause you know them for a long time and what not, you gotta show them. That willingness to come back to blank state (estaca zero), to learn once more.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: The Master of All Powers and The Silence of Truth
Philosophy.content: That a single individual can hold eye to culture, that by holding, by petting, by controlling all powers the like of a great musician, great artist, great man of science, great athlete, great anything, has one got it in him to quiet the storm, to defuse the explosives coming on all directions, commanding the unleashing power of each of himself, does one become ultimate. One has built the overarching edifice of culture above him. Here one too, with the wisdom of crowds, can read many books at the same time, and yet if he holds all these thoughts, if he just endures the pushing and pulling from all sides, if he still finds the courage to be one, he thy one be. There's the edifice above him, rising with the more one can stuff inside without coming apart in a blowing of spirit. One can thus reach the next handle, one can thus see the handles that lie high, one now is given the power of the connections on the graph, not just the nodes. Does one have the power to shut it all down, and yet to hear its fullest noises, what can't this man not express himself. In the limit once again, one would reach everything, everywhere all at once. What comes before it however, is the greatest silence ever assimilated by the like subjects. A silence not merely of sound, for one has internal blood rushing and flushing, not even merely of senses it shall be, not merely enough of thought, for one is aware, not just a collapse of space and time, nor of unconsciousness, or guilt, or protruding self-analyzing alternatives of actions, nor the dimming of every last light, nor the shutting down of computation in the meta-knows-everything embedded space of possibility. Rather a stillness of truth, if one must call it that. A fixing of gaze defying all as of yet conceived. That those who went can't find the way to gather up the words to speak it, for they shoot too low, for they make it unjust, corrupt, inauthentic, the killer of depth. The reluctance to come back, the realization of purpose born out of the go past the point of no return, as much as past the escape velocity, acceleration, and all the infinite therein entails. The readiness to sweat out all the machinery synched into one great punch, the promise of alignment written into one singular output. Untamed. Undone. The infinite compressed into the unfillable. The intertwining of the void with oneself and beyond. The unrepeatable, the relentlessly fresh, the thing that is to finite what finite is to permanent, the decision boiled down to the flip of a bit, the yes or no game of the all-untimely crudeness that nature presents forward, and presents with the greatest fiber of reality. The physically unrememberable yet omnipresent. What perhaps we forget as we rise into the desert of existence, and what we taste as we prepare to be engulfed into the eternal rest. One smooth movement is all there ever was.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Bound vs Free Spirits and the Pain of Clarity
Philosophy.content: That the free spirit devises reasons for his habits, and the reason of the bound spirit is his habits. The latter acquires them, while the former searches, be it for good or bad. One may be bound to tradition, to his country, to his time. When he accepts the imposition on him of what he is he is acting out of faith. Religion is basal for bound spirits, for it gives them all the things to adopt. Not much thinking beyond that required. Nietzsche writes what I had in my head for a long long long time, or at least it illuminates me the way to this thought. That the free spirit has many more options which makes him less certain, less reliable, less willing to accept his role, cause he has none. He has no limits, hence unbounded. That is an advantage for the bound spirit, for he can know what he must do, he can choose how far he pushes himself, he can choose the finish line, get there, have purpose, achieve it and be happy. He knows himself. He has the courage enough to describe himself, to see his dream as a finite thing, to know the path ahead. To know himself, his strengths, weaknesses. So he is a spirit of action, he has great confidence in doing his thing and no more. He has great confidence to know what he wins and admit defeat where he loses. Very real he is. He can go tryhard for tests, leave his parents happy, and then when it's vacation he can enjoy his vacation too, as if all of a sudden he wasn't a beast at what he does. This confuses the hell out of me, for you see someone who you think also has that vision of greatness yet doing all this other stuff on the side that makes you question their mentality. That's where we are different. Yet one who has the power of this has to act the other way so as to it to make sense in the end. You could waste your entire life thinking greatly and doing pretty much nothing. Being the jack of all traits yet the master of nothing. Being vaguely great. But the potential is diminished, it is first principles vs zero principles. The bound spirit can win the battle that he knows he can win. The free spirit can win the battle of the unknown, the battle that he doesn't know he is in. And so you see a lot of good people at what they do, but then you realize their ambition doesn't multiply exponentially exceed that, you are surprised that they don't consider going all the way through. They make a plan for themselves and if they do what they gotta do then they can also enjoy the cake at the end of it. They have clarity. They have a ranking of things all with finite points. The free spirit may have one thing with infinite points, one thing he could give everything away for, and so he is not constrained, he can forget the other things, making a lot of things contingent. The middle of the path, Nietzsche calls it.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: The Middle of History and The Loss of Spiritual War
Philosophy.content: There is this thing that I haven't considered, maybe geniuses are a transitory thing in society, as in certain phases of our evolution they may not be there, meanwhile I thought it was natural they existed throughout. Things are indeed transitory, there will never again be, or at least there isn't no more, that super hard and dense religious feeling. He then suggests that in this period of middle of history, which I think it always seems, since there will always be so much to figure out, there is this imperfectedness and all this turmoil of feelings, we are at the peak of chaos, disorder through the increase in entropy. Maybe if society pulls through and is sustainable in some form for a long long time, then there may not be material for thought, no drive, no hunger of more knowledge. No purpose. "We are the middlemen of history. Our great war is a spiritual war. Our great depression is our lives" (Fight Club). We don't have this kind of purpose no more, to put our lives on the line on war for our people. We've lost that into the softness of the now. Maybe further advances will make pointless the fight to get more money or whatever. Equilibria will be reached, and what that implies is stagnation of big trajectory altering events. There are no great wars no more. Maybe history will cease to make sense writing, maybe time will cease to make a difference. Everything would just come and go, again and again. Maybe then one could have the freedom to choose to recreate the conditions of a fargone time of history, like once I speculated that we may end, coming back to the trees and the hunts in the caves, but here you could choose between many versions, and feel like you have something to fight for. This also happens at our life's scale.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Equilibrium, Highs and Lows, and The Search for Truth
Philosophy.content: If we reach the equilibrium, as in stability on all fronts, our life is good but doesn't have that spice, and looking back, that chaos was the essence, that which we did take for granted even tho it was the best of life. When you admit made it and live like so, now the job is done, there is some sadness to it, as you don't have those problems no more, don't have that urgency and worry no more, don't have doubt that it will all work out. But when you are living on the limit and it pays off, oh boy will it feel good down the line. I think I'd rather prefer to ever play this game of reaching highs and appreciating that yes, but the next day you wake up and you are back to zero. Once again longevity factors into this, if you are gonna live a long time then quitting from progressing midway feels dumb. Argues that the pleasure in science is for those who do it, not the ones learning the result, and that going forward. So I say going forward, more results to learn, harder to give new results, and humans, to regain pleasure, will resort to error, illusion, fantasy. Philosophy and poetry will become usual, as one doesn't have such a learning curve. That the advantage of having that science background is the ability not the knowledge or results, but he "learns how to gain his purpose purposefully". He expands this science pleasure thing further: the results of science then, and still now, give us curiosity for their beauty, but maybe it's because we are in the early stages. Maybe one day this beautiful girl that we chase may become elder, maybe the results will be boring, and then, without the pleasure, how will one find in himself the will to push further, when only truth is left. And yet truth as the most penetrating of all richnesses. It's rare, it's valuable. Truth has no word to say on the appearance, or the abstract things we made up for ourselves. Truth subsides all that, truth breaks those all barriers. A man in search for truth doesn't move a single hair when confronted with wit, Nietzsche says, or seriousness, or professions.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Fantasies, Incompleteness, and Underestimated Evolution
Philosophy.content: Fantasies thrive on size, they feed off of it, they are but it. One builds himself that space for travel does one not choose to collapse reality. Nietzsche calls it the incomplete as the effective. Fantasy needs room to work it, so not realized thought, not realized philosophy, not realized action, not realized being, gives it that room to maneuver. Since it isn't closed, one can make a branch out of it, one can build from there. The exercise of completing the phrase, in summary, is the little game that the poet plays when he wants more investment, more attachment, more attention, more interaction, by the reader. Those poets should too take the opportunities they get to make their presence and their thought prohibited, restricted, limited, as to create the effect of "you want what you can't have". Even in books, when I get to those last 10 pages is when I actually start paying diligent attention to what the author has to say. Nietzsche has amazing thoughts, but it's me who's reading it. From evolution being underestimated results that you must finish what you started, can't expect others to close it for you, even if you are super close to achieving, others may reject what you have. I'm sure had Einstein left his reasoning for general relativity stall at a certain point, say 1913, no one would have finished it, for it presented such a learning curve, to fully adopt the idea of another human and make it your own when you know it isn't.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Abstract Art, Moral Stagnation, and Originality
Philosophy.content: The phenom of modern abstract art, wherein what is has little place in people's minds, rather what means becomes the subject. The uglier is presented, there is less sensitivity to noise, and distraction, one looks through, one tries to read between lines. In music and paintings points out Nietzsche, says that the ear and the eye have become more intellectual. "The world is uglier than ever, but it means a more beautiful world than ever existed." That we are convinced by out there morals to applaud the mysterious and reject the objective. People have become so good that now to get better one has to come up with higher abstract standards. Now the purely good is seen as predictable, dull, so to mask the lack of talent one comes up with new measures, but very vague ones, so that one does not risk them not stacking up. One refuges in the new measures to not lose progress, originality. Abstract art because no one gets it. Both the talented artist and the abstract artist have intention, but the former gives arguments too clear for why his should be recognized, whereas the latter tries to instill in the public's eye that his intention far exceeds what is objective, sensible, so over the top that mere painting mere blush, mere physical things can't explain. So one has to travel to a higher sense higher dimension to find the meaning. That's exactly why one doesn't sense that art. Hard to argue it has physical existence. You have doubts to distinguish a human from a philosophical zombie.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Imposing Narratives and the Mystery of Art
Philosophy.content: That religious worship is a way of directing nature, enveloping it in a human narrative, while with physics it's about submitting to its order, less those attempts of injecting beauty into the narrative, which get us farther from the reality it has presented us. This fun game is played bouncing between imposing and receiving. We want to impose cause vanity, and receive cause ego. The masters become slaves. Says that art strives to be felt as an instantaneous self-evident piece of truth, in that it is an imposition. The artist rather have his art seen as a gift of god. This I wrote as art not wanting to be explained, and the more mysterious connotations and untied branches it leaves, the more room for inspiration on the viewer. Love and art and these abstract things we say are human, are in this very sense human cause they are fantasies, fed by the imagination of those who fall in them traps. Letting people put their inventiveness to fit their own selves, so that a masterpiece is multiple, plural. It is felt by everyone as they wish to feel it, which is to say as they are mistaken to feel it. Letting them earn their own art, cause people have egos man. If you just produce something that no one can do, so that a feeling of separation, an eternal gap to the viewer is felt, only an acknowledgment is given, no investment is made, so they are not committed to fight by your side. Rather, if you give them enough trace to follow, and if You WIN them, that means you will convince them of that which they want to be convinced. They did the work and the adjoining of the art with the observer is thus made. A bond is created. If your art lets people as far away from solving the puzzle as they already are, they won't credit your art even if they achieve truth. Make everyone feel like they are great, and that will make you great. They will make you great. And those elements of mystique, that which people can't know, that's what they want. If you suddenly died after drawing this your last painting, suddenly people will pay due to watch it. If only people thought you would die in 5 minutes, they would hear for once, not listen. If people think they can access your way then they are that much less interested in what you gave out of that way. But if people don't understand how you can do something, even though they simply may not want to, suddenly you are given interest the most when compared to those living the same life as you. Dead authors, you cannot access their time nor them. Chess champions, you cannot access how their greatness arose, so you fantasize about you not being able to and them having this amazing secret. But if only you saw some plays, and learn some theory, then the fog would collapse, for it has zero measure. In the mind it expands the more you are afraid of it, the more you avoid it. It feeds off that in the unconscious realm, but if only you gathered up the balls to look at it dead in the eyes, you would lose this admiration. That's why they say don't meet your heroes, for you will lose hope that you can emancipate yourself from your nature, when you see that those you thought did are as much dirty bastards as you when you started looking up to them, or rather looking down to the ground when talking to them. Heroes are all matter of fantasy.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: The Disappointment of the Known and Leaps of Faith
Philosophy.content: That's the fun thing. These guys doing the great stuff are the ones who least know about it. The fans are the ones who know all the stats, and have narrated the greatness of the protagonists, have taken the time to polish their thought on how everything was meant, they elevate the moments, and elevate with them the people who know nothing about it. Like me with Bolt. I know so much better than him why he is so great. That's why people are even bored if you just stand there admired, they lose interest in you, little fanboy. People in essence salivate for discontinuity. They mirage the inflexion point in their lives, the take-off zone. Then they are surprised no music started playing when they got there. They still have to eat, shit, like when they thought they were something the less. I mean what did you expect when you put someone so far out there of course they are going to disappoint you, cause there's nowhere to go but down. And as much as you don't want to admit it, you don't want the path ahead to be predictable, you don't want to win predictably, for I'd argue that would be total waste of time, even if you ended up out there. Without free will, it wouldn't have motive for joy. Cause if it's predictably winnable, then you know you can have it. Wins that matter come when you take that leap of faith, when you only along the way and sometimes only after winning learn that you will win. When you know you will do something you are just doing it for completion. Your mind has wandered away to the next thing. Cause how sad it is to live without testing the limits, without knowing what you could have done. What you already know you can do already is on that list, chasing it won't change you. Things new are the ones where you gain velocity, beforehand you were only moving forward, new things are where you gain acceleration, new thoughts are where you gain jerk. New being is where you gain movement.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Fake It Till You Make It and Belief
Philosophy.content: "How seeming becomes being". Here Nietzsche lays down his account of fake it til you make it. Says that in all deceptions the deceptor has to develop belief in his lie, and only when that belief overcomes him, I say: when he is not conscient of his belief, that's when he deceives and it's that belief that passes on to the audience. He is being honest, that's the point, the point is that his lie has fooled himself. And that's why you can believe in him easier. And this is great in that it gives you power to lie to yourself on purpose to believe something. But it also means you can be thinking that you don't believe in something around you, but maybe ya do. Maybe the day you saw it first time ya didn't, but now you've learned to believe in it, even if you don't allow yourself to know that. And this we know, we humans adapt to things pretty fucking smoothly considering things. We think we own much more of our identity than perhaps we'd like to admit. And this is why it is absolutely important, or so they say, to choose wisely who you are around. Maybe you were different, and maybe there's still different inside you, but being with the others perhaps slowed that down, perhaps you think you are not one of them but are in the process of becoming. Know thyself. Keep thyself knowing thyself. Perhaps everytime we hear something we get closer to believing it. Perhaps that's why there are self-assuring mantras and what not. But think about it. Everytime someone says something you get closer to believing said thing. "Become vocal with it, and it will happen" - Connor McMystic. And I mean the implications of this are huge. Like you really do create your reality. Imagine knowing this and what you could be as a result of faking it. It may not be a joke that I say that this is the most important thing you will hear. I mean this can be life changing. Join this to the "you do what you believe", like that guy that got the wrong SAT score and believed he was great and became a millionaire. Cheat code. Mind washing is real man.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Confidence, Worship, and the Mass Hallucination
Philosophy.content: "For men will believe something is true, if it is evident that others believe in it firmly." It's sort of cause of the law of least action, at the end of the day you hear so much shit you just gotta believe, it isn't practical to go find counterarguments to everything. Confidence as a trait is then so attractive. It's biased. Even if you don't want you end up believing an unshakably confident person, it's actually unfair. Girls say they don't want the cocky sort of type but they can't help but fall for it sometimes, that's why they hate it. The level of absurdity in this world. "How can you deny him?" Hallucinating into reality. Dreaming into reality. I mean saying it is just a step above dreaming. You do get closer to reaching your dreams by dreaming them. And visualization comes into the picture, makes you more fluent with it. Why does praying work? Makes you more vocal with it. Makes something external created internally, which I call Jesus, believe in you too. Simulate the conversations, hear his confidence in you and surer you will be. That nobody understands what is being built literally or figuratively. Like the bomb. For the conscience of a man on such a big thing would kill him. So bring thousands of people together, so that their wrongdoing is done together, so that they are dependent on each other, trusting one another with good intentions on their path to hell. For we are seeing only one bone of the beast we create, and thus feel at peace with the final result. Put a man alone and he hardly can escalate malice. Put an infinitude of them and you shall have built a beast.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Suicide, Power, and the Wrecker
Philosophy.content: That suicide may come as more natural, empowering, accountable, just, honorous. Suicide as in deciding to end life, not give up on it. Get out on your own terms. But no Hell no I ain't doing that. But could life get instantly more meaningful if you accepted your power to end it? You could believe you were alive for as long as you wanted, alive for when you wanted. Each has as much right as his power is worth. This comes with a notion I love, that of the wrecker. Just getting and doing something no one imagined breaking rules conventions, and doing it all in a confident innocence and ignorance. I must become a wrecker. The dichotomy of the executor and the commander, that one is protected by the lack thereof imagination in what he commands, and that the other is saved by the lack of responsibility in what he realizes. The latter not so judged by contrast, cause people think people have choices, and by contrast his wasn't very there. The former not so judged by committing a mistake, he didn't have reality first hand to help him out of a trap. Society failed him. As for the latter, he failed society. And so is amplified, and at the same time slowed down, made less fluid, more viscous yet more certain, the man who does it all. The man who brings forth a complete idea. Not dependent on other's mistakes he has firm grip on what happens and on what he does. In other words, he has choice. This is the kryptonite of capitalism specialization. That big errors can propagate unseemingly, infiltrating through the halls, like a spider crawling unnoticed. That insanity can go wild, exponential. When you have the openness to see your idea, the balls to put it into language, then when you fancy do it, you will most likely see the shit you have created. Cause everybody gangsta until they responsible. Everybody has great ideas until their life depends on them.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: Angst and the Middle of the Path
Philosophy.content: And that the poet knows how to comfort himself, he that dares not escape, he that willingly stands still, open to the tsunami and the sandstorm of angst. Kierkegaard-like angst, that stops one and makes him fight for action. Fight to make a move just to stay in the game. This is where I've spent some time, but I've come to realize that I need escape from escapism, I have to seize. Either I'll make it or I'll die crazy, and hopefully I'm not already in the process of dying crazy. We are mostly some bastards who can't help but to stay in their lane. And most of all we can't help but to keep moving. That one grows disdain to his motives does one think about it logically, one ought to roll through life. "Like cameos, praising them because they cannot love them, and quick to admire them but even quicker to run away." In this state live a lot of famous people in people's minds. Just the admiration, that vague notion, that blush of something, yet just a title. How many great philosophers have created that space in my head, yet not to be read ever? How can one say he admires Nietzsche when everything one has is an impression. A feeling with null measure in time where one got a glimpse, yet was coward enough to let it at that, perhaps in the attempt to not let go of admiring him, not risking losing interest. This way one can throw the ocasional "oh yea Nietzsche that guy is tough man, respect". It is easy though to let them crawl into your head unaffected. It is feeling part of the collective. The collective that talks about something only the individual can know.

Philosophy.title: Human, All Too Human: The Super Animal and the Rejection of Morality
Philosophy.content: "The super animal. The beast in us wants to be lied to". Says that morality was in a sense created to keep us from suffering from the fact that there is no such thing as morality. Rather it seems to be that we want to lie to our beast, so we don't awaken it. The beast I was cries on the road. I left it there to come be who I am become. Morality, the suggestion presents itself, and this a reason people say Nietzsche is dangerous, morality isn't instinctive, morality is limiting, as we know, but limiting as in not freeing. Morality the sort of thing to leave us at home with the people we know, and not explore the forest from which a beast wanders in the shadows, a beast which we have come to disown, to disinherit, to unknow. "These civilized people, they'll eat each other". And so Nietzsche comes with this concept of an error in our progress, that morality was an error, a dissimulation, just like religion and art, all attempts of the human to justify having caged the beast, all attempts of the human to separate himself from land which he has ceased to touch and feel. He wears the most protective shoes, he only touches that of his kind, an attempt of cutting the roots, an unacceptance of what we came from. A presumption on what we have become.
