Life.content: Not falling pray to one's own story, that one thus shouldn't preach, is in part about accepting the extreme case that all that one did was voluntary and not needed. That all the suffering one put oneself through was voluntary. That all you did it for was for the joy of it, accept this and all other reasons be yours. Giving away, painfully, that your way is no more useful, it was only useful to you in your way. This brings exclusivity. Computational irreducibility in that others can't skip through their journey by knowing yours. Yours is yours. heros don't tell their story. They can put it out there even if it makes them feel better, but there will be no ears.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Knowing the Way Only at the End
Life.content: It is quite ironic that only at the end of the climb does he know exactly how to climb and did what he did. Only when he has much to share does he not have much to do. he doesn't realize he is distancing himself from hero as he remembers his tale. Hero in reality was he that made the way unknowingly, discovered the way. The hero makes itself. He can hope that the structure that has taken so much out of him to be produced to get him somewhere can serve as map to someone else, not realizing it amounts to nothing. His vision amounts to nothing, and can only be as good as the places he got with it.

Life.title: What is Life With Mind and Matter and Autobiographical Sketches- The World That Rewards Shallowness
Life.content: This world at times rewards shallowness and width, for conversations don't usually get to the point where you show depth, rather a lot of conversations are best suited for those who know a lot but don't really know it. Books are read on their covers, their titles, but do you actually know about the thing you know exist? Its cool to know that hitler wrote a book, do you actually know what its about, have you read it? Sometimes we get too fixated on having meta knowledge without actually diving into content.

Life.title: The Champion Has a Name
Life.content: Quotes and questions there is a certain pain, but to me it goes away very fast and only rarely comes about, about being the only one. About seeing most around me making the wrong decisions, not understanding deeper things. There has never been none and there is only one. Me. The champion has a name baby. Not but now in seriousness, this is an experiment on human potential, this is a self actualization of the overhuman. I carry this message. I came so humanity understands itself, so the universe understands itself, so I understand myself. Just from a very projected to the ground point of view, no one out there has the grip like I do on knowledge and mindset, no one has as many doors open as me. There is no world expert at the intersection of many fields, let alone those that don't exist.

Life.title: On the Unexplored and Untamed
Life.content: Quotes and questions- there is this certain conspiracy and camaraderie and partner in crime vibe of you and the stars, you and your play. It isn't filmed or written down, just yours. Cause we get too caught up in what human built, we live inside that human's life, not to realize that there are still things impenetrable to all other humans. You and the world, not you and this of the humans thing. There is still untamed, there is still unexplored, you can step at any time into places no other will know.

Life.title: On Social Mirroring II
Life.content: Quotes and questions. so funny the effect of looking at people. If they look at you for example in a train you look away, and collective movement is a thing, if you all of a sudden move people will follow that and adjust themselves too.

Life.title: On Evolution and Competition
Life.content: Quotes and questions think of someone you know and see their evolution, and do not reject that they've changed, do not regret that they've gotten severely better. See the argument there is to make that they are winning in life against you. Confront that possibility for everyone, as if you are playing by yourself against the world. And the battle never ends. Don't think just because you think you won back then with something that you have the right to not maintain and improve your situation. The fairness of life comes in the absoluteness of the moment, the inexistence of anything beyond that. The past is only your servant, any win either propels you forward or else does nothing.

Life.title: On the Duality of Speed and Depth
Life.content: Quotes and questions- I have these two strong inclinations that yet seem very exclusive to each other. One the efficiency ultra high bandwidth maximizing the speed of my life and consuming more information being exposed to more through information than anyone out there, having seen all the greats and been with them, with their mindsets. And yet the other view of maximum depth and thus no exposure, being only a matter of one's thought, not seeing all thoughts out there. Slow and meaningful. I have been developing them separately, but it remains to be seen how I will crystallize them into something so much bigger.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Preserving the Youth of the Spirit
Life.content: I think its way closer than everyone thinks in terms of what you can preserve young, people think spirit is the only one and still lose it

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Longevity and the Late Bloomer
Life.content: Understand too that if you are going to have more longevity, you are going to be a late bloomer, all will be increasing the distance to you until age starts working against them. It's a long term game. The most ripe fruit takes the most time being prepped.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Surprise Effect of Hidden Knowledge
Life.content: What I was saying about you not giving a damn what others think of you and so not trying to show you know, being content with only you understanding yourself and your worth. This it brings the surprise effect when you actually are asked for the opportunity to show your knowledge in a certain specialty of yours. Like me here and there actually getting excited talking about training or whatever to others.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Relative Aging of Spirit and Body
Life.content: The interplay of staying young till late yet aging through some of the victories and truths of youth. Changing in body but remaining in spirit. Changing in dress but staying in body. Nietzsche says in some the heart ages in others the spirit. And this is one hell of a truth people don't take into account, the aging process of each thing that makes one up. Bryan johnson has unlocked the relative aging of all cells, but one ought to go further and see how certain jokes are relative to thoughts relative to how your maturity ages versus how your fashion ages and how your face ages and how your physical condition ages and how your spirit and beliefs age. you want to evolve, not age, for as long as possible. Never in final form. The natural tendency is alignment of all ages, it's a stable equilibrium. If your spirit is younger than your body, you might go a little too harsh doing sport with your son and end up on the sidelines, effectively aging your body. This perhaps an argument for generality entity, being an all purpose agent. Attacking everything. This I know and do and few do and know.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Endurance and Repetitive Motions
Life.content: And man I genuinely believe I could easily get through that chinese torture of the perpetuous drop of water, I think I'd just be chilling. But there is such a thing as going crazy over these unusual repetitive motions, your brain can actually get freaked out. I think at least I have more endurance in this aspect than most.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Building Yourself Before Overcoming
Life.content: "Over and beyond yourself shall you build. But first you must be built yourself." to prolong and accept as if willing to stop all your life for you, through means of children, asks of you that you have yourself dominated. Now you have accountability, now you can't afford mistakes. All that is to be done is done and all is said too. Are you ready to call it? Well don't if its going to be you and your child against the world, especially if it is against parts of you that you are fighting with. "not only onward shall you propagate onto, but upward!". Overcoming oneself before so then you can reach farther. The higher the leaves are dropped from the tree you have grown to love, the farther they reach, the farther you see.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Accepting the Passage of Time
Life.content: Further accept the passage of time. Here in the train I haven't checked time in a minute, I have no clue, and I'd rather keep it that way. Time can't work against you, rather it works for you. I have accepted staying here forever and just doing this. One must imagine Sisyphus happy. Don't even know if I'm travelling forwards or backwards

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Fluidity of Movement and Unfazedness
Life.content: Remember, peace of mind brings fluidity of movement. Have it sorted out, know the direction and achieve peace through low level thinking. and I'm telling you, if the sound of the subway causes you anger, if you can't take that hit, if hunger makes you lose intention and volition, then my man you are not even playing at the right game yet. You still are battling against yourself. First align yourself, then make the world align to you. And I was guilty of that at some point, by letting small losses that don't matter take me down and make me not understand that I have to be the best, I am. If you let anything external get to you, fix that. Nothing can stop me I'm all the way up. Thus one develops this peace of being with everything external, an unfazedness regarding everything it may bring. You are unreactive to all the fake battles it suggests to you. You know what the real battles are, you know when you should panic and not. The naturality of the cat, unfazed to everything yet knows when its time to go ham. Not afraid of the dark, the last to move, but the first to move the fastest when given enough evidence, the first to get out. Luck takes work. Takes preparation. What stoicism looks like when it isn't covered by all the media spam wording and connotation and glamour vision. Eventually you become virtually undefeatable, there is nothing outside of you that can hurt you. Comfort becomes a luxury, and it can't pollute your heart. In these moments you've seen you climbed, and despite also doing a lot of bullshit, you climbed, this gives you a certainty that you will climb so much more. I am Inevitable. Perhaps because one gets that extra push after seeing others give up. Seeing others getting frustrated gives you energy. Seeing others give up when I ran cross country and endurance events, seeing I won, that gave such an extra push. And a big fat one'- sleepiness. This one is a motherfucker, I lost control on it sometimes

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Freedom of Not Giving a Damn
Life.content: Its above all a peace and rest as in the opposite of unrest to not give a damn what others think and especially not caring if they recognize your worth. Short story, they won't period. But not giving a f about that allows you to win so much more, you are not dependent on that vision that others have of you. It lets you keep moving forward through island and sea, through storm and sunset, through hell and paradise and back. all of a sudden you come back to respecting yourself so much more and valuing your time so much more. being able to vibe to a music with no headphones and through the sound of the train.

Life.title: On Small Worries and the Meritocracy of Competence
Life.content: still quotes and questions: it is quite pathetic too that small worries take over the lives of people. That the more, people let themselves be controlled and submitted by impulses small little impulses. Like hunger. Chill, I know I'll eat when I get home, so this signal my body is sending is to be ignored. Don't let it stop you. Have control over, and now you are free to fly. Now you can focus on the real bigger things that can throw you off. Now you can come back and join that internal locus of control that you have. That deep self that you know you can't avoid, now you can again connect with him and get aligned. fame alone isn't something one wants. It is only constricting. If there was such a thing as just being famous, the hell out with it. You couldn't go by any way, seas of people would swarm you, you'd have the private means you may say, but that is not fame, money we associate with fame, yet distinct they remain. Power we associate with fame, goodness of being, skill, competence, no. the brutal meritocracy of competence Tate I heard say he believes in, and the hell it is true. The hell it is true. It gives the absolute most power and control to us, thus we believe it thus its true, it's the biggest bang for buck belief, it favours you the most, its a fixed point. Investing in your self type of belief.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Men in the Matrix
Life.content: Quite saddening too to look at grown ass men still in the matrix.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Building the Impregnable Identity
Life.content: Again the process of rebuilding oneself. To build the most impregnable identity, unshakable force of nature within you, you can't wait for it to get destroyed out there. You battle test it, you put your beliefs and wills out there through the roughest conditions, you bombard until only the strongest survive, until the most solid foundations let themselves out to be seen. You dress down all into the bare body which you carry with you into the next battle. Too much weight wears you, you must stick with the simplest yet most absolute bases before you start building on top. You thus have the most high density of quality robust trustworthy alignment within. Your troops, absolutely loyal to you. You try to kill these things which you have on your side, yet to strengthen the deserved few.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Sensing Uncomprehended Strength
Life.content: Nietzsche, that there is such a thing as sensing strength but not being able to comprehend it. I feel like this we fear, when we sense strength and we comprehend it after, it diminishes it. We don't fear our place in the will to power wheel. Our ego can sleep rested. But if we see strength where we can't understand it. Then we run, then we either deal with it, and let it become our strength or we deny it, we push it further down, slimming down yet expanding the bubble size of the cookie monster. It is power thus to have unseen strength, to have uncomprehended strength. dude why people admit they are tired. This is a nonsense I cannot tolerate by hearing and not writing no more. Like what. Unbelievable few understand this. Actually ridiculously unbelievable, and perhaps it is children who better grasp the concept. I don't see a world where it making sense. But yeah back on topic,

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- On Knowing Your Power
Life.content: That talking contrary to what you don't know is a greater lie than contrary to what you know. This Nietzsche says. for in the first one you fool yourself, in the latter you betray yourself. You don't yet see what you are, what you will be, the extent of your empire without limits, so talking about it as if you know it is failing oneself. Tens de conhecer o teu poder. What a shame if you get out of this place without knowing your true power, perhaps because you were afraid to unleash it, perhaps you let it slip. But not coming to see your final form, whether physical, which Socrates pointed out, whether spiritually mentally lifely, is failing yourself. Perhaps one of the big questions is what can you be.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Being the Strength, Not Just Having It
Life.content: Not just having a strength, not just having a right, not just having a cause and having a why, but being the strength and the right, and the cause and the why.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Becoming What You Sought to Destroy
Life.content: This thing that adults and cooler teenagers who are living their lives to the fullest get to stay up till late, doing cool people stuff, this wave is now through me, and I am reaching the end of the ride. I confessed not to become this, yet see myself become the thing I sought to destroy, behold, now I present to you the overhuman, the overme. Coming back to truths stronger.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Freedom from Desire and the Champion's Ease
Life.content: Becoming free from them gives you this chill, not replicable by those who visually are holding tension, after battling out with their will, whether about eating that cheesecake, whether to get on studying, see they have pressure on themselves to work, to do what they are supposed to do. You? You just know wholeheartedly you will. You have gone through all those battles, you are now freed from desire. It's refreshing too for people to see champions that seem to take stuff so lightly and value them so much.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Enemies Within, Enemies That Break You Anew
Life.content: That yet your enemies are within you, part of you, symbiotic. Thus they break you into something new. That you should in a sense enjoy the ride as you have it, ride on what's going for you, for war and enemies will eventually bring about another. And so obedience to your command your twinning thought, applying and raising its hand, touring it around your life. Not here for a long time, here for a great time.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- A Slow Suicide Called Life
Life.content: A slow suicide they call life, Nietzsche says, yap there is a lot of losing, but you can only say that do you do what you know you have to do to be the winner you know you are. On a whole other level.

Life.title: The Selfish Gene: Memes, Dunning-Kruger, and the Illusion of Knowledge
Life.content: Em relação aos memes e em conjunção com o Dunning-Kruger effect, parece que quando ouvimos a primeira opinião sobre um assunto, como sabemos que não sabemos, ouvimo-la bem, interiorizamo-la e depois pensamos que sabemos alguma coisa sobre o assunto. Quando estamos a falar depois com alguém, damos por nós algumas vezes a literalmente repetir o que nos foi dito. E o engraçado é que a mudança é gradual. Primeiro dizemos "disseram-me que..." (sem referir quem porque ou isso tiraria autoridade ou a pessoa com quem estamos agora a falar não conhece aquele que nos disse, o que tiraria autoridade) porque temos sempre a mania de que aquilo que conhecemos é melhor que aquilo que os outros conhecem, sem sequer perceber que os outros estão na mesma situação que nós. Vivemos todos algo contentes por termos melhores experiências que os outros, e no entanto é difícil perceber que estamos todos nesta dança de ilusões sem notar nisso. Mas acho que nem valia a pena notar nisso porque sabemos que é completamente subjetivo e só daria confusão. Entra muito nesta parte da rivalidade a game theory, e o Nash equilibrium.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- War as the Way of the Warrior
Life.content: In order to seek new wars of ideas, new wars of concepts and ways of being I add ; one loves times of peace, but the short lived, not the long. Seek greater enemies and diversify. You will increase your level. You are the warrior the more wars you put yourself, the more you fight through. Nietzsche says it is war that makes causes and not causes that make wars. Every hope to become rigorous belief must be battle tested, must be forged in the midst of wars with everything it opposes, and everything you already know and are, so that its pure form may be after compression, at last distilled into you, however little of substance it had in the first place. You must be battle tested to be immovable out there. You must have crossed the 7 layers of hell and come back to be still in soul, unbreakable in spirit. Let victory, not rest be your peace. Let not peace wander around too long. In fact victory lasts not very long at all. War is the way the lifestyle of the warrior. Embracing silent war, silent wins. That perhaps it takes everything out of you to keep the war, to get the wins, and even then not moving one bit to the idea of going again. Needing it. Thriving off it. Even if you are the only one who can hear its sounds, even if you're the only one who loves, who knows, who understands, who cares, who sees. Be him who seizes.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- War Over Peace
Life.content: "war and courage have accomplished more great things than love of one's neighbor" there is no reason to shy away from this. War has accomplished more than peace. War has brought about the best of you, of the warrior. It was never the peaceful times where you got the most. No matter how peaceful this or that is, you know I will find a way to my place to my war. I will find a way to fight, michael jordan style. I will find a way to stop the cuteness and put the heart down.

Life.title: The Selfish Gene: The Blank Slate of Every Generation
Life.content: "As características adquiridas não são herdadas. Cada geração começa da estaca zero." Quando nasces, vens sozinho ao mundo e no mesmo nível que todos os outros.

Life.title: Dennett: Speaking to Ourselves and the Privacy of the Mind
Life.content: A comunicação, diz Dennett, tem as suas vantagens, mas há um problema: abre a porta de saída de informação, o que possibilita dar informação aos adversários. Para isto não acontecer, tem de haver um operador central que tenha noção das coisas. "Você apercebe-se de como é porque foi capaz de nos dizer, ou abster-se de nos dizer, como é ser como você é." "Falamos, não apenas para dizer aos outros o que pensamos, mas para dizer a nós próprios o que pensamos" - John Hughlings Jackson. Tal como neste momento eu estou a escrever para dizer a mim próprio o que penso. Um exemplo pessoal de competência sem compreensão era quando brincava num trampolim e fazia o desafio de quem salta mais alto, e eu, a dada altura, descobri e fiquei mestre em utilizar o timing a meu favor. Eu caía um pouquinho depois do outro cair. E não percebia porque é que saltava tão alto e o outro tão baixo. Foi só anos depois que, ao ver um vídeo sobre ondas, entendi que estava a usar o tempo numa espécie de ressonância, em que quando ele aterrava eu aterrava um pouco depois, dando tempo ao trampolim de vir para cima até ao ponto de equilíbrio, em que tem velocidade máxima e me lançava para cima, e o outro levava com uma onda secundária que eu criei quando aterrei, e perdia o momento. Tocar guitarra sem saber o que significam harmónicos e teoria da música também é competência sem compreensão. Dennett diz que aquela história da nossa mente ser privada é uma excelente ferramenta de design para sobrevivência. A inversão de raciocínio de Hume tem a ver com a sua ideia de causalidade que é projetada da nossa mente para o mundo. Isto pode ser generalizado para a imagem manifesta, que, afinal, influencia o manifesto (mundo). O manifesto influencia-nos (e à imagem manifesta), que depois projeta as propriedades subjetivas.

Life.title: Altered Traits: The Burden of Anticipation and the Good Samaritan
Life.content: O centro executivo do cérebro fornece-nos ao mesmo tempo uma vantagem única entre todos os animais e uma paradoxal desvantagem: a capacidade de antecipar o futuro e preocupar-nos com ele, bem como pensar acerca do passado e ter remorsos. Mas Macário não comeu as uvas, em vez disso deu-as a um eremita ali perto, que estava fraco e parecia ter mais necessidade da oferta. E esse eremita, apesar de se mostrar agradecido pela atenção de Macário, pensou ainda noutro deles que beneficiaria se comesse as uvas e passou-as a esse monge. E assim percorreram toda a comunidade de eremitas, até que voltaram a Macário. Como Martin Luther King Jr comentou acerca do conto do Bom Samaritano, aqueles que não ajudaram perguntaram a si próprios: "se eu ajudar, o que acontecerá a mim?" Mas o Bom Samaritano perguntou: "se eu não parar para ajudar, o que lhe acontecerá a ele?"

Life.title: Altered Traits: The Myth of Multitasking and Habituation
Life.content: "A habituação torna a vida gerível, mas aborrecida." O mito das multitarefas. Não fazemos multitarefas, simplesmente saltamos de umas para outras. É uma aproximação por causa do curto intervalo de tempo entre uma coisa e outra. Os executores de multitarefas são "presas da irrelevância". - Clifford Nass

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Sensing the Climax and Pivoting History
Life.content: That the fit of one's gauge is the ability to sense climax, to know a crisis is coming just by the way the leaves move now, being able to pick on small changes to get a sense of the stage we are on. Being ahead of the curve, preparing for the drop before everyone is still riding the high wave. Sensing the key moments. Having an instinct for when the tides are about to be changed, for when the time is right for the pivoting of history. Revolutions are made of this. Crisis is a dangerous opportunity. Understanding the hugeness of moments when others don't understand. When others can't see the potential in something. This allows you to win the war even if you don't win every battle, it makes you know what is mandatory and what is choiceful. In life, to know what really matters and must be gotten, and what one may let drift a little more.

Life.title: Altered Traits: Meta-Awareness and Practicing the Present
Life.content: Ativando a meta-consciência: um eu mínimo que consegue simplesmente notar na comichão, em vez de dizer "a minha comichão". Quando perguntaram ao Dalai Lama o momento mais feliz da sua vida respondeu: "acho que foi agora mesmo." "An organism that causes a disease that always kills its host will die with it; one that produces sublethal effects will have the opportunity to spread to another host." - if your problems (disease) have no place to live they will die. "Quando estiverem doentes, pratiquem sobre essa doença. Quando estiverem constipados, pratiquem sobre essa constipação. Praticando desta forma, todas as situações surgirão como meditação."

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: The Clash of Forces of Nature and MMA
Life.content: The clash of forces of nature. Everyone is sure they will win, but reality has no conception for this. One shall have to be wrong. Then there is this aspect to MMA that it's pure adrenaline; you don't feel pain any longer because of it. Gladiator-style force of nature. Unstoppable forces of nature, one thing UFC gave me light on. It's a war of the physical, but the mind is the most affected. Egos so exposed.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Cultivating Pressure Immunity
Life.content: Unbelievable stuff, this stuff should've been filmed. I have been putting together ever more to-the-limit pressure games of catching the train or the bus or the subway. All in situations where you depend on yourself but have to adjust to things that don't. High stakes, high pressure, ridiculous scenarios of win or lose. I have had minor losses, but undefeated in the major battles, or maybe not, hard to define, but at the cost of becoming ever more constriction resistant, pressure immune, developing a thick shield from which one cannot panic, one learns to only act, not react to the information. Not react to information that would have others killed of despair, or lose their shit. In the eyes of death, not blinking. Not shaking beyond the stare of war. Like a gladiator, a warrior, thriving in this arena where most crumble. Where only the main character leader can have certainties. Where the most irrational of the kinds of confidence takes place within, that where you don't know a way to win but you know you will win. It's not possible, it's necessary. Once I was going to Queima de Coimbra, I don't remember the details, but as always you try to calculate everything you can control in your head, do your part, let the gods do theirs sort of thing. Committing to something progressively all the while you don't even know the plan will work. You don't even know the plan. You are figuring it out along the way. That time of the Queima it was very high stakes, for it was me going with a buddy and there were not that many buses. I didn't call him for that would be a waste of time. So I went and as I reach the bus is there, we were right on the time to leave, and there he is, we found the bus and each other and get in. Once another I went running and as I reach the place of the bus I see a bus starting to move, I was confused, not sure if it was mine. It was and I wave my hands as it starts to turn left to get out of the parking lot. Many times I run to training and then from training. One time I go out on the subway on the wrong station, one after, had to run for like 3 minutes fast to get home, do all in a hurry and still get there. Today tho, we start by sleeping until later than expected to get a good sleep, got up 10:10, ate fast, only the snack (had ate breakfast the day before so as not to waste time here), get the bag ready, go to a station to buy the ticket, had problems finding the station, had problems with charging the ticket, then problems finding the subway, went into the stadium expecting 30 min workout. Got in the sets, intense focus and intensity of movement, that heightened state of battle, where you become a force to be reckoned with, where all the things come at the right time, all the right thoughts and actions. We get a solid session in, in and out, grip and rip, run away again, totally contrasting with the chill focus of the athletes there practicing. We go out after the limit we set for ourselves, like 1-2 min after, run to the subway. I had missed the subway, but I then to change subway caught just the right one. Once I left one the other one had the doors open and we got through, the doors still took some time to close. All of a sudden that giant win meant I had time, and I let that make me more chill. I then get out of home and notice I have gone way too late. I check Moovit app again to see which to catch, all of the options say 14:05. I think I'm screwed. I don't even know where to go, thought of coming back home, then I see that there is a subway 5 minutes which turns into 4 out from Alameda. I start going fast, and thought this one perhaps made me actually get there earlier than the app claimed. I run with it, convincing myself on the way this could work, while also gradually realizing this is all in. Miss this one and it's over. I go with the bag and the travel bag, walking fast, hard to run, at portions jogged a bit, go into the station and lots of people leaving. I had heard the subway leaving, but then, I think it's not my train, but then I hear another one coming. I rationally thought there was no time, I had to pass the card, run and descend the stairs. The subway was already stopping, but I would have more time than I estimated. I run and people start coming, I go fast through the stairs, not having time to decide internally whether we would make it or not, run, and the glimpse of past experiences where I lost just on the nail, and saw others getting in by throwing themselves while the doors were closing, I wouldn't let it get me this time round. I go and as the doors close I don't slow down, I put the travel bag in front, and the doors hit it and me, and we get in, saying "let's go", all desajeitado. I then run for the train, got that with about 2 min of advance, then it took like 10 min more to leave. This is stuff that won't be teached, you cultivate in yourself. You build the material of which you are made. You make it durable, reliable, immune to everything but your will. Your body it you ride obedient to none but your will. Stuff that you have on you that no one can take away.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: The Attacker's Advantage and Initiative
Life.content: The attacker has the advantage, says Kasparov. The power of taking initiative. It wastes less time, prevents overthinking, makes you learn more if you fail, and puts pressure on you. Being naive and going for the attack. Risking. Less counterfactual game in the head and more intuitive level play on the ground. This develops your intuition for these things further, so next time you are sharper and more right. You got scars on you, son. Aggressiveness in attack. Not letting the opinion of others derail your aggressiveness in action. Ambition and aggressiveness, not being shy of admitting you wanna be hella better than the environment around you now implies. It puts even more pressure on yourself, makes you much more self-critical and bullshit-avoiding. As risky as taking action is, committing to something is, not doing so is riskier still. Kasparov says that innovating is risky, but failing to do so is riskier. "The first essential to an attack is the will to attack." Intention. Attacking is about manifesting intention. Limitless. You can only be as good a defender as the attacker is. Your defenses are only as good as the greatness of the weapons they oppose. Attackers are all there ever was.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Urgency and Playing Without Tomorrow
Life.content: Having the voice as you go to sleep tell you, remind you, that you passed one more day without achieving your dreams. It's a rough voice, that sets you going by pain of not going. One that is real, even if it doesn't mean much. Because usually you don't achieve dreams everyday, yet to achieve dreams it means there is a day where you don't have to tell yourself this. This ties back to the point made by Kasparov: That you should lose as much as you are willing to. There is urgency. Also when you understand that you went and didn't come back with dreams achieved or what else, all of a sudden you don't let the day pass so gently. You actively search for bringing something home. Playing as if you are losing and running out of time, because are you not? Not delaying. There is no tomorrow.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: The Patience of Building in Silence
Life.content: Building is again such an undervalued thing by us and them. One feels a need to express, to take that semi-open angle and shoot the ball. There is power in the patience of keeping building in silence, keeping progressing unnoticed. Keep the ball a little longer to get an even better angle, letting the defenders slide, waiting for the shot, and only then shoot.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Distractions and the Easy Routine
Life.content: The mistake others did early, perhaps people do now. Like seeing the kids in school that didn't study, and now you feel like they were playing a losing play, in that you are smarter and very much took back all the fun that was lost back then. But then not realizing that this too is settling, just for an upper level. In a sense, this is just distraction; they are losing themselves without knowing. Keep an eye open so you don't fall into this trap. Playing music is a way people keep themselves at surface bay. When they have the opportunity to see the depths of the mind, they prefer to keep the music going. The light - the phone, mostly. The easiness of routine and blind following is that you are protected by walls even if they hide behind all that you want.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Missed Chances and Staying in the Bubble
Life.content: I get this tremendous truth out that my first year in uni, even for all the greats in it, was mostly não aproveitar. Not taking all the chances. So many chances missed. Unbelievable. Instead procrastinating at home, using the time of the opportunities to this end of watching YouTube or whatever. Staying home. Staying in your bubble can be about maintaining your girlfriends for too long, not risking. Be content with the risk you took to get the relationship but unwilling to try again but better.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Home as a Checkpoint and Local Maximization
Life.content: On a more down-to-earth note of this game, home is your checkpoint. You spawn there and you are safe. You can then explore the world to form new checkpoints, expand your home to accommodate so much more potential. So even if you go working on the outside, if something goes wrong you can just come back to your home and restart. So things can't get so bad that they are really bad. You build a soft enough bed, and even if you fall reaching for the heights, you won't fall below your bed to greater lows. Most won't search for a winning direction, but instead locally maximize their life to their wish. Exploring becomes more of a search for local improvements, changing some parts for better ones in your machine. This you shouldn't underestimate. You should never underestimate how great something can get that locally improves. Very many times it stumbles on something that alters its trajectory. Don't underestimate the power of just getting a better position, as opposed to going all in for the win. Very smoothly does an attentive mind live like a fucking boss; does he here and there take the best to come with him. The nomad of the space of discovery. He is not bound to a routine, to a time to eat, to a place to study. He instead is building and building better and better, and when you least expect it, he is gaining position on everyone.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Facing the Best to Elevate Yourself
Life.content: Challenging and facing the best that each opposition has to offer. This I do consciously: to be better at arm wrestling than the better arm wrestler, to be up there with the best climber, and the best gigachad, and be on par with the best test taker. If your opposition is the greatest at their things, you can very much be great at everything—not the best so easily, but very close. Drop your ego and face the best at every time; elevate yourself by losing and learning.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Interruption, Momentum, and World Building
Life.content: And much more I realize that, as I very recently start to write after some period of retrieval, the thoughts come shit at first. Just like when you open a water tap that hasn't been opened in God knows how long, there are blockages. When opening yourself to thoughts, they may still port some leftover uneasiness from the drought that filled them. This makes me wonder all the more whether interrupting may truly be the killer of some innovation. That after interrupting you are brought back to ground zero, to that token prediction, non-competitive, non-instinctive, purely operational yet reliable mode of being. The most reliable you can manage to arrange within yourself. Going out and keeping doing it leads to the rabbit holes that bring on the unexpected. Yet you know that you can lose it all at once. That you will want to lose it, or to convince yourself that just camping one night there in the place of the unknown won't harm. That after setting your mind to sleep, the force that all your walk brought along will be ever more vibrant. You are sure of that right before sleeping. It's one hell of a fine way your body submits you, and makes you tap out. That promise of tomorrow with everything of today. I say no more. Hard to gain back things that made you alleviate. All that is alleviated is in some form lost. As the pressure creeps up, as you gain territory unseen, you unchart newer places, yet longing for home. At last, you return to the safe start, to your world, the world you control, love, and know, however low it may place. Interruption is nothing more than the frustration of your attempts at getting out of this place. It stops, and in stopping weakens and destroys. Momentum is of the essence; when you have it, may you find the strength to keep capitalizing on the "it" of the thing. Creating worlds is very hard; one has to remain unaffected and disinfected from the smells that you learned to embrace as family in the other worlds. A sniff of the old, and the bubble collapses. Back to mode zero. This demands of your self more than an attention span; it is some kind of devotion or focus span, one that carries indifference, one that needs no numbers, non-negotiable. Having the capacity to keep at it even when overheating from the amount of compute, to keep on adding layers and opening threads, to keep on exploring the tree of plays. To not be discouraged by the immensity of the project ahead. To keep building block by block without seeing the whole. Inasmuch as it is about losing the sense of the whole. Ego isn't as affected by putting a block, by taking a step.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Finding the Winning Direction
Life.content: To ask who is winning is a direction question. Look around at the players of the game of life, and assess who is winning. If you are righter about this, you are closer to winning. People can be totally fooled by side quests and miscellaneous activities; they can catch all the accolades without passing the main mission. People find all sorts of goals along the paths but fail to recognize the winning way, the direction that ultimately will take them to the win. Even if you blunder or stop, going the winning direction may be superior to wandering around everywhere but it. But who is winning? Is it the richest person on earth, or the person with the best lifestyle? Is it the monk searching for nirvana? Is it Bryan Johnson on the "don't die" main mission? Yeah, the argument for "don't die" is pretty much that with the information you are given, you ought to keep playing; that's where opportunity starts and time begins. Some even learn to hesitate and not get rolling even if they find the winning direction. Who is losing? Perhaps a more offensive question: what if they don't even want to play the game, do you count them?

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: The Intelligence Agent Mindset
Life.content: Perhaps a great way to stay sharp is to learn new languages. In general, whatever resonates with being a high-level spy or intelligence agent. Those are pretty sharp, if I say so myself. Now you should start to hear the whispers of the narrator floating over the moment, floating in space and time. He says: you are in this place. But are you really? You are in this time? Are you? Well, be more. You are woken to this very moment; forget the past, forget whatever led you here. Most people are still in what leads them. It can be their train of thought, for example. But you are different. You have the framework that you are a special intelligence agent, deployed strategically with a mission. Find the bomber. Yes, look around. Something doesn't add up. There's something wrong. Find it. Enter the room and scan it fast, see everybody, and register their every movement. Assimilate the much that is happening without your notice. Widen your attention span. Make your attention worthy of value, make it have consequence. This is the crime scene. There are your suspects. Read each person into your system, ID-like identification. You will surprise yourself about the details you find. Stand one layer above everyone. You may find some awake ones too along the way. You were just dropped, you gotta have as much as possible. Find in front of you the matter of your poetry. Yes, there is loads. This is a silent meditation of the environment. It doesn't cost nothing and no one will even give a damn, unless they are on it themselves. Notice that in movies when there is action, the special agents know who is a special agent. Only they do. The others didn't even notice where they were. And they are the center of movement on the chessboard. The others are extras, NPCs of the simulation. In fact, there are way too many humans for you to run out of inspection material. It's also just a way of being the main character in the scene.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Introspection and Position Evaluation
Life.content: Analyzing yourself is crucial to improving. Especially the things which you can't directly measure, Kasparov argues, must be the subject of your thinking and introspection. This requires time of reflection. Evaluation of position. Understanding who is winning is crucial for the development of the victor. Knowing how to win and knowing where to go is the basic essence of the battle. You know where you want to go; most people have this, but they leave much less thought for clearing the fog that lies within them. For ego, they don't want themselves to be limited to being in a clear position; they'd rather be spread out, far-reaching all possibilities.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Murdering the Inner Child and Aliveness
Life.content: In fact, being alive is something that most of us lose along the way, after being children. Then we either come to the rescue or give up on our inner children. This is the problem of murder. Most of us flat-out murder our child selves, and with it all the dreams, questions, expectations, daring, main character energy, and shameless attitude. The system has us becoming awkward. If I was a religious person, I would say that it is a test of God to retrieve what was taken, to get back the thing we held most solidly about ourselves, now our slaves. It is likewise a test of God to see what you do when there isn't direction. When you are set free. Being set free allows us to gauge closer than at any other event your inner self. This is the way to measure the worth of one's time. This is also how one measures the height to which one has thought about what he is and wants to be. This measures the aliveness of the individual. The inner compass. Another form of suicide, or rather another feature of this separation from our child selves, is the loss of curiosity. One really dies when one stops wanting more. The rest is just time and space to burn. There is, however, a smooth path to death. First, one may give up the dreams of his child self, then he may give up ambition altogether. You can't beat someone who never gives up. By the same token, how could you beat someone that is alive forever?

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Death as a Choice and Escaping It
Life.content: This is the competition that Bryan Johnson is taking part in. It's not about winning the game here. It's just getting a better position. It's positional play in the game of life. It is visible that you die when you drop the sword, when you stop fighting. That can happen long before your organs fail. Just look at someone dying. They die when they stop moving their extremities and stop fighting for air. Then stillness in the heart rate comes. Piiiiiiiiii. You can sense the at-last acceptance of the time that has come. Thus suicide. As far as I'm aware, every death by age was a committed suicide in plain daylight. When you go gentle into that dark night, when you don't fight against the dimming of the light, you suicide. Way, way more people commit suicide than credit is given. Death only happens when you accept its arrival, your departure. I believe you have power in escaping death. I believe you can push through it. If you just have that same naturality with which you now sleep, certain that you will wake up to see the sunrise once more, then you might just do it, even at old age. That age is your choice. People had this urgency of becoming teenagers; I, on the other hand, wanted to push against that force that wanted me not being considered a child anymore. No, you command when you are young. You can't be old if you never think of yourself as old. Being old starts with believing so, another shape of suicide. I'm in charge. It is your fault if you accepted some beliefs, even if they were led on to you. Extreme ownership.

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Sharpness and Staying in Control
Life.content: Positional play is not only to win by being alive and not dying but also by just being sharper. There will be ways of keeping someone breathing, half-walking through life as dead meat, almost as if all they do is occupy space and ask for food. Sharpness is that ability to keep charge, to stay with the hand on the wheel. Especially in this ever-rapidly evolving world, sharpness is huge; the ability to adapt is crucial so you don't get behind. Because we are all running with the pack. If you lose the pack, you are a dead target for wild animals. You will get taken advantage of. So even if your only advantage is this, wear it proudly. Play in such a way that you are in control, that you have your arm wrapped around every corner. Don't turn off the engine. Have grip on every ground, so as to not slip. Only like that will you be the last to laugh. Physical exercise serves this purpose. Now everyone will be going different directions, and I don't ask you to get out of yours, but rather that your reference frame is path-connected with theirs. That you don't let them go so far to places where you got no vision towards. Have leverage. Stand tall so you can see all of them, even as they threaten to disappear into the horizon. Sharpness a la Kasparov. Strategic. Decaying brains is a shame of a thing. It's sad to lose your vigor, your strength of living. And yeah, those that care for you would be sad at your decay. They would perceive you as unsavable. As gone. As weak, as not self-sufficient, as not able to protect yourself.

Life.title: The Death of Ivan Ilyich: Childhood, the Ponzi Scheme of Life, and the Failed Existence
Life.content: "Só a infância se salvava: só ela valeria a pena ser vivida se a vida regressasse." A vida é um esquema em pirâmide (Ponzi). Os mais velhos trabalham para que na infância os outros sejam mesmo felizes. O problema é que vem primeiro a bonança, e depois vem a idade adulta. E só se sabe aproveitar a infância e só se sabe que já a perdemos e queremos voltar, e só se percebe que era a melhor parte quando acabou. "Que significa isto? PORQUÊ...? Não é possível!... Não pode ser que a vida seja tão feia, tão estúpida!... E, se o é de verdade, para quê morrer, para quê morrer em agonia? Há aqui qualquer coisa que não bate certo. Será que eu não vivi como devia? Bem, fiz tudo o que tinha a fazer, respondeu imediatamente, AFUGENTANDO A ÚNICA MAS IMPOSSÍVEL SOLUÇÃO DO PROBLEMA DA VIDA E MORTE." Depois de começar a pensar na sua infância e memórias: "o melhor é não pensar nisto... É DOLOROSO DEMAIS." "Quanto mais se remonta ao passado, mais se sentia vivo." "Uma progressão inversamente proporcional ao quadrado da morte" - Genius. "É impossível resistir, pensava, mas ao menos que eu pudesse compreender. Ora, isso é proibido. Poderia explicar tudo dizendo que não vivi como devia. É isso, recuso-me a reconhecer..." "E se a minha vida consciente não foi realmente o que deveria ter sido? Conceber pela primeira vez o inconcebível e admitir que na realidade a sua vida podia ter sido uma vida falhada." "É mentira, é mentira! Toda a existência se resumiu a uma constante mentira, destinada a disfarçar as questões da vida e da morte!"

Life.title: How Chess Imitates Life: Don't Watch the Competition
Life.content: "Don't watch the competition more than you watch yourself" - this is a huge one. And the generalizations quickly grow. It means don't watch sports more than you do them. Even everyone on the internet, you can see them as competition. Don't watch Elon Musk more than you do your reading. Don't watch others solving problems more than you solve them.

Life.title: Fear and Trembling: Overcoming the Fear of Being Great
Life.content: And that's savage. To say hey imma do my thing, you write down my achievements. And yet it comes as such a shame, to retreat to a position of writing history, to observe others which at birth were your equal agora a desbravar o mundo que ninguém conhece. It feels stupid to follow those who don't know shit about the way, just like you don't know shit about the way. To hide in this shell of safety, to let be consumed by the infinite, to fool yourself and to fall prey. In the end predators ran this thing, and no soft rationale could take away from what they took away from you. Balls. People come back to this state of observing your life. Few looked at the impossible and dared it. Few looked at destiny and made it. Few embraced the mission of the impossible. Few didn't let the height of the mountain shake the belief. Few kept going. Few kept taking step after step, leap of faith after leap of faith. Most delayed leaps of faith. Some never had leaps of faith since their child years. Dare to believe. Dare to be wrong. Dare to take your dreams seriously. Dare to embrace destiny. Dare to become what you are. Dare to mean what you say. Most people shy away from profundity. If the life of your dreams was given, you'd be surprised at how many people couldn't hold it together, how many people couldn't aguentar-se. If they could they would, it's right in front of them. People are actually scared of being great, like most would prefer to stay than to go, and that's why they do what they do. Hence another meaning of the phrase, behind your fears is the man you want to become. They are shy of becoming that which they want to become. So they sabotage themselves. They are prey of their subconscious. They shy away from simplicity. They shy away from the defined. Preferem-se refugiar numa bolha, uma nuvem que não deixa penetrar os olhos, onde não tenham de escolher isto ou aquilo. Perdem-se perante o infinito, não querem encontrar-se nem olhar para cima, pois assim verão o quão longe estão do cume da montanha. They fail efficiency-wise. Like you don't just touch the bubble, that would break their hearts, to know reality. So their imagination takes over, and they live in the matrix of their own selves. They don't own themselves. E aqui eu falho de um pecado que é cair na superficialidade. Quando digo algo, digo sempre a seguir que é a brincar. The neutralization of neutrality.

Life.title: The Death of Ivan Ilyich: The Banal Life and Saving Appearances
Life.content: "A história de Ivan é simples, banal e atroz como nenhuma outra." "A idade em que a inércia é recompensada" - velhice. "No fim de contas, a sua casa era tal e qual as de todas aquelas pessoas que, sem serem ricas, pretendem passar por tal e, ao fim e ao cabo, se copiam umas às outras." Eles em conjunto constroem um quadro que pensam que só eles é que não gostam mas os outros todos gostam. Cada um pensa para si mesmo: sou o único ao qual isto não me diz muito, e como todos gostam, tenho de gostar. E todos pensam isto e falam uns com os outros como se gostassem daquilo mas ninguém gosta. Finalmente alguém diz que não gosta e os outros ficam: eu também não, pensei que era o único. "Fazia comme tout le monde, tinha um salão comme tout le monde." Tout le monde quer ter uma cena comme tout le monde, que na verdade é uma cena de muitas poucas pessoas. "Já não se tratava de viver uma vida feliz, mas sim de salvar as aparências." Tout le monde pensa que tout le monde quer saber dessas coisas mas tout le monde está a marimbar-se fundamentalmente.

Life.title: Jon Fosse: Intentional Ambition and the Power of the Present
Life.content: What came from the first 9 pages was not coincidence, it may have been from my mindset that I was believing that now I should strike genius. I don't think no more that intention is only decisive in the gym and in plyos and what not. Intention might be what takes me to greater heights than ever. If your intention in reading some pages of a book is to be productive, then you will read pages of the book to be productive. Intention. How could you fool the world if not even inside your head you have won it? Spirit. Don't assume modest intentions, why limit yourself like that. In your head is the place for extreme ambition, absolute dominance. That the present is the only real thing, and that maybe Kierkegaard or whomever never existed, hold yourself in the highest of regards, no dead person can control you, no unalive person can dictate you, you have the power of existence over everybody that isn't real.

Life.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: The Erasing of Moments and the Plurality of Lives
Life.content: Imagine even more that I hadn't decided to start writing things on this OneNote thing. Like how close I was to not have these thoughts written. How close was I to being a floating escaping thought, flying at the speed of light away from anything in the world to see. At least I'm a thought in someone else's thought. I'm an advertiser. Time isn't counted linearly in minutes. Another timely mistake of our people. If life of people was represented in some other equally valid way of tracking, man would they be surprised to see that their life was nothing but a couple fluctuations, some peaks, and then just silence. The lives of people are lived in silence. That's where memory fools us once again. Its tracking function, not at all linear, makes the bumps go closer. Erases all these moments which I now write. Erases so much. If other worldly creatures saw our memories they would think our lives were 3 days and that's being generous. Just like Phineas and Ferb put it. So many days that make up this life but no one knows how to use them. Like it's so much easier to live a more continuous life, but bro, make no fucking mistake, you could incorporate a plurality never before seen of lives. Let go. Most time of your life is spent looking at the erratic nature of thoughts. Contemplation. If people just had grip.

Life.title: Uma Brancura Luminosa: The Power of Intention and Ambition
Life.content: What came from the first 9 pages was not coincidence, it may have been from my mindset that I was believing that now I should strike genius. I don't think no more that intention is only decisive in the gym and in plyos and what not. Intention might be what takes me to greater heights than ever. If your intention in reading some pages of a book is to be productive, then you will read pages of the book to be productive. Intention. How could you fool the world if not even inside your head you have won it? Spirit. Don't assume modest intentions, why limit yourself like that. In your head is the place for extreme ambition, absolute dominance. That the present is the only real thing, and that maybe Kierkegaard or whomever never existed, hold yourself in the highest of regards, no dead person can control you, no unalive person can dictate you, you have the power of existence over everybody that isn't real.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Things Move On Without You: Freedom
Life.content: That things move on without you should only give you strength, that you can come in and out at anytime, you owe no responsibility to it. You can be a total unknown to everyone and infiltrate yourself in many worlds, knowing and following only your passion.

Life.title: Jon Fosse: Erased Moments and the Illusion of Memory
Life.content: Imagine even more that I hadn't decided to start writing things on this OneNote thing. Like how close I was to not have these thoughts written. How close was I to being a floating escaping thought, flying at the speed of light away from anything in the world to see. At least I'm a thought in someone else's thought. I'm an advertiser. Time isn't counted linearly in minutes. Another timely mistake of our people. If life of people was represented in some other equally valid way of tracking, man would they be surprised to see that their life was nothing but a couple fluctuations, some peaks, and then just silence. The lives of people are lived in silence. That's where memory fools us once again. Its tracking function, not at all linear, makes the bumps go closer. Erases all these moments which I now write. Erases so much. If other worldly creatures saw our memories they would think our lives were 3 days and that's being generous. Just like Phineas and Ferb put it. So many days that make up this life but no one knows how to use them. Like it's so much easier to live a more continuous life, but bro, make no fucking mistake, you could incorporate a plurality never before seen of lives. Let go. Most time of your life is spent looking at the erratic nature of thoughts. Contemplation. If people just had grip.

Life.title: [NEEDS TITLE — Tuesday, 3 June 2025 22:05:29]
Life.content: Que gostos nao se discutem, mas que a vida é sobre disutir gostos, that all life is disputing over taste and tasting says Nietzsche. life as the battle of wills presents no more than the subjective to be discussed. Because it isn't discussed says the proverb, can you discuss it without hearing anyone. Because no one can take away from you your wills, for they are totally subjective. You can actually have them and change them and see where they lead you, whereas in non willed things you progress onto something, which is good but isn't generator of time evolution, rather it sets the time we are in.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Freedom in Present-Moment Adaptability
Life.content: There is much freedom in adaptability, in present moment to moment adaptability. Being able to carry all the past and the future within, yet being able to let go of all of it, be here now and change direction, and then succeed in the new avenue you find yourself in. That is survival, power and freedom.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Being Active with Your Investments of Time
Life.content: Being active with your investments, always controlling that which can either be controlled by you or you have no control over. That means when given 5 minutes starting to read, willing and prepared to read for 5 hours if the train doesn't arrive.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- He Who Has a Why, He Who Has a How
Life.content: That he who has a why to live can bare anyhow. But also that he who has a how to live can bare any why, said Jonathan Bi probably from someone else too.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Capable of All Disorder, Master of Stillness
Life.content: Be capable of all disorder, yet as much capable of shutting it all down and be still. May no wild beast be able to wreck your focus. That you be able to handle all chaos and everything everywhere all at once. Maybe you overcome those beasts, not avoid them. May you not be withdrawn from the full experience there is.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Choose With Whom You Cross the River
Life.content: You choose with whom you cross the river. With your becoming or someone else's being.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Always Win: The Meta-Belief
Life.content: My brother ruben said hypnosis- win, I always win. What if you are crazy enough to believe you always win? That is advantageous, if of course you still learn from your mistakes, you simply have the meta belief that whatever happens was still a win in the long run. That makes you keep up the pace, never letting go for you are in a winning position and keep it must be. You never have reason to be let down, you always go into the next moment as a winner shall you be.

Life.title: [NEEDS TITLE — Sunday, 18 May 2025 13:31:39]
Life.content: Commanding takes really that you expose yourself, that you allow yourself to be object of obeying, that you go one to many hand to hand. That you must gather the multiplicity within to take on multiple people at the same time, this is one way to grow tall, to be equivalent to many, physically mentally linguistically. Commanding yourself the more multiplicity it yields, for for all yous you have to battle with. Both go to war with side by side, but also be able to take on every last one of them at once, thus you form your strongest self, which in risk and experiment, by winning can only be the one commandant of all the squad and all the forces that you are. There you must reach.

Life.title: On Life Affirmation as Practical Course of Action
Life.content: Faced with the reality of the world it is the most useful, the most pragmatic course of action yet much of ideal course of inaction it is too. Its this affirmation of life and the self and the world and the yes saying to all that exists, all that all the more is but a spark, rather a persistent quality, persistent qualities are scarce in a world that at every moment takes from you all that you had that very moment.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Drives That Are Contrarian and Inaccessible
Life.content: Drives those that are as tested as much as they are contrarian, inaccessible. Those that language or program treat poorly, in modernity or in humanity. Yet find no reason to ditch the easy ones that most already have. There is no further merit to contrarian than that it can work

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Every Hero Must Go Through Their Own Journey
Life.content: All the heros that will be made will have to go through the journey themselves, developing their own structure in many ways probably different. They can't make others understand their way. Thus one alone, yet in parallel with all the heros that there once was, rises above the masses and is unable to give back to them. To even show one has something to give to them

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Overcoming Builds the Height to Fly
Life.content: Thus with one's overcoming of values and concepts, one builds the height within, one wins the game, before one can fly away to level up. Weapons these values and concepts are for the destruction of the life you've been living into a greater one without them

Life.title: On Talent, Equality, and Winning the Moment
Life.content: I still hold the absolute belief of an equality but that to me is not this one. And maybe it is. Maybe I'm incoherent, and that gives me more power. But I have this belief from a very long time that Everyone has the same talent when integrated over all possible parameters. At least when one is born. That everyone has one thing they were born to be the best in the world in. But even here equality doesn't hang on very long, for environmental factors outside your control and, above all, your actions that are in your control Yet at every living moment there can be as a semi thought experiment leaderboard of who is winning the moment, who is winning life in this very moment, who is having the best life this very moment. And it is possible that one wins every single time. So no equality in that sense can make for this. It is possible to live a life better than every other combined.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Undefeated by the Pressure of the Moment
Life.content: Once again the pressure came, but we are still undefeated, had to get out of training, lost the subway because took too much time finding the pass in the bag, thought the pressure was catching up but after all it was chill, then get home and there take too much time but still chill getting to the subway, about a 5 min margin from sub to train, but the subway gets late, is full, had to force myself in, then it stops at a station for like 5 minutes, because someone went unconscious leaving the subway. I sprint to the train, and it was late and we got the w.

Life.title: Undefeated by the Pressure of the Moment II
Life.content: Quotes and questions :Once again the pressure came to us, but we are still undefeated, had to get out of training, lost the subway because took too much time finding the pass in the bag, thought the pressure was catching up but after all it was chill, then get home and there take too much time but still chill getting to the subway, about a 5 min margin from sub to train, but the subway gets late, is full, had to force myself in, then it stops at a station for like 5 minutes, because someone went unconscious leaving the subway. I sprint to the train, and it was late and we got the w.

Life.title: Quotes and Questions: The Champion Has a Name
Life.content: The body is still the best piece of engineering and architecture, the best autonomic system ever conceived and built, the most expensive work of art, the most prolific machine. This truth can easily fly over one's head. One can search himself everywhere but in the body, where all that he is lies astill. We do not want to admit the greatness that our body presents. We are now evolving into selfism. To play the game one has to play along the most reliable system, that which runs ourselves. It is thus wisest to cultivate the body high up, for you don't know if there will be another such of controller of your player. Lose contact with the body and you risk becoming NPC. Perhaps here is too the greatest freedom. To free ourselves from our controllers. But it's a Pascal's wager, and all humans as of today have lost that bet. All of humans till now have had to live in their own body. Furthermore paramount is the importace of being in charge of the controller, so as not to get played. If you can't get rid of it, you might as well own it. Each one has Jesus inside him, the on-earth god's presence. It's his body, and even for the unfaithful ones, those that are only content with a higher presence, that higher presence has only existed in the depths of his earthly body. Those that don't trust enough in the power of their fiber. If you can't beat it, join it. Maybe the body, the so previously despised piece of unpure meat, has everything codified in him. Has all the marks of creation. The search within could be our ticket to paradise. Maybe our body plays a part in consciousness, we are just too weary of associating the two, one cruel and old, the other transforming and fundamental. We have been lied to, people. We lied to ourselves. The first stage is denial, denial that we are stuck to this thing, this random body of ours. This being a stunning realization dates back. We've been through it all, now beyond accepting it we must leverage it. We like to think our minds play dimensions above our bodies. But again, we are the controllers as much as the controlled. A brain alone isn't able to reproduce, to survive. Long ago this the most decisive of our species's treaties was signed. The symbiosis of mind and body.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Natural, After Taming
Life.content: Being a natural, and following nature are worthy of admiration. The perfect innocence of senses, after they were deadened and taken into your control, and then by immense control did you give yourself to free them. After taming all conquers of yours, letting them flow their new yours given way.

Life.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Climb Past, Then Fly
Life.content: Being able to climb past, but then fly.

Life.title: Human, All Too Human: Marching Forward, Perpendicular Growth, and Independence
Life.content: Why marching forward is of importance. Growth requires change they say. Ideally you would be outgrowing yourself constantly to become the highest form of yourself. Going beyond each phase you milk the shit out of, to understand them more fully. The things that trap us are the ones we don't understand, we only have of them the sight of the interior. We lack angles. Travelling places anew gives you more angles. Think of it as you wake up suddenly in a black space, and you want to know the space as well as possible. Well you must be constantly on the move, looking for new sights and crossings, both to expand your horizon as to clarify even more where you've already been through. In fact if you are on the boat to experience everything, in a well behaved space, you would want to go perpendicular to the space that you have already covered. This elicits the greatest stimulus, to go in totally independent directions. If you are however in this for other reasons, as almost everyone is, to the extent that it is also hard to go for the other option, then it is valuable to first search the space around you for better possibilities before you settle to build your house. There's a great perk to exploding all at once in your utmost vigour and display of color, like suddenly putting yourself out there go on this rampage of conquering. Nietzsche poses this strong constraint to be free, that you have at least 2 thirds of the day for yourself. Interesting how much discrepancy there is between this standard and what has become usual nowadays. That wisdom walks along age so you accept when your time has come. It as just a trick to make you more pleasurous. This another reason why natural, that meaning, comes from nature, which is no trivial of a fact.

Life.title: Human, All Too Human: Imposing Thoughts, Partying, and Artificial Behaviors
Life.content: When you have to expose that which you consume and impose that which you create you are closer to truth. Reading aloud is how you expose what you consume and writing down your thought is how you impose what you create. Reading aloud thus allows you to learn about your own character, says Nietzsche. In the first sense I laid out that is the case. But in a more literal sense too, for it makes you play the characters of the story, it makes you see more clearly what kind of character you are yourself, to which do you find natural saying their passages, to which do you adopt the words and not just read them. To which you believe what you are saying. Intention is very hardly hidden from plain sight when you speak aloud. That partying is about pretending you belong there. This first stems from an adoption of what we consider fun from Hollywood, from pop culture. It is societal standard that at a certain age you start to enjoy going out getting hammered dancing getting chicks. It's made up, it's not natural. Few people at the start enjoy alcohol, just like few at the start enjoy only getting home at 5 am and feeling shit the next day, or somehow enjoy listening 3 hours in a row music at super high volumes with epileptic type lights and everyone banging each other in their own world because they literally can't talk to each other, either in their own world of thoughts observing the absurdity of the beast or trying to forget themselves in the sound of the music. This Hollywood thing makes expectations high, like there's epic scenes in movies about partying, and here I'm referring to this type of partying which I guess evolved from more natural types of partying, in an attempt to industrialize it perhaps. Like when people say oh my god this is going to be the most epic night ever. Do they think that is going to happen by drinking and going to the disco? I doubt it. If it's the best night ever it's just cause you surpassed yourself, either by letting go harder than before or doing great in social settings or with girls. Notice how fast some people go from all out banging music and rocking side to side, wilding and tearing, to complete normal in about 5 seconds as they get out of the club. Not genuine. Almost like they got paid to be an extra.

Life.title: Human, All Too Human: Deep Thoughts vs Popular Answers, and Finding Love in Actions
Life.content: That when answering questions of opinion we don't give our opinion less we have given it great thought and let the deep rise to the surface, where we can fish it into our message. Mostly however, we are most worried about piecing the phrase together, and so, with this added pressure, we default back to giving a popular feels-right type of answer. That's why this space of writing lets you make your thoughts. There is no pressure. No time limit. No social expectancies hindering the way of pure thought thereof. That we sometimes are annoyed at the world or others because we are annoyed at us. One must look out for this type of mistake. Maybe my annoyance at always going out was that I was annoyed that I wasn't doing much at home and then projecting onto the fault that it was because I was with others. That in wanting to know something it would do us good to find our way into loving it. To assume its greatness as we dive into it, to give it such a headstart. This was for me the most useful in school, to do something very well you wanna love its cause, or learn to love it, so that you align yourself with it, you allow its collaboration. When I had to do a presentation on a book, this is exactly what is the most useful to deliver a great presentation about the book. If you say you don't enjoy the book, it's kinda hard to elaborate and have will to even write about it. However if you find the points that to you make it the best book you ever read then of course you will be in a better position to find greatness in it. This applies mostly to those places where you are given what to do. Then, complaining about not doing this or that won't lend you much far, that is, if you are really stuck to it. Then maximizing it is the proper goal. In the real world of possibility however, this allows you to choose what you want to do, by making you love the things you a priori didn't. It allows you to intentionally morph yourself.

Life.title: Human, All Too Human: Sleep, Memory, and the Harvest of Nonsense
Life.content: Says that memory is most sacrificed in sleep. In my words, that when one's body wants one's body to sleep, one's mind simulates and dissimulates one's mind. One achieves a temporary yet working everyday effect of lightness of conscience in committing the deadly sin of sleep and what it entails. Dream. An harvest of nonsense, perhaps to dissimulate the sense in when we are awake. Perhaps an attempt of deconstructing reality the right way. It seems like a dimensions' worth leap to figure the meaning of these things. Could we just be getting the local picture? Can this mean more than what at first it might seem? Nietzsche says that it has to do with the primitive brain. That it's a way of closing the gap between our lives of today and those from which today was built. We live in a very high level place during the day; when sleeping we return to ones and zeros, to assembly. We get a grasp of how we first made things, by incorporating them without questioning, by believing in the direct explanation.

Life.title: Human, All Too Human: The Rawness of Art and the Era of the Highlight
Life.content: That the advantage of art is you only get the state function-valued final product, no evolution is implicit in what is created. You gotta know the evolution to do it, but don't be afraid of rejecting it all when showing it. Leave it raw, live it spicy. Don't put sauce on it, don't mix in other ingredients, just put the plate down with the product. No words can cover it up, when you nailed it, no song can filter it, it just sits as it is, there is no denying what it is. The rest will they do. Journalling by this reasoning should be for yourself, work in the shadows to shine in the light. Sou um gajo sem noção. Essa é a minha vantagem. O que trago para a mesa é não ter limites, conseguir estar no plano mais acima mais fictício mais infantil, e no entanto ter sempre por detrás da orelha a parte real desta coisa. Não é fácil transitar de uma faixa para a outra, não é fácil mudar o chip instantaneamente, mas é isso que vou conseguir fazer. Go high, I can go low. Hardly hear each other, how would they give a glimpse of care into a poem you wrote as training, they don't, it's the era of the highlight, whether you want it or not, your life may be registered in the 21st and 22nd century, through your highlights, maybe not what you consider to be your highlights. Yet if you leave it so raw that it smells, that others can't explain, that others can't rationalize but only appreciate, that others can only fantasize about, you have got a place reserved deep inside their mind, my dear friend. And that's what your modern day world genes have wanted.
