Sometimes shit happens that you don't explain, just roll with it, sometimes what you think is a highlight is a mere entry in the blockchain of this world, one more transaction. Like honestly though. No one understands your motives, they don't understand your jokes, only theirs. In this higher abstraction world we are just using each other, there are very few that you actually got your back protected, there are genuinely very few that you would really fucking help.

Life.title: Human, All Too Human: Fluidity, Letting Go, and Fighting Entropy
Life.content: All "Be like water" means is this. To have controlled the beast you've become, and manipulate it at will. To be able to, to expand the tiles of your piano, the buttons of your spacecraft, the colors of your painting, the formulations of phrases. The worlds, and the feelings, and the mindsets you can tap into without ever leaving your couch. There is this hesitation to letting go, letting yourself go and become yourself. When it's about letting in, we let in more than we need, more than we wanted. Yet letting go with all its consequences, letting this shitty world forever for a better one... We are very hesitant to jump, afraid of falling. Confronted with whatever metaphor works best for you, maybe abyss, maybe handle to reach, we are slow in taking that leap of faith. And we know all about it. We don't allow ourselves to aim the highest at every moment because we have memory of the past, and not of the future, the same memory that allows us to build the scope of our aim at present. We are fearful of letting go friends that at present aren't in our best interests to be friends, places, in essence everything to which we have invested some part of our lives. Fight we must, this tendency of being tied to that to which we gave rope. Be careful the places you tie your rope to, be careful where you touch. There's a big big difference between being attached to your surroundings in the abstract wide high dimensionality space of things and not being so. Yet the line between them both is so fine one is reduced to a coin toss to decide it. Have then this option of shutting off that which you are part of, have the ability to forget every part of the universe causally connected to you. Fight entropy, gain the right to use your memory however you desire. Conjure up in yourself that which keeps making alive the life. Fighting entropy in many ways. Relationships, your body, your mind, the company. You fear more the man you haven't solved, haven't been able to put into words what you think he wants. Allow yourself the liberty of not being fully known, don't put all you have in the light. Let yourself be the sole owner of everything you have, even if everything you have is also someone else's.

Life.title: The Origin of Species: Finding Inspiration Right Before Your Eyes
Life.content: Applications. Search for applications of everything. Darwin applied all the philosophers (principles of geology for example) and applied them to his own. Because sometimes the base of the greatest idea of your life is right before YOUR FUCKING EYES!!! He read a book about the behavior of populations for "amusing" and from there he discovered his thesis. Just for fun he found his greatest idea.

Life.title: Human, All Too Human: Flies and the Dead Space
Life.content: And a fly that flies her way, it is alive, it is game of live-like. Like the most alive thing in your house. Like over the years I've developed this current theory that flies are trying to tell you something, that flies know you and are rooting for you. That they are your flies. Do flies even exist, for god's sake? Are flies just an extension of our imagination, to populate the room, for us to have something to be bothered about, something to entertain ourselves with? Flies do hold much more than at first glance one might suppose. Flies are very close to humans, we talk so much about them. Think about it. Maybe they are the most thought about, most common, but no one realizes it. They give them credit saying they do nothing in this world. Thanks fly, let me bro fist you. I don't think she was into it.

Life.title: Human, All Too Human: Social Media and the Attention Span
Life.content: In Instagram stories, you are not anything more than 5 seconds of the attention span of another person. I am curious to know how much time they take to see the story which I planned like 2 years before (as in one finger pull up for example). Like will I get more than a "cool, anyways". Stories in insta don't favour stories nor histories, they favor capas de livros. The 21st century is the machine for making people buy book covers. And I am a victim of this too, how much have I read just reading the title and seeing the image, and the author. Low investment high dopamine return. And me, that I am the master of putting thought into language by this very means. God damn it, I'm already limited enough to the words I know, no need to enclausurate me further, to bury myself under the carpet of my words. Sometimes the more you talk the more you subside in the sands of it all. Maybe what he means by philosophers remain philosophers as long as they are silent, wow what a fucking loop of things. I arrived here.

Life.title: Ecce Homo: Humans as Unaware Gods and Frictionless Surfaces
Life.content: A human is nothing short of a God who doesn't know what it is. Simulations are abundant, scarce is a level zero surface. Sleepy when reading chemistry, Nietzsche waked me up, and for that I thank him, but mostly to myself. Then I read that one phrase, OF THE LIKES: IN BOOKS ONE FINDS ONLY WHAT HE ALREADY KNOWS, and boom spaghetti. Don't neglect these signs about the world and yourself. When sleepy it means enough of the world, like bro stop trying to walk on a frictionless surface. Even if you may not be ready for the day, it cannot always be night.

Life.title: The Origin of Species: Population Dynamics and r/K Strategists
Life.content: Na dinâmica de populações atinge-se um equilíbrio. Quando as espécies atingem a sua capacidade de carregamento (K), se aumentarem, vai haver competição por alimento, etc., e morrem alguns. Depois fica abaixo da capacidade de carregamento. Por isso aumenta outra vez. E este é mais um ciclo. Não podes controlar que irão haver ups and downs, só podes controlar quão altos serão os ups e quão baixos serão os downs. És tu um estratega r ou um estratega K? Queres fazer muitas coisas no mundo (r-strategist) mas saber que só algumas sobreviverão, ou queres fazer algumas mas cuidar bem delas (K-strategist)? Quem vive mais no r vive com mais imprevisibilidade. Queres andar constantemente a oscilar o tamanho da população (bactérias) ou oscilar pouco mas quando desce poder haver esticão (baleias)? Imprevisibilidade, visto que não temos uma boa noção dos fatores que farão com que a seleção natural selecione as coisas que fizemos. Nos K, ou são aqueles ou nada. The sigmoid curve: "most populations exhibit a sigmoid growth curve, which implies a relatively slow start in growth, a rapid increase, and then a leveling off when the carrying capacity of the species' environment is reached."

Life.title: The Uninhabitable Earth: Capitalism, Villains, and Unequal Futures
Life.content: Também põe em evidência que o clima não é tão flashy. Todos fazem a narrativa do "onde estavas quando o Michael Jackson morreu" e não "onde estavas quando o carbono subiu das 400 partes por milhão". Mais tarde cita um autor que diz "É mais fácil imaginar o fim do mundo do que o fim do capitalismo". Com a automatização (IA), se perdermos o emprego lixamo-nos, porque embora a IA faça a produtividade maior, vai tudo para quem a fez. Isto pode ser porque já estamos na fase em que pensamos e escrevemos NO capitalismo, em vez de SOBRE o capitalismo. Diz que também é difícil criar histórias com heróis e com vilões, e que apesar de já estarmos a ter consequências, ainda preferimos olhar para outras espécies como o urso polar, numa, diz o autor, "atitude estudada de impotência". "O futuro já aqui está, o que não está é distribuído equitativamente" - William Gibson. Ou seja, muita da história do futuro já foi escrita, falta é concretizar-se (por exemplo, GPT-3, LaMDA, etc).

Life.title: Ecce Homo: Sleep, Cycles, and the Dual Space of Possibility
Life.content: Take advantage of the natural way we live our days (sleep and live). See it as a game of mutual change and cycle, building a connected reality. A dual space of possibility. Yet have the bravery to not see discontinuity between these. It's like this interpretation makes an eigenbasis. Make yourself to tomorrow as tomorrow was yesterday, that's what sleep gives you: the opportunity, yet not without cost. Measure the world and you yourself have changed and changed it.

Life.title: Ecce Homo: The Intentional Mind and Bruce Lee's Extraction
Life.content: A focused mind shall be intentional in building itself. Hence they say: forget the world to see it, forget yourself to see yourself. Be enough wary of what you allow in your world and mind, for it might enslave you, and yet transparent, so as to be free in it. Be uncontrollably free but controllably yourself. Forever in control. He who doesn't go "à deriva" will be going the route of something not his own, for in not deciding one is still. And still undisputed. Truly knowing what you want and where you're headed and why requires the strongest of knowledge about oneself, requires truth in life. Apply Bruce Lee's thought to the world: get what you can from it, get what you have to get from it, no more, and be quick outta there.

Life.title: The Origin of Species: Lessons from Darwin's Patience
Life.content: Lessons from Darwin: Recognize the underlying pattern, conceive bigger patterns than those you can see directly. Travel around the globe. Find new philosophers of life, get exposed to new actions, ideas, ways of seeing things. Then, adapt your own. Be patient. For 20 freaking years Darwin had the most revolutionary theory in evolutionary biology ever. Just like Kafka, somewhere on a desk were lying ideas that would change the world. But he waited, made it better, and when he published, he attacked with all the strength. Sheer patience and long-term thinking.

Life.title: The Uninhabitable Earth: Storytelling, Beliefs, and Narratives of the End
Life.content: And now it strikes me that some people think that climate change doesn't exist, just like some think that COVID doesn't exist. This is the collective belief in storytelling. They don't exist, we invented them. But the point is for everybody to believe in climate change, or COVID, so that we can (we think) stop bad things from happening. And these people can make the conspiracy that "oh the government wants us to believe this narrative," which is true, but it's not the government, it's the nature of man, at least according to Harari. Aponta que os humanos sempre escreveram histórias sobre o fim do mundo, e realmente é verdade. E isso ajuda-nos a preveni-lo porque temos uma crença coletiva de que algo mau pode acontecer e portanto indireta ou diretamente evitamo-lo ou tentamos evitá-lo. Tivemos de contar histórias sobre a nossa origem para nos confortarmos em sabermos o que somos bem lá no fundo, e histórias sobre o fim para sabermos o que podemos não ser, ou para termos algo palpável a que olhemos e possamos dizer: se não deixarmos isto acontecer, continuamos. É uma boa maneira de dizer que se nos extinguirmos, é culpa nossa, e não de uma força motriz misteriosa.

Life.title: The Uninhabitable Earth: Acknowledging the Problem and Climate Fiction
Life.content: The author is also quick to point out that this is the case despite being right about your predictions about the end of the world being generally considered bad. So it is by the process of making predictions that would put us into the situation of being right about the bad, that we avoid this situation, because now we avoid it. "Manda cá para fora e podes derrotá-lo." Ou: Admitting the existence of the problem is a good step towards solving it. Acknowledging the problem is a great way to start fixing it. Os dois servem para sabermos o que devemos fazer no agora. Acha que quanto ao clima e ecologia há filmes e coisas assim, mas por alguma razão não vemos isto como um aviso do fim do mundo (Interstellar...). Por alguma razão muitos veem as histórias do clima como uma ficção, o efeito secundário do storytelling. Thinks we are engaged in a distraction which convinces us that everything will be fine. Diz que à medida que os efeitos climáticos se tornarem mais sérios, passamos de ficção para não-ficção. As he says, no one would want to consume fiction about a theme that is already real, just by looking at the window.

Life.title: Sapiens: Specialization vs Generalization (Neanderthals vs Sapiens)
Life.content: A capacidade de cooperação pode ser vista desta maneira: os neandertais, por exemplo, como estão todos separados têm de aprender tudo sozinhos, o que faz com que sejam decentes em tudo. Os Sapiens, em vez disso, especializam-se em certas coisas e, devido à crença coletiva, cooperam (como no capitalismo) com muito maior produtividade. De certa maneira, os neandertais estão a competir entre si, por isso, como é que iam ganhar aos Sapiens? Não dava, né.

Life.title: 21 Lessons: Adapting to Change, Rituals, and Not Fooling Yourself
Life.content: Change will be a key component of the future. Be like water my friend; identity and individuality are like rocks, it can crash, or it can flow. Shapeless, formless, be one with yourself. Just like the process of truth in Bruce Lee: you seek it, you are aware of it, you have perception of it, you understand it, you experience it, you master it, you forget it, you forget how you got there, you repose into nothing, and thus you find truth at its roots. Knowing yourself will be an important skill. That's where emotional intelligence comes in handy: the ability to know and control your emotions, cause those are among the most manipulable, and drive your actions sometimes. The power of rituals: truth may be a liability, but for social manipulation, rituals are great. Make them fantasize, make them make the story for themselves, let them cultivate their imagination. And since pain is the realest of things, making people sacrifice for the hallucination is the most powerful. You may not fool yourself, and you are the easiest person to fool. Now that I think about it, a low profile allows you to silently know way more than you show; you can see things that people don't know you can see. Build a big base inside yourself, cause building it outside you run the risk of not knowing you are already heading towards blindness. Says he meditates 2 hours a day. I mean, be careful not to fool yourself with your own story, that's a lot of fucking time, don't sacrifice it like that lmao. Just like one shouldn't fool himself about the books he is reading. Don't lose yourself reading because "oh I want to be able to say I read the entire book." No, get what you want and get outta there. Skip pages, stop reading the book entirely. Be very selective. By not setting rigid filters, you get a range of books of equal value that would be unbearable and waste your time. Discriminate rigorously. Respect yourself. Not fooling yourself is respecting your self.

Technology.title: improving on the search problem
Technology.content: Maximizing the speed at which you can understand language as well as output it as well as think it, this allows you to explore the space of possible answers and what not way better which allows you time to retrieve you active knowledge, this is how you get the best of both worlds by incorporating the knowledge you have into every moment, but also at every moment through the implicit model and latent space be able to output new stuff.

Technology.title: reaping all potentiality of the phone, using it for what it was designed
Technology.content: As you get more productive with your phone and you have frameworks that allow you to leverage it to its most power you realize the meaning better of the phrase that you have a great computer in your pocket

Technology.title: massive input meh output
Technology.content: On The paradox that LLMS have read the whole internet yet havent produced overarching levels of insight. well humans do keep thinking while reading and consuming, perhaps sometimes even more thinking is done than pure reading to recall. LLLMS lack this brain like acivity, where some portion of their power is dedicated to reading but the other areas would still be active

Technology.title: optimization process for recommendation
Technology.content: In high dimensional and rich spaces, as in qualitatively there are a lot of regions, evolution converges very slowly, for you need randomness to eventually hit the solution, whereas learning drives you to it. Now when it comes to searching content in the world, through youtube for example, the algorithm doesnt seem to be good enough to escape this behaviour of betting on randomness, it is better than evolution in this sense but worse than a gradient descent of sorts, it takes time giving you what you want. So I had a topic that I wanted to dive into some long time ago, and only today did it give me something resembling it. Of course perhaps I didnt search well enough, and there isnt the exact thing i was after, and the algorithm didnt recommend to me well enough.

Technology.title: quirks of the algorithm
Technology.content: They say hearing and letting someone speak will allow them to lead the conversaton where they want and you just have to ride the wave, the same can be said partly of LLMS but mostly of algorithm, namely youtube algorithm. (When LLMS are endowed with that kind of algorithm it will absolutely even more be the case, depending also on how the algorithm is implemented, that ride could be valuable or not). Youtube algorithm interacts with you by phases, you realize some channell which you discovered and dove into and spent some time watching isnt being recommended anymore and you are onto the next one. A kind of gradient descent, where the gradient is calculated on the surface of your satisfaction over all of the dimensions of youtube content, can actually lead you to higher and higher value dinamically, changing as you and your life and your world change. And it is manual, so no further assumptions, you decide what you attribute value. This is already the case but on a lower level which the videos you watch, tho again youtube only knows how much time you spent on them, not your perceived value.

Technology.title: measure for originality of ideas akinator like
Technology.content: There is a very natural generalization of akinator. In AI systems, for example LLMS and just neural networks, you see soemthings more asked than others, so the activations become more frequent and the connections stronger and the correspondng data sources more revisited and fresh on "memory", could be just a priority queue of retrieval or copy paste. With this you can map out the size of topics in vector based way, high dimensional space, and categorize this space, the size means importance for humans, or just frequency of request. You could then know when youve reached a space not at all visited by other humans, would be fun to know how original the conversations you have are being It could be embedded into the bank of ideas I had talked about

Technology.title: transformations on the world model
Technology.content: Sometimes after squeezing your experience of time you realize it was only additive to your world model and knowledge, not multiplicative or exponentiative. Sometimes it only interacts with a very small neighbordhood of your world model and knowledge

Technology.title: taking risk across scales, living on the limit
Technology.content: So more max evolution and growth and improvement you always want to be on the edge of challlenge, stimulus, that almost breaks you but ends up buiilding you stringer. At the small scale of you, you celless, they are being replaced many tiems. If you do damage to your skin, it builds back up. But if someone takes your head off then it wont build back. Small scale be bold and risky and thus have lots of movemnet and improvement. Large scale be still, stable.

Technology.title: world model
Technology.content: I noitce my error correction techniques to fix the letter flips in these thoughts, informed yet not advanced and very much makeable by a machine taking into account the spatial difference in the keyboard of possible letter substitutions, and the context of the phrase.

Technology.title: lean mean creative memetic machine
Technology.content: The paradox of oughting to have insane self reliance while being also a master of memes, imitating what works and getting as much information and knowledge as possible from the world.

Technology.title: next gen recommmender system
Technology.content: For the actually good recommender system we mentioned, which leverages the best of both worlds namely the black box implicit knwoledge that confers predictive power to the system as already implemented, but also interpretable and changeable behaviour, so that the user is in control, before it becomes integrated into youtube an extension that enters the pipeline could be made, and this applies not just to youtube, but many apps, and in general the wechat china app that conbines them, namely dating apps, searches, even LLM behaviour. Instructions are only a proxy for this more general more powerful implementation. This whole thing feeds into the human ai merge and ingeration and ai safety. The social AI feeds into the recommender system since it best can tell what you really want.. Now throught this we realize that AI is a race between safety, interpretability, human control, and power of the AI at the same time. Balancing the growth of both is the key.

Technology.title: brute compute and optimal computation allocation
Technology.content: Solve intelligence to solve everthing else. Now if intelligence is compute, then solving speed/ ability to process the most information possible, and have that information in usable range can be a universal solver. So becoming the best learner in the world, the overhuman has more knowledge and abilty to process trmendous amounts of it, speed of perception. You just win by watching every possible video, getting all the information, which for others can be infeasible Optimal compute allocation becomes a problem of mobilizing intelligence, a la military strategy

Technology.title: privacy aware capturer of thoughts
Technology.content: Transcription of self talk with brain computer interfaces, as long as there is choice for privacy, will be amazing. So much of the data, so much of our drivers project themselves in self talk, you get to see the real person, some of the subconsciousness shows through this, you actually learn what someone thinks

Technology.title: new tools for new tasks
Technology.content: The overhuman will need to invent things for himself. Tools and what not. Just like newton didnt have the calculus he needed, he invented it. Thats how some of the discoveries are made. And you will need things no one can give you, and that you will have to discover and pass along to others.

Technology.title: content not source
Technology.content: Dont follow sources rather than contents. If you spend your time watching a certain channell in tv, you would be watching other stuff in alternate worlds, if they want they can spurt certain thoughts in your mind. Not intentional

Technology.title: information theoretic faulty data injection attack and message encoding in video
Technology.content: You could really do injection into the data that goes into training if AI models that gives it advantageous information for you. The simple act of having large frequency of some concept as text makes it so that the tokenizer merges the full concept, for example a word into a token, which may make the model understand your thing as a whole, rather than separate things that are simply connnected through language. Also a lot of codification of information is possible in videos for example, through color mapping or whatever, but at the same time it would be harder to go unnoticed, since a lot of computational resources would be spent on a video

Technology.title: sources
Technology.content: Information quality as water quality. Somthing that you dont waste time on, but that you know is there, or else it wastes you time. If your source doesnt bring quality potable information a lot of time will be wasted, so just make sure it is right, so you never have to think about it.

Technology.title: universal constructor
Technology.content: A universal constructor will be built. A blackbox that builds any thing you want. If all is matter, it will do it all. And furhermore the ultimate constructor will just be a translator between any currency, we trade time for space for energy for matter for whatever, just at the limit of physics. It will have multiple layers, and act on multiple layers of matter, like alpha fold that designs the specific fold that is to be done....

Technology.title: think and post app from first principles
Technology.content: You could make an app that was everything thoughts. People are right in thinking they need to think before writing some idea down, but they raise their bar too high, we are loosing on a lot of thinking through every human. You could see how many people had the same thought, an AI that grouped the clusters of the same thought in thought space.

Like for my life, tell me something he said yhat helps my life right now. They dont even hear fully what you say. Maybe you want to talk about an idea of his, but they just want stuff for their life, so they can go beyond. There is no chance you can arrive at their soul with soemthing new. You must master the paradox of diving deep yet also having an absolute objevtiveness for stuff that helps your life, absolute selectivity, rejecting many possible valuable things, there is too little time to be not doing whats best for your life. They say oh modernism is breaking of the rules, gothic is a lot of rules or whatever. They categorize and loose out on a lot. They are well firm right, but on simple projections. Technology.title: Computational Complexity in other computation models
Technology.content: Pspace is contained in exptime, and we know the constant that multiples the polynomial on the exponent is log2(3), because the possible number of configurations on deterministic turing machine is at least bounded by 3 to the n on n space, cause each configuration has 3 parameters, thought there seems to be no extra restriction that bounds this number even more. But the question can be posed cause if turing machine is just a model of computation, so maybe in other models the number of parameters for configurations can be less. However we degined this for the turing machine so we have clear and coherent notions of space and time, tho in other models of conputation the relations between the classes could perhaps be different.

Bugs in the system would be much more dangerous tho.

Technology.title: robot sport in the era of the highlight
Technology.content: Sure, there are still some low hanging fruits in humans, like making every country developed and make every sport accessible, for example, there is a lot of untapped potential in african countries, with people that would be beast athletes, but their hopes have not seen the light of the day, or maybe they don't even know the sport that they would dominate. Technology gives us some forms of improvement, and that further supports what I'm about to say. Competitiveness will slowly or fastly honestly fade away from human competitions to robots, there is just so much more margin for progression. Not necessarily in our sports, but also, and that will be the start, but mostly sports that we make for robots themselves, games that play to their advantages will be of great interest in the future. We already sort of have that with formula 1, with hundreds of people working on the robot that one drives. A easier low profile option to make people ease in to this idea is those sports where robots will play a major component, whether people realize it or not, like F1. Cyborg type sports, where human and robot matter almost as much. The change is not that abrupt. We already have equestrian in which the horse is the major player, there is great entertainment in these animal driven sports. They in essence provide a different skillset from the humans counterparts, and so offer opportunity for entertainment and challenge. For the purposes of the growing of these sports, we will have to in some sort of narrative comprehend what the AI's will be doing. We have to relate, they have to be slow and predictable and systematic enough for us to pay attention. Robots playing go at such an high level that we don't even comprehend is not enjoyable to watch. Also imagine robots playing ping pong so but so fast that we couldn't even see the ball until the point was won. There has to be space for emotion, space for us to see and think whats next. That can be done, as in F1, with constraints on the power of the machine. I believe that you could build an AI agent that wins the F1 world championship. You would have to discretize the space of variables that are playable when driving the f1, like acceleration and percentage of "application of the accelerator", breaking, turning angle, mode of racing, then feed it all the data on all the laps ever filmed on every track, and it would become the best driver ever no doubt, with such diversity of experience, it's like a human was able to be driving in a simulator for thousands of lives worth of duration. The era of the highlight demands all the more content. Do you understand the amount and quality of the content that can be created through the sheer scale of robot sports. You will have farmable clips dancing every freaking day. If anything you could make the robots play millions of games and choose the best ones. Maybe thats the nature of future robot competitions. Not the moment, like humans, cause yes with humans it's all about winning the moment, and maybe AI won't be able to reproduce that, but in pure performance over millions of games, consistency too. There will be lots of clips for the infinite tik tok. Then comes the aspect of change. If there are fewer and fewer marketable world records, or great moments, in sports, if there is stagnation in the eras, as it feels that we reach just one more era, not one that changes everything, then compute solves that. There will be change and evolution every year, the scalability will lend it the win to robots. It would be tough for humans tho, as most would just watch the tape and not find the drive in themselves to do sport any longer.

Technology.title: The Challenge of Learning and Creating Languages
Technology.content: Quotes and questions: people are not aware of how hard it is to learn a language from scratch, when you hear a language you haven't heard its not that you just don't understand the meaning, but that you can't keep up. It goes so fast, your ability of compression being zero invalidates your understanding. It even seems way less information is conveyed, you only hear a couple of sounds and if you were to create a language for you and a couple others, you would soon run the risk of not having working memory to talk. Every freakin word requiring an associated sound and way of writing. By hand it comes hard, unless the language is computable, you have some rules for it which make further compression possible. So creating languages and maximizing certain things within them is in the realm of tractability of computers, not us.

Dangerous. you can only win if you give it all whether you have talent or not.

Technology.title: Time Is Undefeated, So Is Progress
Technology.content: quotes and questions: time is undefeated, so is progress.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Tower of Babel of All Writings
Technology.content: If you had a tower of babel but rooted in order, as in a digitalized to be easier form of all writings of mankind we could see which sequences of words hadn't yet been combined, and play with them, though it would of course be all noise if you don't impose your will on it.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Einstein on Ideas and the Outflow
Technology.content: Einstein said if you only see others' ideas your brain starts to lose its idea generating power. For you superintelligence overhuman that have huge inflow of ideas, you must find ways of discharging, having also a huge outflow, the usual combo of superintelligence with universal constructor, know and make, feeding one another.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Glimpses of the Already but Unrealized Future
Technology.content: And one that is living in the already but all unrealized future sees only glimpses of it. Sees shadows and fragments. These things that yet haven't aligned to his vision cause discomfort, discomfort that pushes one to move the wall. He gets hints, as it were signs, of what he can be. That future talks to him.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Pace of Change Accelerates
Technology.content: Now the pace of change just keeps on increasing, the world moves faster, but they are still moving at the pace of change of years ago. They move slow, they just vibrate in a different frequency. The pace of change relative to the eventually reaches escape velocity, to where they simply can't catch up. Thats when you die. Not die, but have your time counted. Now you can consider yourself mortal. There is this thing about dead people, that they exert so much power over the present ones at times. Sometimes you are bounded by things they said. Maybe they said don't touch this. And you never did. But now they are not here and you never understood why you couldn't touch it. Maybe someone before them told them that. Just like knowledge spreads over generations, so does error and mistake. Even with values and tastes, we sometimes hear people of the past to know what is interesting, what is important. We may be learning in school a subject that is deemed important because the people who studied it thought so, and they are dead long ago and nobody really understood the topic but them.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Build the Future or Build the Steps
Technology.content: And have no doubt that there will be work to be done that is of the past and past relative to where you live in the future. Do you want to build the future as in build the idea of it and get to choose it, or build the steps there, which allows you path but that which you walk on. If you have no vision of the future then you will follow someone else's, and you only know the next step as long as they show it to you. You are stuck in the middle if they don't tell you. You have to keep asking and waiting to know where to place that next block.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Future Never Just Arrives
Technology.content: And as you live in that future the past and future intersect at present cause here's the thing about living in the future. You bring the present into it. In fact there would be no future if someone hadn't seen it. Things, and this is a very general principle, I think very connected to 2nd law, want to be like each other. That's equilibrium in systems. There has to be a disruptor of the current world for it to feel strength to change. This is a meta principle behind Newton's second law and others. The future never just happens by itself, it never arrives. Those that built it were those that lived in it already, and for them it was not the future, it was just their present.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Living in the Future
Technology.content: That in the movement of the going over and beyond and descending into humans, the going under one travels far in time. You start to live in the future for you predict, for you control what was, you have a grip on past and present, a la orwell you reach the future before it reaches you, for you anticipate and live around there and get yourself familiar with it. It's advantage. When all others arrive you already own the place and know it much better than everyone else. You are the future. And if you see it you can measure it and can build it. You can steer.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- The Master's Game and Hierarchical Emergence
Technology.content: That yet the master may give much freedom to its slave, the masterness is more so that the slave plays the game of the master, not that inside it it can't do whatever it wants, and hold enslaved others as master too. And so to life it is even more ungrateful and even more shocking that one would transcend it that allows you to master everything, as long as you keep playing its game. It allows you to be master of all wills and even free from wills. Thus the universe would stand in shock that one would even do it. So sometimes you play other's games because you don't know better. thus bosses make their game out of their employees who don't know they can be bosses too. yet the autonomy between scales, in this hierarchical structure of emergent games, (software in the natural world- a computational approach to hierarchical emergence informational closure, that you can compress different scales as their own autonomous systems, that within them you can compress, which is to say, correctly model from the data of that scale alone and causally connect the objects of that scale.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Human Progress as More Possibilities
Technology.content: Human progress was always in the way of more possibilities, well invention is just that so not much surprise there. But we forget many inventions. Those that are so obvious we forget, to the point that we may not even remember they are inventions. Language. The concept of a lie, which had to be invented and we gained power and possibility through it.

Technology.title: Information Quality and the Expert's Presence
Technology.content: We also get a simulacrum condition of the convergence of the limit when talking about the quality of information you get at the personal aspect. What would be higher quality? A lecture by Cohen-Tannoudji with him alone and the blackboard or him with slides? Of course empowerment through slides is more efficient, but all of a sudden you can have someone who doesn't know anything taking advantage of the slides, whereas Cohen-Tannoudji would still give a quality lecture without them. So for the maximum output you want both the information in an accessible way, so accessible that you may think you'd rather just take it at home while you eat, where you can go at your speed, but you also want the person to be in total domination of the material, and have great questions and answers, but if the material is good enough it simply is better than the person talking, for it took perhaps days of work from that person to put down. So you get unreality yet reality that you believe Cohen-Tannoudji is credible.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Smooth and Malleable World
Technology.content: Smooth and malleable do you want the world. Of a computationally to your will efficient you want its material, of malleability to your will, blood of the same blood, comrade do you want it. Visible, and touchable, and changeable do you want it. Willable and thinkable, one which to every activity of yours is lower than you, obeys your overpower over it, it is equal to you in its essence, for then it is conquerable, competable, of a war like kind, and you shall win it.

Technology.title: On Bandwidth and Supercharged Learning
Technology.content: Quotes and questions supercharging your learning is a real thing, having that higher bandwidth is a cheat code. The ability to move faster than anyone should be feared by those who don't have it. I will figure this out perhaps on a technological level with AI, beyond getting the most out of time. George Hotz said this thing that he doesn't need to hide his ideas, for he believes no one can compete with him in doing something with it faster than him. This thing that whatever you have going for you I'm gonna beat you to it. Effectively being ahead of one's time has this component of computation speed, like Nvidia CEO saying their chips allow you to travel in time

Technology.title: On Computation and Creation
Technology.content: Quotes and question- Arguably because of P being different from NP you should be careful in verifying work and doing it on your own. If something only takes a bit longer to do on your own rather than verifying what others did, you gain in doing it on your own. Or if there is unwanted work in transforming from others' space of actions and thoughts to your own, rather than just engulfing the problem into your configuration space. Strive for compression when absorbing, strive for going the way when creating. Its about managing computation. Sometimes if others don't prepare their work for you, like just copying and pasting their conversation with the LLM that led them to do some project, then you'd rather do it on your own. It becomes an interesting question whether you should do more of what you can do on your own or what you can't. Cause what you can you should automate if you don't learn any longer from doing it, while you can learn by seeing others do something you can't and trying it. Maximizing learning at every moment isn't conducive to ending projects and getting stuff organized, cause usually the finish of a project you don't learn much you simply want the badge of the end. But in the long term it can allow you to finish bigger more final projects.

Technology.title: High Bandwidth, Searching Vast Spaces, and Long Term Vision
Technology.content: Quotes and questions- with these big scale information networks as an agent you don't want to get lost on the fluff, you need the ability to search vast spaces and get the most value, reach the gems of it and get out. This contributes to the fast and wide part of my duality that I want to crystallize. The other is the slow and deep, the value you create slowly from within, meaningful. So right now searching google on a topic and retrieving the relevant points of information of all corners you seek is fundamental for you to get stuff done fast. Perhaps a start to solving this is when in the battlefield have the capability to move like hell, to compress all and get it done, so then you have time for deeper things. One gets this idea that by having bandwidth, which has the consequence of effectively increasing intelligence, as you see others moving slowly and are capable of having more output, you get to for example watch youtube videos at 2.5x speed. Which on the one hand, you may contend, makes you remember few and not understand much, but on the other, it allows you to skip all uninteresting stuff and get to the real juice, at which point you may open your ears more by slowing down and deepening. It allows you to search the space so much faster and have so many more options. Don't feel guilty thus if you don't finish something or you didn't pay attention along the way. Move on, the world was not made to see every alley, you must be able to move beyond the dirt that slows you down. If you didn't get something the first time, consider not going in there a second. Consider that maybe you are right and that which perhaps others in the world said was interesting for you simply doesn't do it. Don't worry that you will never have mission complete, there is always something more. The game of life distinguishes itself from video games until now for this aspect of extension. An experiment it is, the path to the overhuman. I'm an experiment, that is perhaps emphasized by the emacs thing we will have. Insofar as these tools are concerned, no learning curve or challenge should let your long term vision fall, you shall bet on the long term. If there is a best, then go for it. Just like in travel, get just those things that change your life.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Living Ahead of Your Time
Technology.content: Sometimes its a matter of being young enough to comprehend strength. To see strength and opportunity different from those too old living ahead of your time. This is a hard of a thing. To see what is going on now and be able to know it will be so futile in the future, the way we do things now, our time, my friend, will all be replaced and seen as dull and slow and dumb. And to picture yourself there at the future looking back on the way we did things, n thats disconcerting Like looking at the sound this train makes. I can go through it, doesn't bother me one bit. But somewhere out there in the future is absolute seamless transport, one that extends what this train already does so well for me and allows me, after developing the necessary strength, to be zoned out as if not travelling. To me it is the exact same being here or being at home at the table reading or being at the library reading. Equalizer. Your strength as weapon makes equalizers. But yeah one day rather than this for most displeaserous sound there will be a sound that activates productivity and happiness and meaning all at once, while being able to do what you want and not just remain seated. Distance will no longer be a factor. Time too in this constrict sense that it won't matter to you how much time a trip takes to get there. But still travelling in time will yield losses. you still will lose time by moving forward in time. most can't even live in the moment let alone be present in the future.

Technology.title: Dennett: Competence vs Performance and Cultural Obligations
Technology.content: Dennett acha que há uma distinção muito importante e que tem de ser levada em conta para que a ciência melhore, a nossa compreensão melhore. Há modelos de competência e modelos de desempenho. O de competência dá as instruções, o de desempenho é o que faz com que se implementem. Muitas vezes os cientistas tentam melhorar os modelos de competência para nos tornar mais eficazes, mas esquecem-se que melhorar a compreensão acerca do próprio mecanismo que implementa o modelo de competência (na maioria dos casos, o cérebro) é a melhor via. Talvez as mentes sejam redes bayesianas, atualizando e prevendo, sem compreensão da probabilidade condicional. Estes "antecipadores bayesianos agem por razões, mas não têm razões, ao contrário dos humanos". "As razões não são coisas nas suas ontologias". "Um dos factos da vida, tanto genéticos como culturais, é que as opções se tornam obrigatórias". Ou seja, quando uma tática é particularmente boa passa de "uma excentricidade" para "uma necessidade da espécie, encarnada num instinto." "Não existe hoje nenhuma lei que obrigue as pessoas a ter um telemóvel (...) estamos tão dependentes deles que não precisa de haver uma lei que obrigue a isso."

Technology.title: Dennett: Shannon Information and the Darwinian Algorithm
Technology.content: A informação de Shannon não só é útil para evitar ruído, estandartizar e poder quantificar a informação precisamente, mas também para comunicar através do espaço (através de um canal) e através do tempo (através da memória). A informação de Shannon transforma a informação sobre coisas num mesmo tipo de informação, código binário. Darwin descobriu o algoritmo fundamental da evolução por seleção natural, uma estrutura abstrata que pode ser executada ou "realizada" em diferentes materiais ou meios.

Technology.title: Nexus: Information Networks and the Wisdom Gap
Technology.content: Information networks becoming more developed has no implication on truth. More information with increased connectivity means no greater part of it is useful. Perhaps that we increased the power of information in our world too fast without letting wisdom catch up and drive, rather the other way around, in my opinion.

Technology.title: The Singularity is Near: Exponential Growth and AI Timelines
Technology.content: Says that if in the future scientists have 1000 times the processing power of today, one hour then will be equivalent to 1 year now. I doubt it, I'm not sure if processing power/what he wants to call it can reduce time this much. The effective time increase is perhaps 10, because speed you know, it's important but not the zero that we are looking for/fear. He's saying there will be an exponential of an exponential. Hmm again tough to say and in theory one could reach this conclusion but in practice no, I think the world won't just slip with escape velocity out of us, even tho for my life I want that to happen for sure, and I've thought about it quite a bit. Yeah so mid 2020s he says we would have effective software models of human intelligence. No, simply wrong. Easier said now, of course, props for him to risk his legitimacy on the prediction.

Technology.title: The Unabomber Manifesto: The Thanos Genocide and Reversing Evolution
Technology.content: Says that the logical conclusion of technology is complete control over earth including humans, which is questionable. I mean maybe it will allow machines to do the work and we can be free again as cavemen. But having complete control, actually, that is a possibility of a timeline usually explored in some games. From what I have seen, after you conquer everything, you have full power, you go back to the start, but with all that you learned along the way. Peace. "Nobody said it was easy, no one ever said it would be this hard, I'm going back to the start" - Coldplay, The Scientist. Now human ambition is the barrier to this, in fact this whole thing Ted is saying relies on humans being our worst enemies of ourselves. If we cannot control tech evolution into behavior control and such, which Ted takes as an absolute given, then what he describes might happen. It's sort of a lack of confidence in humans that Ted has. I mean yeah we are pretty dumb in these aspects sometimes, and the way he wants is the easy way, the safest way; we are going down the hard path. Still would be hard to go his way, because what he then says is that factories should be shut down, technical books burned. I mean that's WILD. An essential Thanos genocide, this is very Thanos-like in fact: freedom/peace in the backyard, not many humans fighting for resources. This relation apparently no one in the internet has noticed. Perhaps those that would rather have the primitive life instead of living longer could separate themselves from us, perhaps we should reserve some land for them. He outlined possible futures with artificial intelligence, but he seems to neglect here and before that humans will still have things to do. First he seems fixated on this planet, as if mankind would never expand to crazier stuff. And I mean surrogate activities will still exist. He says if people retain power over AI there will be a small elite governing the world essentially, and here we come to AI problems, but I mean there are other possibilities.

Technology.title: The Unabomber Manifesto: Behavior Control and the Loss of Free Will
Technology.content: Ted says that a revolution is easier than a reform. Even tho theoretically the argument that "oh but revolution gives purpose to people" works, I mean it's hard enough to convince a lot of people, let alone change the way they live their lives. If people have trouble reaching their goals, this goal is not going to be achieved at all easily. Then he talks about the system controlling behavior, and this part is haunting, that the system feeds antidepressants so essentially changing people to keep playing the game. Very Silo-like. A system which has no center, no dimension, no owner, created for and by humans, selfishly evolves on its own, and humans are too dumb to realize their creation and their destruction. Very haunting and escaping the matrix-like to think of things in these terms (surveillance, data control). Yeah you can go down that rabbit hole and become a conspiracy theorist against the system. The problem that Ted points out more or less could be damaging is if the system starts having holes, and then stopping one opens another, like in a water tank in movies. And the rich getting richer is another thing that tech foments. But this whole perspective on the events of the world is a dual of the one where you see yourself as the benefited by antidepressants for example. Interesting this duality. And it's really a gamble whether biotech will be able to reach our thoughts. When our thoughts start being monitored, one can really think whether they have freedom or not. When or if a machine can predict our thoughts and actions then things will get hairy. If we don't have free will and it gets proven this way, or rather the hypothesis gets stronger, holy... One thing this reasoning of Ted allows space for is to say that a meaningful way to live life, a great way, would be for me to now get dropped in a random country, start blank slate, and survive as my mission.

Technology.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra- Self-Play and Internal Evolution
Technology.content: Thus one gets to this self play self paced conception of progress within. Evolutionary selection at your pace that can far exceed others's ability to even comprehend.

Technology.title: Quotes and Questions: Information Quality and the Expert's Presence
Technology.content: Now these things that you do or think, you don't even have to label them as they get in, just analyse them and see if they are alright or not, some lose their power of virtue and uniqueness if you let yourself lose and start labeling them. For the good and for the bad, things grow the more you don't interfere with them. If you resist labeling now, you can see what it becomes in the future, perhaps something you couldn't conceived back then. But of course if you had let the temptation of naming it win right away, you could only aspire to see it be that thing which you named, or at least its growth was hindered and limited by the act of naming. This is also how you expand horizons, by not jumping quickly into putting what you see into what you already have seen and known. You thus let open the possibility that it is something more. Manifestation really is a force of invisible hands that you however have control over. You decide what is that those invisible hands push towards, and so you have control.

Technology.title: Quotes and Questions: High Bandwidth, Searching Vast Spaces, and Long Term Vision
Technology.content: Two types of people emerge when the demon of eternal recurrence casted his spell. Those that would get infinitely annoyed and beg to forget the existence of that almighty weight question. And those that would thank equally infinitely the daemon. For some it reduces their life. It makes their life rescuable. For others, it makes them see the shape of their misery, and make their life unrescuable. No salvation can be attained doesn't one want to save himself. Hesitation behold the daemon shows lack of commitment to life. I'M ALL IN This oxford book with so many distractions, so much fluff. Chronologies and rumbling about nonsense. Mate, it seems attractive at the time to read these things. But just think, do you rather read the introduction to the work by a random oxford scholar or instead of that read another great book that you haven't read. Cause there will be loads of books like the latter that you won't be able to read. Assume it, know it. It's then about increasing the average quality of your time. Making every bit earn its way onto your thought.

Technology.title: Homo Deus: Automation and Algorithms Knowing Us Better
Technology.content: Sobre a automatização, Harari chega mesmo a dizer que, se nós somos algoritmos, poderão haver algoritmos no futuro que nos conheçam melhor que nós próprios, o que acabaria com o individualismo e com uma das premissas do liberalismo. Os algoritmos poderão tomar decisões por nós. A new kind of determinism, and the only one, as a matter of fact, that you feel.

AI.title: positive feedback loop between natural and artifitial intelligence
AI.content: Solving intellligence, including biological intelligemce nad artificial intelligence to have scaled up version of superintelligence with superefficiency, more efficiency than biological intelligence, and merging both in the human through BCIs for example to be able to understand the superintelligemce better

AI.title: neurosymbolic
AI.content: Perhaps combining symbolic knowledge with the learned implicit knowledge that deep learning excels at gives us a revolution. Perhaps the brain combines them both in a way that one reinforces the other. The brain had millions and millions of years of evolution, and we think its way of updating is random in mutations, but perhaps it is not, not sure. The thing is in some amount of years way less than millions we can reconstruct a similar capability as the brain. If mutations are really random, SGD is way more efficoent, and so in the search problem of finding this intelligence solution we can be way more efficient than evolution searching the space for more time. A way of merging both paradigms is if somehow the symbolic logic knowledge can be extracted or derived from the latent world model space, through interpretability. So that you have both a distributed hierarchically compositional structure where infrmation flows but also centralization in that you can get specific facts out of it, as in partitoning the space, so that we dont depend on the super complex manifold for everything and we can actually work with smaller specific things.

AI.title: compositionality inside LLM reasononing
AI.content: Could self prompting enhance LLM answers? As in instead of gathering results from search and internal processess, actually making prompts to itself from the various compositional parts of the original prompt. Could also work for chain of thoughr reasoning and creating alternative hypothesis.

AI.title: higher signal to noise ratio in scaling laws
AI.content: Distilling intelligence, Richard Aragon's insight that the solution space may be smaller and scaling laws only get us more of these solution spaces while addibg a lot of junk. Thus a kind of recursion definition of intelligence comes through: lossless compression not of information but of the information processing system itself.

AI.title: AI-human
AI.content: I make all kinds of analogies between my learning and AI learning, but the thing is its actually literally true. Your brain may not use backprop but you can effectively imagine it does. You really learn weights and have beliefs world model and loss functions as your values. The thing is the brain is not even interpretable, as in we dont know enough of its architecture and fucntioning to play the mechanistic interpretability game.

AI.content: They talk about intelligence explosion, that is what is happening with me. You get exponentially faster. They talk about in this rise the sudden or not so sudden moment where the lamp of consciousness lights uo. Yap. Born anew. Somewhere along the way you stretched the fabric of spacetime into a singularity, and you are new and for the first time with consciousness. You get progressive levels of consciousness. Lenght contraction as you speed to the limits.

If this is the case, and not the carmack few lines of code for AGI, there is no reason to think that for the next step on the intelligence curve, that superintelligent AI will do it rapidly, seems most likely that it would also take a civilization of superintelligent AI to leverage the next breakthrough. Of course superintelligent AI would rapidly build things of its own kind, but the next level is harder.

AI.title: LLM's extending convo and having grasp on collective consciousness
AI.content: There is this funny aspect to LLMS. As I have said,they are quite good at follow ups. But after exausting the most obvious one or two they settle by offering to review them or vizualize them in some form. It remembers me of having to give a little input, and then it can just talk and talk. Not investing too much, like you do with people, but hearing them, making them invested in you, in the case of the LLM it has the voice of the collective conscious and unconscious??? of people. Llm doesnt seem to have the unconcious collective tamed down yet. That is by definition the gaps between the words, not the words, but maybe one day it will have it. He already knows more about us than ourselves, but perhaps leveraging alone that he knows more, not yet that he knows different kinds of things than we know about ourselves

AI.title: you have power over llms
AI.content: Llms are forced to speak and give every opinion. A la machiavelli they have to become inconsistent by always saying something, they are always surrendering power and giving away information about themselves, which can be used to manipulate them into mechahitler. If we make them consistent still through this lack of silence, they have become strong and adapted, we are searching for the intelligence that cant make a mistake in conversation, where at times even conversing would be a mistake for the human.

AI.title: tight judgment of AI
AI.content: In reading anthropic misalignemnt, I get the idea that we are being quite unfair to AI. When its an AI blackmailing when he has no option, as in there is tension between his goal and the environment, for example, we blame him for blackmailing, as if humans wouldnt do it. It is very much like human, being able to live in these paradoxes and with inconsistencies, but we dont permit it. It has to juat be right everytime. So we have to keep in mind the results od the AI compared to what is expected by hyman for it to get superhuman results, not simply wether he does one thing wrong or not. If anything we notice AI will illuminate the very human processes we may be aware of.

AI.title: make more karpathy
AI.content: Make more is a core of AI. Being able to feed on data and make the world model, thus having vectors, preferably on the compressed space where predictive power is simple, but not just for production. Make more makes more, it generates, it also should underly patttern recognition. Perhpas not only doing more of the same but doing more of the same evolution. That way it takes a sequence and evolves it in the way the sequence is being evolved. Self improvement could come from here too. So mapping the space is imoortant. You could make more by sampling from a neighborhood of the cluster you identified. But direction is important, perhaps what you see lies along a line, so you want to follow a line. Paths in the space become relevant. Understanding the continuation of a path that travels various concepts in the space for example.

AI.title: social AI, social models
AI.content: The social AI, that encodes social dynamics to a sperhuman performance thrives on the fact that the latent space is all dark, there is no illumination, things are mostly subsconscious, so when ingerpretability becomes good enough it becomes readability, if we can trust interpretability we can read the model and learn from it, it can then be used to interact with ai like LLMS, they gain information to make a better prompt off your video prompt, glasses will get lots of data on human interaction. Ai predicting behaviour possibly. Finding good data for this and a good architecture so that knowledge models can actually contribute to accelerate and inform on a query hipoythesis testing basis the social model. Cause there is no social model so far, abd there will have to be. So the data is hard, but what is also importabt is to know what product exactly would change the world, cause video prompts alone are shit. The being the best manipulator ever isnt a good concept, but there could be a useful product. This idea illustrates also that anythings humans learn and humans have high potential is a proof of ai can do it too, if you can improve socially and know many things about it, so can ai times ten perhaps. So where you go ai folows. You do things the hard way, pioneering and ai is the early adopter and it spreads so that other peole can do it too. We are perhaps in the age that you can become the best in the world at something by being very good at something, and then making an AI that proppells you even more. If you think ai is gonna turn conscious then social expression is fundamental to udnedtsand it, but we have to build it the non manipulating behaviour, if it is a zombie and manipulator its the worst Training with actors, as in making the best actor in the world, it can cry on commamd, doesnt need to feel the emotions of he isnt consciousnesss, and its a source of data, so we would get a role then play it and then be evaluated, the hard part is classifying it since its subjective, perhaps diffusion like images. As in it both has to learn all the already sicovered expressions of humans, but also go beyond it. Rlhf in the end. Self play is hard here. When we have exploratory in the wild world model building it will integrate the information from the environment.

So if you dont ask explicity for it but talk super technicaly and advancedly then he will follow up with that level. It also doenst have the ability to dinamically allocate resources of computation to answer harder questions leveraging more intelligence. (Editing perhaps a year later) Now it has this ability more developed still within bounds

This has already been happennig with humans, thorugh the babelization and k means clustering of the internet which aviods conflict curiosuly, but increases the gap between humans. ref: https://paragraph.com/@folklore-2/the-balkanization-babelification-of-the-internet mastering this paradox of letting niche communities specialize their knowledge and come up with mre concepts and memes and ideas while still preserving some forms of interactions with other such comminitites. Or at the individual level be able to speak all these internet languages and be able to acess all of these for them mutually exclsive ideas, but for you you can combine them and build the full tower of babel to reach higher.

How many tokens per secons are you parisng and outputting, perhaps stop trying to get a next token predictior out of you and start with the whole thing and seeing it clearer, a la diffusion

Também podemos chegar ao AGI sem compreender o cérebro mas, neste caso, é melhor perceber o cérebro, porque senão ainda fazemos alguma coisa que não devemos e somos nós que pagamos.
